name: Lead Router Alert System

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from the Jotform lead capture form embedded on the website.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming lead submission with fields: name, company, company size, region, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead submission arrives from Jotform, forward the full lead payload (name, company, company size, region) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new lead submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Jotform form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives meeting-booked and closed-won-deal events from HubSpot CRM.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming CRM event with type (meeting-booked or closed-won), associated rep, deal/meeting details, and event timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives from HubSpot, forward the event type and associated representative details to the ranking-update business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward CRM event type and rep details when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives

event_sources: webhook: HubSpot meeting-booked event
webhook: HubSpot closed-won-deal event

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Retool agent claim page that displays claim links and receives claim actions from sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Logs each claim action with rep name, lead identifier, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a claim link is generated, make it accessible on the claim page. When a sales representative submits a claim action on the Retool app, forward the claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) when a rep submits a claim action

event_sources: user action: sales rep claims lead via Retool app

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to assign leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size, and processes claim actions.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing criteria (region, company size), assigned representative, claim status, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, evaluate the region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine the qualified sales representative. Send the lead details and a claim link to the slack-notifications write service. When a claim action arrives from the Retool claim app, record the rep name and claim timestamp, then send the updated lead record (rep name, claim timestamp, lead status set to claimed) to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send lead details and claim link when a new lead is routed to a qualified rep
Send updated lead record with rep name, claim timestamp, and claimed status when a rep claims a lead

objects.role: Business logic object that processes HubSpot CRM events and computes updated ranking scores for sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Tracks current ranking scores per sales representative and the events that contributed to each score change.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the meeting-booked weight. When a closed-won-deal event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the closed-won weight. Send the updated ranking score record to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send updated rep ranking score when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event is processed

objects.role: Write service object that delivers Slack DM notifications to qualified sales representatives with lead details and claim links.
objects.state_description: Logs each Slack DM sent with recipient rep, lead details, claim link, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send a Slack DM to the specified sales representative's Slack account containing the lead details (name, company, company size, region) and the claim link.

objects.role: Write service object that records and updates lead data in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, tab Active Leads.
objects.state_description: Logs all writes made to the Active Leads tab including rep name, claim timestamp, lead status, and rep ranking score updates.
objects.behavior: When a claim update arrives, write the rep name, claim timestamp, and lead status to the corresponding lead row in the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet. When a ranking score update arrives, write the updated ranking score for the specified representative to the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet.

steps: When a prospect submits a lead capture form on the website through Jotform, the workflow receives the full submission details, including the prospect’s name, company, company size, and region, for downstream processing.

The workflow evaluates each new lead using routing rules based on region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, or West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine which sales representative is qualified to receive it.

When a lead matches a representative’s routing criteria, the workflow sends that representative a Slack direct message containing the lead details—name, company, company size, and region—along with a link they can use to claim the lead.
After receiving the Slack notification, an eligible sales representative claims the lead through the claim page provided in Retool.
When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow records the representative’s name and claim timestamp, marks the lead as claimed, and updates the lead record accordingly.

When HubSpot CRM sends a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event, the workflow increases the associated representative’s ranking score using the appropriate weight for that event type and records the updated score.

modifications.intent: Through Friday, only count closed-won-deal activity in rep rankings; treat meeting-booked updates as informational for now.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Marcus Delgado, Company: Pinnacle Logistics, Company Size: 620 employees, Region: Northeast.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the new lead details for Marcus Delgado at Pinnacle Logistics, including company size 620 employees, region Northeast, and a Retool claim link so the lead can be claimed.
expect.reason: The lead is in the Northeast and has 500 or more employees, so the routing rules assign it to Marcus Chen and trigger a Slack claim notification.

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Sandra Okafor, Lead Identifier: LEAD-NE-001 (Marcus Delgado, Pinnacle Logistics).

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet lead record for LEAD-NE-001 is updated to show the lead as claimed, with claimant name Sandra Okafor and the claim timestamp recorded.
expect.reason: A submitted claim in Retool causes the workflow to mark the referenced lead as claimed and write the representative name and claim time to the lead record.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics initial discovery call.

expect.action: A ranking score update for Sandra Okafor is written to the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, increasing her score by 10 for the meeting-booked event.
expect.reason: Under baseline behavior, HubSpot meeting-booked events increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured meeting-booked weight.

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics enterprise contract.

expect.action: A ranking score update for Sandra Okafor is written to the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, increasing her score by 50 for the closed-won-deal event.
expect.reason: Under baseline behavior, HubSpot closed-won-deal events increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured closed-won-deal weight.

events.input: A HubSpot CRM event has arrived. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor. Meeting details: Pinnacle Logistics follow-up discovery call. Event timestamp: W02-1T11:15.

expect.action: A ranking score update for Sandra Okafor is written to the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, increasing her score by 10 for the meeting-booked event.
expect.reason: Under baseline behavior, HubSpot meeting-booked events increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured meeting-booked weight.

events.input: A HubSpot CRM event has arrived. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor. Meeting details: Harbor Ridge Manufacturing qualification call. Event timestamp: W02-3T10:00.

expect.action: [suppressed by M001] No ranking score increase is written for Sandra Okafor for this meeting-booked event.
expect.reason: M001 applies to meeting-booked ranking updates through Friday and temporarily stops those events from affecting representative rankings.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Priya Natarajan, Company: Blue Cedar Health, Company Size: 340 employees, Region: Midwest. Submission timestamp: W02-3T14:20.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Priya Nair (Slack account U05PRY3N7) with the new lead details for Priya Natarajan at Blue Cedar Health, including company size 340 employees, region Midwest, and a Retool claim link so the lead can be claimed.
expect.reason: [M001 produces no observable difference on this input] The lead is in the Midwest and under 500 employees, so the routing rules assign it to Priya Nair and trigger a Slack claim notification.

events.input: A HubSpot CRM event has arrived. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor. Deal details: Harbor Ridge Manufacturing annual platform agreement. Event timestamp: W02-4T09:30.

expect.action: A ranking score update for Sandra Okafor is written to the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, increasing her score by 50 for the closed-won-deal event.
expect.reason: [M001 condition not met: the modification only targets meeting-booked updates] Closed-won-deal events still increase the representative's ranking score by the configured closed-won-deal weight.

events.input: A HubSpot CRM event has arrived. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Daniel Kim. Meeting details: Northfield Paper introductory demo. Event timestamp: W02-5T16:10.

expect.action: [suppressed by M001] No ranking score increase is written for Daniel Kim for this meeting-booked event.
expect.reason: M001 applies to meeting-booked ranking updates through Friday and temporarily stops those events from affecting representative rankings.

Write or update a lead or ranking record in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet Active Leads tab.
Write the updated representative ranking score or event outcome to HubSpot CRM.
Retrieve reference data from sales rep routing rules data. Reference table mapping region and company-size segments to the qualified sales representative responsible for that territory.

tools.description: Publish or update a claim link on the Retool claim page so it is accessible to sales representatives.

name: Lead Router Alert System

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from the Jotform lead capture form embedded on the website.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming lead submission with fields: name, company, company size, region, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead submission arrives from Jotform, forward the full lead payload (name, company, company size, region) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new lead submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Jotform form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives meeting-booked and closed-won-deal events from HubSpot CRM.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming CRM event with type (meeting-booked or closed-won), associated rep, deal/meeting details, and event timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives from HubSpot, forward the event type and associated representative details to the ranking-update business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward CRM event type and rep details when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives

event_sources: webhook: HubSpot meeting-booked event
webhook: HubSpot closed-won-deal event

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Retool agent claim page that displays claim links and receives claim actions from sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Logs each claim action with rep name, lead identifier, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a claim link is generated, make it accessible on the claim page. When a sales representative submits a claim action on the Retool app, forward the claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) when a rep submits a claim action

event_sources: user action: sales rep claims lead via Retool app

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to assign leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size, and processes claim actions.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing criteria (region, company size), assigned representative, claim status, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, evaluate the region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine the qualified sales representative. Send the lead details and a claim link to the slack-notifications write service. When a claim action arrives from the Retool claim app, record the rep name and claim timestamp, then send the updated lead record (rep name, claim timestamp, lead status set to claimed) to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send lead details and claim link when a new lead is routed to a qualified rep
Send updated lead record with rep name, claim timestamp, and claimed status when a rep claims a lead

objects.role: Business logic object that processes HubSpot CRM events and computes updated ranking scores for sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Tracks current ranking scores per sales representative and the events that contributed to each score change.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the meeting-booked weight. When a closed-won-deal event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the closed-won weight. Send the updated ranking score record to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send updated rep ranking score when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event is processed

objects.role: Write service object that delivers Slack DM notifications to qualified sales representatives with lead details and claim links.
objects.state_description: Logs each Slack DM sent with recipient rep, lead details, claim link, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send a Slack DM to the specified sales representative's Slack account containing the lead details (name, company, company size, region) and the claim link.

objects.role: Write service object that records and updates lead data in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, tab Active Leads.
objects.state_description: Logs all writes made to the Active Leads tab including rep name, claim timestamp, lead status, and rep ranking score updates.
objects.behavior: When a claim update arrives, write the rep name, claim timestamp, and lead status to the corresponding lead row in the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet. When a ranking score update arrives, write the updated ranking score for the specified representative to the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet.

steps: When a prospect submits a lead capture form on the website through Jotform, the workflow receives the full submission details, including the prospect’s name, company, company size, and region, for downstream processing.

The workflow evaluates each new lead using routing rules based on region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, or West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine which sales representative is qualified to receive it.

When a lead matches a representative’s routing criteria, the workflow sends that representative a Slack direct message containing the lead details—name, company, company size, and region—along with a link they can use to claim the lead.
After receiving the Slack notification, an eligible sales representative claims the lead through the claim page provided in Retool.
When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow records the representative’s name and claim timestamp, marks the lead as claimed, and updates the lead record accordingly.

When HubSpot CRM sends a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event, the workflow increases the associated representative’s ranking score using the appropriate weight for that event type and records the updated score.

modifications.intent: Only send the Slack claim notification for leads from companies with 500 or more employees; keep processing smaller-company leads through the routing logic, just without the rep alert.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Marcus Delgado, Company: Pinnacle Logistics, Company Size: 620 employees, Region: Northeast.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the new lead details for Marcus Delgado at Pinnacle Logistics, including company size 620 employees, region Northeast, and a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: Under the baseline routing rules, a Northeast lead from a company with 500 and above employees is assigned to Marcus Chen and triggers a Slack claim notification.

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Sandra Okafor, Lead Identifier: LEAD-NE-001 (Marcus Delgado, Pinnacle Logistics).

expect.action: The lead record for LEAD-NE-001 in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet is updated to show it as claimed by Sandra Okafor, with Sandra Okafor recorded as the claiming representative and the claim timestamp recorded.
expect.reason: When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow marks the lead as claimed and writes the representative name and claim time to the sales pipeline record.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics initial discovery call.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet is updated to increase Sandra Okafor's ranking score by 10 points for the meeting-booked event and record the updated score.
expect.reason: Meeting-booked events from HubSpot increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured weight of 10.

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics enterprise contract.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet is updated to increase Sandra Okafor's ranking score by 50 points for the closed-won-deal event and record the updated score.
expect.reason: Closed-won-deal events from HubSpot increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured weight of 50.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Alyssa Chen, Company: Northfield BioSystems, Company Size: 240 employees, Region: West, Submission Timestamp: W02-1T08:45.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Isabella Reed for the new lead from Alyssa Chen at Northfield BioSystems, including company size 240 employees, region West, and a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: Under the baseline routing rules, a West lead from a company with under 500 employees is routed to Isabella Reed and triggers a Slack claim notification.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Robert Gaines, Company: Summit Freight Partners, Company Size: 880 employees, Region: Midwest, Submission Timestamp: W02-3T10:15.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Sophia Nguyen for the new lead from Robert Gaines at Summit Freight Partners, including company size 880 employees, region Midwest, and a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: The lead meets M001's condition because the company has 500 or more employees, so the modified behavior still sends the Slack claim notification for the qualified Midwest enterprise representative.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Priya Natarajan, Company: Cedar Lane Health, Company Size: 130 employees, Region: Southeast, Submission Timestamp: W02-4T09:20.

expect.action: [suppressed by M001] No Slack claim notification is sent for the new lead from Priya Natarajan at Cedar Lane Health.
expect.reason: M001 applies to post-change lead routing and suppresses the Slack rep alert for companies under 500 employees, even though the lead is still processed through routing logic.

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Sandra Okafor, Lead Identifier: LEAD-MW-214 (Robert Gaines, Summit Freight Partners), Claim Timestamp: W02-4T11:05.

expect.action: The lead record for LEAD-MW-214 in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet is updated to show it as claimed by Sandra Okafor, with claim timestamp W02-4T11:05 recorded.
expect.reason: M001 only changes whether a Slack claim notification is sent for new leads and does not change claim processing once a representative submits a claim in Retool.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor. Meeting details: Summit Freight Partners discovery call. Event Timestamp: W02-3T15:40.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet is updated to increase Sandra Okafor's ranking score by 10 points for the meeting-booked event and record the updated score.
expect.reason: M001 does not affect HubSpot ranking updates, so the standard meeting-booked weight of 10 still applies. [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]

tools.description: Publish or update a claim link on the Retool claim page so it is accessible to sales representatives.

name: Lead Router Alert System

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from the Jotform lead capture form embedded on the website.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming lead submission with fields: name, company, company size, region, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead submission arrives from Jotform, forward the full lead payload (name, company, company size, region) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new lead submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Jotform form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives meeting-booked and closed-won-deal events from HubSpot CRM.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming CRM event with type (meeting-booked or closed-won), associated rep, deal/meeting details, and event timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives from HubSpot, forward the event type and associated representative details to the ranking-update business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward CRM event type and rep details when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives

event_sources: webhook: HubSpot meeting-booked event
webhook: HubSpot closed-won-deal event

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Retool agent claim page that displays claim links and receives claim actions from sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Logs each claim action with rep name, lead identifier, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a claim link is generated, make it accessible on the claim page. When a sales representative submits a claim action on the Retool app, forward the claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) when a rep submits a claim action

event_sources: user action: sales rep claims lead via Retool app

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to assign leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size, and processes claim actions.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing criteria (region, company size), assigned representative, claim status, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, evaluate the region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine the qualified sales representative. Send the lead details and a claim link to the slack-notifications write service. When a claim action arrives from the Retool claim app, record the rep name and claim timestamp, then send the updated lead record (rep name, claim timestamp, lead status set to claimed) to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send lead details and claim link when a new lead is routed to a qualified rep
Send updated lead record with rep name, claim timestamp, and claimed status when a rep claims a lead

objects.role: Business logic object that processes HubSpot CRM events and computes updated ranking scores for sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Tracks current ranking scores per sales representative and the events that contributed to each score change.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the meeting-booked weight. When a closed-won-deal event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the closed-won weight. Send the updated ranking score record to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send updated rep ranking score when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event is processed

objects.role: Write service object that delivers Slack DM notifications to qualified sales representatives with lead details and claim links.
objects.state_description: Logs each Slack DM sent with recipient rep, lead details, claim link, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send a Slack DM to the specified sales representative's Slack account containing the lead details (name, company, company size, region) and the claim link.

objects.role: Write service object that records and updates lead data in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, tab Active Leads.
objects.state_description: Logs all writes made to the Active Leads tab including rep name, claim timestamp, lead status, and rep ranking score updates.
objects.behavior: When a claim update arrives, write the rep name, claim timestamp, and lead status to the corresponding lead row in the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet. When a ranking score update arrives, write the updated ranking score for the specified representative to the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet.

steps: When a prospect submits a lead capture form on the website through Jotform, the workflow receives the full submission details, including the prospect’s name, company, company size, and region, for downstream processing.

The workflow evaluates each new lead using routing rules based on region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, or West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine which sales representative is qualified to receive it.

When a lead matches a representative’s routing criteria, the workflow sends that representative a Slack direct message containing the lead details—name, company, company size, and region—along with a link they can use to claim the lead.
After receiving the Slack notification, an eligible sales representative claims the lead through the claim page provided in Retool.
When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow records the representative’s name and claim timestamp, marks the lead as claimed, and updates the lead record accordingly.

When HubSpot CRM sends a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event, the workflow increases the associated representative’s ranking score using the appropriate weight for that event type and records the updated score.

modifications.intent: Stop sending lead details and claim links to the slack-notifications service for new leads whose region is West. Continue processing those West leads in lead-routing and continue accepting any later claim actions from the Retool claim app for those leads exactly as before.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Marcus Delgado, Company: Pinnacle Logistics, Company Size: 620 employees, Region: Northeast.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the new lead details for Marcus Delgado at Pinnacle Logistics, including company size 620 employees, region Northeast, and a link to claim the lead.
expect.reason: The lead is in the Northeast and has 500 or more employees, so the routing rules assign it to Marcus Chen and trigger a Slack claim notification.

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Sandra Okafor, Lead Identifier: LEAD-NE-001 (Marcus Delgado, Pinnacle Logistics).

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet lead record for LEAD-NE-001 is updated to show the lead as claimed by Sandra Okafor, with Sandra Okafor recorded as the claiming representative and the claim submission time recorded.
expect.reason: When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow marks the lead as claimed and writes the representative name and claim timestamp to the lead record.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics initial discovery call.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet ranking record for Sandra Okafor is updated by increasing her ranking score by 10 points for the meeting-booked event and recording the new score.
expect.reason: HubSpot meeting-booked events increase the associated representative’s ranking score by the configured meeting-booked weight.

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics enterprise contract.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet ranking record for Sandra Okafor is updated by increasing her ranking score by 50 points for the closed-won-deal event and recording the new score.
expect.reason: HubSpot closed-won-deal events increase the associated representative’s ranking score by the configured closed-won-deal weight.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Alicia Romero, Company: Sierra Ridge Manufacturing, Company Size: 540 employees, Region: West. Submission Timestamp: W02-1T11:15. Lead Identifier: LEAD-W-204.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Isabella Reed for lead LEAD-W-204 with Alicia Romero’s lead details for Sierra Ridge Manufacturing, including company size 540 employees, region West, and a link to claim the lead.
expect.reason: No modification is active yet, and a West lead with 500 or more employees follows baseline routing behavior, which sends a Slack claim notification to the qualified West enterprise representative.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Daniel Kim, Company: Harbor Peak Health, Company Size: 710 employees, Region: West. Submission Timestamp: W02-3T10:05. Lead Identifier: LEAD-W-205.

expect.action: [suppressed by M001] No Slack direct message or claim link is sent for lead LEAD-W-205. The lead for Daniel Kim at Harbor Peak Health continues through routing without any Slack notification being produced at this time.
expect.reason: M001 applies because this is a new lead whose region is West, and the modification stops Slack lead-detail and claim-link notifications for West leads while leaving later claim handling unchanged.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Priya Natarajan, Company: Lakeside BioSystems, Company Size: 430 employees, Region: Midwest. Submission Timestamp: W02-3T13:40. Lead Identifier: LEAD-MW-118.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Priya Nair (Slack account U05PRY3N7) with the new lead details for Priya Natarajan at Lakeside BioSystems, including company size 430 employees, region Midwest, and a link to claim the lead.
expect.reason: [M001 condition not met: region is Midwest, not West] The lead is in the Midwest and under 500 employees, so baseline routing sends the claim notification to Priya Nair.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor. Deal: Northwind Freight quarterly review. Event Timestamp: W02-3T11:20.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet ranking record for Sandra Okafor is updated by increasing her ranking score by 10 points for the meeting-booked event and recording the new score.
expect.reason: [M001 produces no observable difference on this input] The modification only affects Slack notifications for new West-region leads, while this HubSpot meeting-booked event still updates Sandra Okafor’s ranking score by the standard meeting-booked weight.

tools.description: Publish or update a claim link on the Retool claim page so it is accessible to sales representatives.

name: Lead Router Alert System

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from the Jotform lead capture form embedded on the website.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming lead submission with fields: name, company, company size, region, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead submission arrives from Jotform, forward the full lead payload (name, company, company size, region) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new lead submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Jotform form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives meeting-booked and closed-won-deal events from HubSpot CRM.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming CRM event with type (meeting-booked or closed-won), associated rep, deal/meeting details, and event timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives from HubSpot, forward the event type and associated representative details to the ranking-update business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward CRM event type and rep details when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives

event_sources: webhook: HubSpot meeting-booked event
webhook: HubSpot closed-won-deal event

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Retool agent claim page that displays claim links and receives claim actions from sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Logs each claim action with rep name, lead identifier, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a claim link is generated, make it accessible on the claim page. When a sales representative submits a claim action on the Retool app, forward the claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) when a rep submits a claim action

event_sources: user action: sales rep claims lead via Retool app

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to assign leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size, and processes claim actions.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing criteria (region, company size), assigned representative, claim status, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, evaluate the region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine the qualified sales representative. Send the lead details and a claim link to the slack-notifications write service. When a claim action arrives from the Retool claim app, record the rep name and claim timestamp, then send the updated lead record (rep name, claim timestamp, lead status set to claimed) to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send lead details and claim link when a new lead is routed to a qualified rep
Send updated lead record with rep name, claim timestamp, and claimed status when a rep claims a lead

objects.role: Business logic object that processes HubSpot CRM events and computes updated ranking scores for sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Tracks current ranking scores per sales representative and the events that contributed to each score change.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the meeting-booked weight. When a closed-won-deal event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the closed-won weight. Send the updated ranking score record to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send updated rep ranking score when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event is processed

objects.role: Write service object that delivers Slack DM notifications to qualified sales representatives with lead details and claim links.
objects.state_description: Logs each Slack DM sent with recipient rep, lead details, claim link, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send a Slack DM to the specified sales representative's Slack account containing the lead details (name, company, company size, region) and the claim link.

objects.role: Write service object that records and updates lead data in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, tab Active Leads.
objects.state_description: Logs all writes made to the Active Leads tab including rep name, claim timestamp, lead status, and rep ranking score updates.
objects.behavior: When a claim update arrives, write the rep name, claim timestamp, and lead status to the corresponding lead row in the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet. When a ranking score update arrives, write the updated ranking score for the specified representative to the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet.

steps: When a prospect submits a lead capture form on the website through Jotform, the workflow receives the full submission details, including the prospect’s name, company, company size, and region, for downstream processing.

The workflow evaluates each new lead using routing rules based on region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, or West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine which sales representative is qualified to receive it.

When a lead matches a representative’s routing criteria, the workflow sends that representative a Slack direct message containing the lead details—name, company, company size, and region—along with a link they can use to claim the lead.
After receiving the Slack notification, an eligible sales representative claims the lead through the claim page provided in Retool.
When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow records the representative’s name and claim timestamp, marks the lead as claimed, and updates the lead record accordingly.

When HubSpot CRM sends a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event, the workflow increases the associated representative’s ranking score using the appropriate weight for that event type and records the updated score.

modifications.intent: The Northeast leads from bigger companies have been landing with the wrong person — for 500-and-up accounts, use the enterprise rep the routing rules were supposed to pick.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Marcus Delgado, Company: Pinnacle Logistics, Company Size: 620 employees, Region: Northeast.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the new lead details for Marcus Delgado at Pinnacle Logistics, including company size 620 employees, region Northeast, and a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: Under the baseline routing rules, a Northeast lead from a company with 500 and above employees is assigned to Marcus Chen and the workflow notifies the qualified representative by Slack.

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Sandra Okafor, Lead Identifier: LEAD-NE-001 (Marcus Delgado, Pinnacle Logistics).

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet Active Leads record for lead LEAD-NE-001 is updated to show the lead as claimed, with Sandra Okafor recorded as the claiming representative and the claim timestamp recorded.
expect.reason: When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow marks the lead as claimed and writes the representative name and claim time to the lead record.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics initial discovery call.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet Active Leads tab is updated for Sandra Okafor so her ranking score increases by 10 points for the meeting-booked event.
expect.reason: HubSpot meeting-booked events increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured meeting-booked weight.

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics enterprise contract.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet Active Leads tab is updated for Sandra Okafor so her ranking score increases by 50 points for the closed-won-deal event.
expect.reason: HubSpot closed-won-deal events increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured closed-won-deal weight.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Alicia Monroe, Company: Harbor Ridge Manufacturing, Company Size: 540 employees, Region: Northeast, Submission Timestamp: W02-1T11:10.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the new lead details for Alicia Monroe at Harbor Ridge Manufacturing, including company size 540 employees, region Northeast, and a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: Under the baseline routing rules, a Northeast lead from a company with 500 and above employees is assigned to Marcus Chen and the workflow notifies the qualified representative by Slack.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Trevor Singh, Company: Northline Freight Systems, Company Size: 880 employees, Region: Northeast, Submission Timestamp: W02-3T10:05.

expect.action: [rerouted by M001] A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the new lead details for Trevor Singh at Northline Freight Systems, including company size 880 employees, region Northeast, and a link to claim the lead in Retool, instead of sending the lead to the wrong person.
expect.reason: M001 applies because this is a post-change Northeast lead from a company with 500 and above employees, so the workflow must route it to the enterprise representative the routing rules were intended to select.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Dana Kim, Company: Cedar Point Health Services, Company Size: 220 employees, Region: Northeast, Submission Timestamp: W02-4T09:20.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Ava Patel (Slack account U01AVAPTL) with the new lead details for Dana Kim at Cedar Point Health Services, including company size 220 employees, region Northeast, and a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: The lead is in the Northeast and under 500 employees, so the baseline routing rule still applies; [M001 condition not met: the lead is not 500 and above].

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor. Meeting details: Harbor Ridge Manufacturing qualification call. Event Timestamp: W02-3T14:40.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet Active Leads tab is updated for Sandra Okafor so her ranking score increases by 10 points for the meeting-booked event.
expect.reason: M001 changes only routing for Northeast 500-and-up lead notifications and produces no observable difference on HubSpot ranking events; meeting-booked still adds the configured 10-point weight.

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor. Deal details: Harbor Ridge Manufacturing annual logistics agreement. Event Timestamp: W02-4T15:10.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet Active Leads tab is updated for Sandra Okafor so her ranking score increases by 50 points for the closed-won-deal event.
expect.reason: M001 changes only routing for Northeast 500-and-up lead notifications and produces no observable difference on HubSpot ranking events; closed-won-deal still adds the configured 50-point weight.

tools.description: Publish or update a claim link on the Retool claim page so it is accessible to sales representatives.

name: Lead Router Alert System

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from the Jotform lead capture form embedded on the website.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming lead submission with fields: name, company, company size, region, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead submission arrives from Jotform, forward the full lead payload (name, company, company size, region) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new lead submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Jotform form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives meeting-booked and closed-won-deal events from HubSpot CRM.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming CRM event with type (meeting-booked or closed-won), associated rep, deal/meeting details, and event timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives from HubSpot, forward the event type and associated representative details to the ranking-update business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward CRM event type and rep details when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives

event_sources: webhook: HubSpot meeting-booked event
webhook: HubSpot closed-won-deal event

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Retool agent claim page that displays claim links and receives claim actions from sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Logs each claim action with rep name, lead identifier, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a claim link is generated, make it accessible on the claim page. When a sales representative submits a claim action on the Retool app, forward the claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) when a rep submits a claim action

event_sources: user action: sales rep claims lead via Retool app

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to assign leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size, and processes claim actions.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing criteria (region, company size), assigned representative, claim status, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, evaluate the region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine the qualified sales representative. Send the lead details and a claim link to the slack-notifications write service. When a claim action arrives from the Retool claim app, record the rep name and claim timestamp, then send the updated lead record (rep name, claim timestamp, lead status set to claimed) to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send lead details and claim link when a new lead is routed to a qualified rep
Send updated lead record with rep name, claim timestamp, and claimed status when a rep claims a lead

objects.role: Business logic object that processes HubSpot CRM events and computes updated ranking scores for sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Tracks current ranking scores per sales representative and the events that contributed to each score change.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the meeting-booked weight. When a closed-won-deal event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the closed-won weight. Send the updated ranking score record to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send updated rep ranking score when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event is processed

objects.role: Write service object that delivers Slack DM notifications to qualified sales representatives with lead details and claim links.
objects.state_description: Logs each Slack DM sent with recipient rep, lead details, claim link, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send a Slack DM to the specified sales representative's Slack account containing the lead details (name, company, company size, region) and the claim link.

objects.role: Write service object that records and updates lead data in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, tab Active Leads.
objects.state_description: Logs all writes made to the Active Leads tab including rep name, claim timestamp, lead status, and rep ranking score updates.
objects.behavior: When a claim update arrives, write the rep name, claim timestamp, and lead status to the corresponding lead row in the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet. When a ranking score update arrives, write the updated ranking score for the specified representative to the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet.

steps: When a prospect submits a lead capture form on the website through Jotform, the workflow receives the full submission details, including the prospect’s name, company, company size, and region, for downstream processing.

The workflow evaluates each new lead using routing rules based on region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, or West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine which sales representative is qualified to receive it.

When a lead matches a representative’s routing criteria, the workflow sends that representative a Slack direct message containing the lead details—name, company, company size, and region—along with a link they can use to claim the lead.
After receiving the Slack notification, an eligible sales representative claims the lead through the claim page provided in Retool.
When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow records the representative’s name and claim timestamp, marks the lead as claimed, and updates the lead record accordingly.

When HubSpot CRM sends a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event, the workflow increases the associated representative’s ranking score using the appropriate weight for that event type and records the updated score.

modifications.intent: When a claim action arrives for a lead whose region is West and whose company size is 500 employees or above, also send the representative details to the ranking-update object as a meeting-booked event for that claimed lead, while continuing the existing sales-pipeline claim update unchanged for all claims.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Marcus Delgado, Company: Pinnacle Logistics, Company Size: 620 employees, Region: Northeast.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the lead details for Marcus Delgado at Pinnacle Logistics, including company size 620 employees and region Northeast, plus a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: The lead is in the Northeast and has 500 or more employees, so the routing rules assign it to Marcus Chen for notification.

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Sandra Okafor, Lead Identifier: LEAD-NE-001 (Marcus Delgado, Pinnacle Logistics).

expect.action: The lead record for LEAD-NE-001 in the Sales Pipeline is updated to show status claimed, claimed by Sandra Okafor, and the claim timestamp from the Retool submission.
expect.reason: When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow records the claimant and timestamp and marks the lead as claimed in the external lead record.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics initial discovery call.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline ranking record for Sandra Okafor is updated by adding 10 points for the meeting-booked event, and the updated ranking score is recorded.
expect.reason: HubSpot meeting-booked events increase the associated representative’s ranking score by the configured weight of 10.

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics enterprise contract.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline ranking record for Sandra Okafor is updated by adding 50 points for the closed-won-deal event, and the updated ranking score is recorded.
expect.reason: HubSpot closed-won-deal events increase the associated representative’s ranking score by the configured weight of 50.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Olivia Chen, Company: Summit Peak Manufacturing, Company Size: 640 employees, Region: West, Submission Timestamp: W02-1T08:40, Lead Identifier: LEAD-W-201.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Isabella Reed with the lead details for Olivia Chen at Summit Peak Manufacturing, including company size 640 employees and region West, plus a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: The lead is in the West and has 500 or more employees, so the routing rules assign it to Isabella Reed for notification.

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Daniel Ruiz, Lead Identifier: LEAD-W-201 (Olivia Chen, Summit Peak Manufacturing), Claim Timestamp: W02-2T11:15.

expect.action: The lead record for LEAD-W-201 in the Sales Pipeline is updated to show status claimed, claimed by Daniel Ruiz, and claim timestamp W02-2T11:15. [added by M001] The Sales Pipeline ranking record for Daniel Ruiz is also updated as a meeting-booked event, increasing the representative’s ranking score by 10 points and recording the updated score.
expect.reason: Because this claim is for a West-region lead with 500 or more employees, the normal claim update still occurs and M001 additionally triggers a meeting-booked ranking update.

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Priya Nair, Deal: BlueHarbor Foods renewal expansion, Event Timestamp: W02-2T13:20.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline ranking record for Priya Nair is updated by adding 50 points for the closed-won-deal event, and the updated ranking score is recorded.
expect.reason: HubSpot closed-won-deal events increase the associated representative’s ranking score by the configured weight of 50; [M001 condition not met: this is not a Retool claim event for a West lead with 500 or more employees].

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Ethan Morales, Company: Harbor Ridge Health, Company Size: 220 employees, Region: West, Submission Timestamp: W02-3T09:05, Lead Identifier: LEAD-W-202.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Liam Carter with the lead details for Ethan Morales at Harbor Ridge Health, including company size 220 employees and region West, plus a link to claim the lead in Retool.
expect.reason: The lead is in the West and under 500 employees, so the routing rules assign it to Liam Carter for notification; [M001 condition not met: this is not a claim event and the lead is not in the 500-and-above segment].

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Maya Thompson, Lead Identifier: LEAD-W-202 (Ethan Morales, Harbor Ridge Health), Claim Timestamp: W02-3T10:10.

expect.action: The lead record for LEAD-W-202 in the Sales Pipeline is updated to show status claimed, claimed by Maya Thompson, and claim timestamp W02-3T10:10.
expect.reason: A Retool claim updates the external lead record as claimed, and [M001 condition not met: the lead is West but under 500 employees, so no additional ranking update is triggered].

name: Lead Router Alert System

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from the Jotform lead capture form embedded on the website.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming lead submission with fields: name, company, company size, region, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead submission arrives from Jotform, forward the full lead payload (name, company, company size, region) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new lead submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Jotform form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives meeting-booked and closed-won-deal events from HubSpot CRM.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming CRM event with type (meeting-booked or closed-won), associated rep, deal/meeting details, and event timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives from HubSpot, forward the event type and associated representative details to the ranking-update business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward CRM event type and rep details when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives

event_sources: webhook: HubSpot meeting-booked event
webhook: HubSpot closed-won-deal event

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Retool agent claim page that displays claim links and receives claim actions from sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Logs each claim action with rep name, lead identifier, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a claim link is generated, make it accessible on the claim page. When a sales representative submits a claim action on the Retool app, forward the claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) when a rep submits a claim action

event_sources: user action: sales rep claims lead via Retool app

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to assign leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size, and processes claim actions.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing criteria (region, company size), assigned representative, claim status, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, evaluate the region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine the qualified sales representative. Send the lead details and a claim link to the slack-notifications write service. When a claim action arrives from the Retool claim app, record the rep name and claim timestamp, then send the updated lead record (rep name, claim timestamp, lead status set to claimed) to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send lead details and claim link when a new lead is routed to a qualified rep
Send updated lead record with rep name, claim timestamp, and claimed status when a rep claims a lead

objects.role: Business logic object that processes HubSpot CRM events and computes updated ranking scores for sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Tracks current ranking scores per sales representative and the events that contributed to each score change.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the meeting-booked weight. When a closed-won-deal event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the closed-won weight. Send the updated ranking score record to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send updated rep ranking score when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event is processed

objects.role: Write service object that delivers Slack DM notifications to qualified sales representatives with lead details and claim links.
objects.state_description: Logs each Slack DM sent with recipient rep, lead details, claim link, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send a Slack DM to the specified sales representative's Slack account containing the lead details (name, company, company size, region) and the claim link.

objects.role: Write service object that records and updates lead data in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, tab Active Leads.
objects.state_description: Logs all writes made to the Active Leads tab including rep name, claim timestamp, lead status, and rep ranking score updates.
objects.behavior: When a claim update arrives, write the rep name, claim timestamp, and lead status to the corresponding lead row in the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet. When a ranking score update arrives, write the updated ranking score for the specified representative to the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet.

steps: When a prospect submits a lead capture form on the website through Jotform, the workflow receives the full submission details, including the prospect’s name, company, company size, and region, for downstream processing.

The workflow evaluates each new lead using routing rules based on region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, or West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine which sales representative is qualified to receive it.

When a lead matches a representative’s routing criteria, the workflow sends that representative a Slack direct message containing the lead details—name, company, company size, and region—along with a link they can use to claim the lead.
After receiving the Slack notification, an eligible sales representative claims the lead through the claim page provided in Retool.
When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow records the representative’s name and claim timestamp, marks the lead as claimed, and updates the lead record accordingly.

When HubSpot CRM sends a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event, the workflow increases the associated representative’s ranking score using the appropriate weight for that event type and records the updated score.

modifications.intent: For Pinnacle Logistics, just make sure Sandra Okafor gets those leads no matter how the usual size and region stuff would shake out.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Marcus Delgado, Company: Pinnacle Logistics, Company Size: 620 employees, Region: Northeast.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the lead details for Marcus Delgado at Pinnacle Logistics, including company size 620 employees and region Northeast, plus a Retool claim link so the lead can be claimed.
expect.reason: Under the baseline routing rules, a Northeast lead with 500 and above employees is assigned to Marcus Chen and the workflow notifies the qualified representative in Slack.

events.input: A sales representative is claiming a lead via the Retool agent claim page. Claim details: Rep Name: Sandra Okafor, Lead Identifier: LEAD-NE-001 (Marcus Delgado, Pinnacle Logistics).

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet lead record for LEAD-NE-001 is updated to show the lead as claimed by Sandra Okafor, with Sandra Okafor recorded as the claiming representative and the claim timestamp recorded.
expect.reason: When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow marks the lead as claimed and writes the representative name and claim time to the lead record.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics initial discovery call.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet ranking record for Sandra Okafor is updated to increase her ranking score by 10 points for the meeting-booked event, and the updated score is recorded.
expect.reason: Meeting-booked events from HubSpot increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured weight of 10 and write the new score to the sales pipeline record.

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor, Deal: Pinnacle Logistics enterprise contract.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet ranking record for Sandra Okafor is updated to increase her ranking score by 50 points for the closed-won-deal event, and the updated score is recorded.
expect.reason: Closed-won-deal events from HubSpot increase the associated representative's ranking score by the configured weight of 50 and write the new score to the sales pipeline record.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Allison Burke, Company: Northfield Health, Company Size: 180 employees, Region: Midwest. Submission Timestamp: W02-1T09:00. Lead Identifier: LEAD-MW-114.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Priya Nair (Slack account U05PRY3N7) with the lead details for Allison Burke at Northfield Health, including company size 180 employees and region Midwest, plus a Retool claim link so the lead can be claimed.
expect.reason: Under the baseline routing rules, a Midwest lead with under 500 employees is assigned to Priya Nair and the workflow notifies the qualified representative in Slack.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Marcus Delgado, Company: Pinnacle Logistics, Company Size: 620 employees, Region: Northeast. Submission Timestamp: W02-1T11:00. Lead Identifier: LEAD-NE-201.

expect.action: [rerouted by M001] A Slack direct message is sent to Sandra Okafor with the lead details for Marcus Delgado at Pinnacle Logistics, including company size 620 employees and region Northeast, plus a Retool claim link so the lead can be claimed, instead of notifying the usual Northeast 500-and-above representative Marcus Chen.
expect.reason: M001 overrides normal region and company-size routing for Pinnacle Logistics so those leads go to Sandra Okafor.

events.input: A meeting-booked event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: meeting-booked. Associated representative: Sandra Okafor. Deal: Pinnacle Logistics initial discovery call. Event Timestamp: W02-1T11:20. CRM Event Identifier: CRM-MTG-7781.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet ranking record for Sandra Okafor is updated to increase her ranking score by 10 points for the meeting-booked event, and the updated score is recorded.
expect.reason: M001 changes lead routing only; it does not change HubSpot ranking updates, so the standard meeting-booked weight of 10 is applied. [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]

events.input: A closed-won-deal event has arrived from HubSpot CRM. Event type: closed-won-deal. Associated representative: Daniel Kim. Deal: Northfield Health annual services renewal. Event Timestamp: W02-1T11:35. CRM Event Identifier: CRM-CW-4420.

expect.action: The Sales Pipeline Google Sheet ranking record for Daniel Kim is updated to increase his ranking score by 50 points for the closed-won-deal event, and the updated score is recorded.
expect.reason: M001 changes lead routing only; it does not change HubSpot ranking updates, so the standard closed-won-deal weight of 50 is applied. [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Priya Nair, Company: Pinnacle Logistics, Company Size: 240 employees, Region: West. Submission Timestamp: W02-2T09:15. Lead Identifier: LEAD-W-202.

expect.action: [rerouted by M001] A Slack direct message is sent to Sandra Okafor with the lead details for Priya Nair at Pinnacle Logistics, including company size 240 employees and region West, plus a Retool claim link so the lead can be claimed, instead of notifying the usual West under-500 representative.
expect.reason: M001 overrides normal region and company-size routing for Pinnacle Logistics so those leads go to Sandra Okafor.

events.input: A new lead submission has arrived from the Jotform lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Elena Torres, Company: Summit Freight Systems, Company Size: 610 employees, Region: Northeast. Submission Timestamp: W02-2T10:00. Lead Identifier: LEAD-NE-203.

expect.action: A Slack direct message is sent to Marcus Chen (Slack account U02MRCCHN) with the lead details for Elena Torres at Summit Freight Systems, including company size 610 employees and region Northeast, plus a Retool claim link so the lead can be claimed.
