name: Brand Media Monitoring & Sentiment Triage

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Google Alerts-based RSS feed for new articles mentioning Acme Corp or specified keywords
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen article identifiers and timestamps to detect new mentions
objects.behavior: Poll the RSS feed continuously for new articles matching 'Acme Corp', 'AcmeCorp', or 'Acme products'. When a new article is detected, forward its URL, headline, and raw content to the brand-mention-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward new article URL, headline, and raw content when a new mention is detected

event_sources: external system: Google Alerts RSS feed new item

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes article content, extracts key information, classifies sentiment, and routes mentions appropriately
objects.state_description: Tracks current article being processed including extracted headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, and routing decisions
objects.behavior: When a new article payload arrives, extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source metadata. Classify sentiment as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on tone, language, and context of the article. Once analysis is complete, send the enriched record to brand-mentions-store and slack-brand-monitoring simultaneously. If sentiment is Positive, also send to slack-brand-wins. If sentiment is Negative, also send to slack-brand-escalations for approval workflow. When a weekly digest trigger arrives from the weekly-digest-scheduler, generate a comprehensive summary of trends and key articles from the prior week and send it to slack-brand-weekly-digest. When a natural language search query arrives from slack-bot-interface, query brand-mentions-store for matching records and return results to slack-bot-interface. When an error notification arrives from any peer or internal failure occurs, send an alert to slack-brand-monitoring-alerts.

peers.relationship: Send enriched article record with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment, and metadata for persistent storage
Send enriched mention with sentiment classification for all new articles
Send positive mention details when sentiment is classified as Positive
Send negative mention details requiring Communications Manager approval when sentiment is classified as Negative
Send generated weekly digest summarizing trends and key articles every Monday at 08:00 UTC
Send error alert with failure details when any part of the workflow fails

objects.role: Entry-point service object that triggers the weekly digest generation every Monday at 08:00 UTC
objects.state_description: Tracks the last scheduled trigger time to ensure no duplicate triggers
objects.behavior: At 08:00 UTC every Monday, emit a scheduled trigger event to the brand-mention-analysis peer to initiate weekly digest generation.

peers.relationship: Send weekly digest trigger every Monday at 08:00 UTC

event_sources: scheduled trigger: every Monday at 08:00 UTC

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the custom Slack bot that receives natural language search queries from team members and returns results
objects.state_description: Tracks active search queries and their requestors
objects.behavior: When a team member submits a natural language search query via the Slack bot, forward the query and requestor context to brand-mention-analysis. When search results are received from brand-mention-analysis, post them back to the requesting team member in Slack.

peers.relationship: Forward natural language search queries from team members for processing

event_sources: webhook: Slack bot slash command or mention event

objects.role: Write and read service object representing the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table used to persist and query article records
objects.state_description: Accumulates all tracked article records with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, source URL, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an enriched article record is received, write it to the Airtable Brand Mentions base, Tracked Articles table. When answering queries, call the `brand_mentions_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring Slack channel where all new brand mentions are posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all posted brand mention messages with sentiment and article details
objects.behavior: When a brand mention message is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring Slack channel including the headline, sentiment classification, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-wins Slack channel where positive brand mentions are shared
objects.state_description: Accumulates all positive mention messages posted to the channel
objects.behavior: When a positive mention message is received, post it to the #brand-wins Slack channel including the headline, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-escalations Slack channel where negative mentions are posted for Communications Manager approval
objects.state_description: Accumulates all negative mention escalation messages and their approval status
objects.behavior: When a negative mention escalation is received, post it to the #brand-escalations Slack channel with the article details, sentiment rationale, and a request for Communications Manager sign-off before any response is issued.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel where the Monday weekly digest is posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all weekly digest posts with their timestamps
objects.behavior: When a weekly digest message is received, post it to the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel including trend summaries and highlighted key articles from the prior week.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel where workflow failure alerts are sent to the Marketing Operations Administrator
objects.state_description: Accumulates all error and failure alert messages with timestamps and failure details
objects.behavior: When a workflow failure alert is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel with the failure description, affected workflow step, and timestamp so the Marketing Operations Administrator can investigate.

steps: The workflow continuously checks a Google Alerts RSS feed for newly published articles that mention the brand or configured keyword variants, such as alternate brand spellings or product-related terms. For each new article found, it captures the article link, headline, and available content for downstream processing.

When a new mention is detected, the workflow uses AI to extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source information from the article, then classifies the mention as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on the article’s tone, language, and context.
The enriched mention record is automatically saved in Airtable so the organization maintains a searchable historical record of brand coverage over time.

After analysis, every mention is posted to Slack for monitoring, and the workflow routes it based on sentiment. All mentions are shared to a general monitoring destination, while sentiment determines whether any additional Slack triage occurs.

If a mention is classified as Positive, it is immediately shared to a Slack destination for brand wins. If it is classified as Negative, it is sent to a separate Slack escalation path that includes the sentiment rationale and requests approval before any response is issued; Neutral mentions are only included in the general monitoring flow.
Every Monday at 08:00 UTC, the workflow generates a weekly AI digest covering trends and key articles from the prior week, then posts that summary to Slack for team review.

Team members can submit natural-language search questions through a Slack chatbot, and the workflow searches the Airtable record of stored mentions for relevant matches before returning the results in Slack to the requesting user.
If any part of the workflow fails or reports an error, the system sends an alert in Slack with the failure description, the affected step, and a timestamp so an administrator can investigate.

modifications.intent: Let’s give the Monday roundup a little breathing room this week and have it go out Tuesday at 08:00 UTC instead.

events.input: A new article has appeared in the Google Alerts RSS feed matching the keyword 'Acme Corp'. Article URL: https://techcrunch.com/2024/01/15/acme-corp-launches-new-product-line. Headline: 'Acme Corp Launches Revolutionary New Product Line, Analysts React Positively'. Raw content: 'Acme Corp announced today the launch of its next-generation product line, receiving widespread praise from industry analysts. CEO Jane Holloway stated that the new lineup reflects the company's commitment to innovation. Shares rose 4% on the news. Several customers quoted in the piece described the products as game-changing.'

expect.action: A new brand mention record is created in Airtable in the Acme Corp Brand Mentions Repository for the TechCrunch article, including the headline, summary, notable quotes, source publication, source URL, matched keyword, and a Positive sentiment classification. A Slack message is posted to the general brand-monitoring destination summarizing the new Acme Corp mention and its positive sentiment. A second Slack message is posted to the brand-wins Slack destination highlighting the positive coverage from TechCrunch.
expect.reason: New Google Alerts article mentions are enriched, stored in Airtable, shared to general Slack monitoring, and positive mentions are additionally routed to the brand-wins Slack path.

events.input: Scheduled trigger firing at 08:00 UTC on Monday 2024-01-15 to initiate weekly brand mention digest generation for the prior week (2024-01-08 through 2024-01-14).

expect.action: A weekly AI digest covering Acme Corp brand-mention trends and key articles from 2024-01-08 through 2024-01-14 is posted to Slack for team review.
expect.reason: On Mondays at 08:00 UTC, the workflow generates and posts the prior week's brand-mention digest to Slack.

events.input: Slack bot slash command received from team member Marcus Rivera, Senior Marketing Analyst (user ID: U08XR4291, channel: #brand-research, thread_ts: 1705320000.000100): '/brandbot How many negative mentions did Acme Corp receive in the past 30 days, and what were the main themes?'

expect.action: A Slack reply is posted in #brand-research to Marcus Rivera in the command thread reporting that Acme Corp received 2 negative mentions in the past 30 days, with the main themes summarized as supply chain delays, operational issues, customer support response-time complaints, customer frustration, and risk of customers switching to competitors.
expect.reason: Slack chatbot questions trigger a search of the Airtable brand-mentions history and return the relevant results to the requesting user in Slack.

events.input: Scheduled trigger firing at 08:00 UTC on Monday W02-1 to initiate weekly brand mention digest generation for the prior week (W01-1 through W01-7).

expect.action: A weekly AI digest covering brand-mention trends and key articles from the prior week is posted to Slack for team review.
expect.reason: [M001 out of scope: one-shot mod targeted this week's Monday roundup delay to Tuesday at 08:00 UTC, and this event occurs before that deferral takes effect under baseline behavior]

events.input: Scheduled trigger firing at 08:00 UTC on Tuesday W02-2 to initiate weekly brand mention digest generation for the prior week (W01-1 through W01-7).

expect.action: [fired-on-schedule by M001] A weekly AI digest covering brand-mention trends and key articles from the prior week is posted to Slack for team review at Tuesday 08:00 UTC instead of Monday 08:00 UTC.
expect.reason: The Monday roundup was deferred for this week, so the digest is produced when the rescheduled Tuesday 08:00 UTC trigger occurs.

events.input: A new article has appeared in the Google Alerts RSS feed matching the keyword 'AcmeCorp'. Article URL: https://www.retaildive.com/news/acmecorp-expands-same-day-fulfillment/710245/. Headline: 'AcmeCorp Expands Same-Day Fulfillment to 12 More Cities'. Raw content: 'AcmeCorp said Tuesday it is expanding same-day fulfillment to 12 additional metropolitan areas after a strong pilot. The article cites favorable customer response and analyst commentary that the move strengthens the company’s competitive position in e-commerce logistics. COO Melissa Tran said the rollout reflects sustained demand for faster delivery options.'

expect.action: A new brand mention record is created in Airtable in the Acme Corp Brand Mentions Repository for the Retail Dive article, including the headline, summary, notable quotes, source publication, source URL, matched keyword variant, and a Positive sentiment classification. A Slack message is posted to the general brand-monitoring destination summarizing the new Acme Corp mention and its positive sentiment. A second Slack message is posted to the brand-wins Slack destination highlighting the positive coverage from Retail Dive.
expect.reason: [M001 condition not met: the modification only changes this week's digest schedule and does not affect RSS article processing, storage, or sentiment-based Slack routing]

events.input: Scheduled trigger firing at 08:00 UTC on Monday W03-1 to initiate weekly brand mention digest generation for the prior week (W02-1 through W02-7).

expect.action: A weekly AI digest covering brand-mention trends and key articles from the prior week is posted to Slack for team review.
expect.reason: [M001 out of scope: one-shot mod targeted this week's Monday roundup delay to Tuesday at 08:00 UTC, and this later week's Monday digest returns to baseline scheduling]

tools.description: Create or update an article record in the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table.
Post a brand mention message to the specified Slack channel.
Send a direct message in Slack to the requesting team member with search results or follow-up information.

name: Brand Media Monitoring & Sentiment Triage

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Google Alerts-based RSS feed for new articles mentioning Acme Corp or specified keywords
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen article identifiers and timestamps to detect new mentions
objects.behavior: Poll the RSS feed continuously for new articles matching 'Acme Corp', 'AcmeCorp', or 'Acme products'. When a new article is detected, forward its URL, headline, and raw content to the brand-mention-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward new article URL, headline, and raw content when a new mention is detected

event_sources: external system: Google Alerts RSS feed new item

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes article content, extracts key information, classifies sentiment, and routes mentions appropriately
objects.state_description: Tracks current article being processed including extracted headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, and routing decisions
objects.behavior: When a new article payload arrives, extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source metadata. Classify sentiment as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on tone, language, and context of the article. Once analysis is complete, send the enriched record to brand-mentions-store and slack-brand-monitoring simultaneously. If sentiment is Positive, also send to slack-brand-wins. If sentiment is Negative, also send to slack-brand-escalations for approval workflow. When a weekly digest trigger arrives from the weekly-digest-scheduler, generate a comprehensive summary of trends and key articles from the prior week and send it to slack-brand-weekly-digest. When a natural language search query arrives from slack-bot-interface, query brand-mentions-store for matching records and return results to slack-bot-interface. When an error notification arrives from any peer or internal failure occurs, send an alert to slack-brand-monitoring-alerts.

peers.relationship: Send enriched article record with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment, and metadata for persistent storage
Send enriched mention with sentiment classification for all new articles
Send positive mention details when sentiment is classified as Positive
Send negative mention details requiring Communications Manager approval when sentiment is classified as Negative
Send generated weekly digest summarizing trends and key articles every Monday at 08:00 UTC
Send error alert with failure details when any part of the workflow fails

objects.role: Entry-point service object that triggers the weekly digest generation every Monday at 08:00 UTC
objects.state_description: Tracks the last scheduled trigger time to ensure no duplicate triggers
objects.behavior: At 08:00 UTC every Monday, emit a scheduled trigger event to the brand-mention-analysis peer to initiate weekly digest generation.

peers.relationship: Send weekly digest trigger every Monday at 08:00 UTC

event_sources: scheduled trigger: every Monday at 08:00 UTC

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the custom Slack bot that receives natural language search queries from team members and returns results
objects.state_description: Tracks active search queries and their requestors
objects.behavior: When a team member submits a natural language search query via the Slack bot, forward the query and requestor context to brand-mention-analysis. When search results are received from brand-mention-analysis, post them back to the requesting team member in Slack.

peers.relationship: Forward natural language search queries from team members for processing

event_sources: webhook: Slack bot slash command or mention event

objects.role: Write and read service object representing the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table used to persist and query article records
objects.state_description: Accumulates all tracked article records with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, source URL, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an enriched article record is received, write it to the Airtable Brand Mentions base, Tracked Articles table. When answering queries, call the `brand_mentions_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring Slack channel where all new brand mentions are posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all posted brand mention messages with sentiment and article details
objects.behavior: When a brand mention message is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring Slack channel including the headline, sentiment classification, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-wins Slack channel where positive brand mentions are shared
objects.state_description: Accumulates all positive mention messages posted to the channel
objects.behavior: When a positive mention message is received, post it to the #brand-wins Slack channel including the headline, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-escalations Slack channel where negative mentions are posted for Communications Manager approval
objects.state_description: Accumulates all negative mention escalation messages and their approval status
objects.behavior: When a negative mention escalation is received, post it to the #brand-escalations Slack channel with the article details, sentiment rationale, and a request for Communications Manager sign-off before any response is issued.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel where the Monday weekly digest is posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all weekly digest posts with their timestamps
objects.behavior: When a weekly digest message is received, post it to the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel including trend summaries and highlighted key articles from the prior week.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel where workflow failure alerts are sent to the Marketing Operations Administrator
objects.state_description: Accumulates all error and failure alert messages with timestamps and failure details
objects.behavior: When a workflow failure alert is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel with the failure description, affected workflow step, and timestamp so the Marketing Operations Administrator can investigate.

steps: The workflow continuously checks a Google Alerts RSS feed for newly published articles that mention the brand or configured keyword variants, such as alternate brand spellings or product-related terms. For each new article found, it captures the article link, headline, and available content for downstream processing.

When a new mention is detected, the workflow uses AI to extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source information from the article, then classifies the mention as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on the article’s tone, language, and context.
The enriched mention record is automatically saved in Airtable so the organization maintains a searchable historical record of brand coverage over time.

After analysis, every mention is posted to Slack for monitoring, and the workflow routes it based on sentiment. All mentions are shared to a general monitoring destination, while sentiment determines whether any additional Slack triage occurs.

If a mention is classified as Positive, it is immediately shared to a Slack destination for brand wins. If it is classified as Negative, it is sent to a separate Slack escalation path that includes the sentiment rationale and requests approval before any response is issued; Neutral mentions are only included in the general monitoring flow.
Every Monday at 08:00 UTC, the workflow generates a weekly AI digest covering trends and key articles from the prior week, then posts that summary to Slack for team review.

Team members can submit natural-language search questions through a Slack chatbot, and the workflow searches the Airtable record of stored mentions for relevant matches before returning the results in Slack to the requesting user.
If any part of the workflow fails or reports an error, the system sends an alert in Slack with the failure description, the affected step, and a timestamp so an administrator can investigate.

modifications.intent: We jumped the gun on the digest timing — have that weekly summary go out Tuesday at 08:00 UTC rather than first thing Monday.

events.input: A new article has appeared in the Google Alerts RSS feed matching the keyword 'Acme Corp'. Article URL: https://techcrunch.com/2024/01/15/acme-corp-launches-new-product-line. Headline: 'Acme Corp Launches Revolutionary New Product Line, Analysts React Positively'. Raw content: 'Acme Corp announced today the launch of its next-generation product line, receiving widespread praise from industry analysts. CEO Jane Holloway stated that the new lineup reflects the company's commitment to innovation. Shares rose 4% on the news. Several customers quoted in the piece described the products as game-changing.'

expect.action: A new record is created in Airtable "Acme Corp Brand Mentions Repository" for the article "Acme Corp Launches Revolutionary New Product Line, Analysts React Positively," storing the article URL, source publication TechCrunch, extracted summary, notable quotes, matched keyword "Acme Corp," and a Positive sentiment classification. A Slack message is posted to the general brand-monitoring destination summarizing the mention, including the headline, source, summary, and positive sentiment. Because the mention is Positive, an additional Slack message is posted to the brand-wins destination highlighting the favorable coverage.
expect.reason: New Google Alerts mentions are enriched with AI, stored in Airtable, and always posted to general Slack monitoring, with Positive mentions also routed to the brand-wins Slack path.

events.input: Scheduled trigger firing at 08:00 UTC on Monday 2024-01-15 to initiate weekly brand mention digest generation for the prior week (2024-01-08 through 2024-01-14).

expect.action: A weekly AI digest covering brand-mention trends and key articles from 2024-01-08 through 2024-01-14 is posted to Slack for team review.
expect.reason: Under baseline behavior, the workflow posts a weekly Slack digest every Monday at 08:00 UTC summarizing the prior week's stored brand mentions.

events.input: Slack bot slash command received from team member Marcus Rivera, Senior Marketing Analyst (user ID: U08XR4291, channel: #brand-research, thread_ts: 1705320000.000100): '/brandbot How many negative mentions did Acme Corp receive in the past 30 days, and what were the main themes?'

expect.action: A Slack reply is posted in #brand-research to Marcus Rivera (user ID U08XR4291), answering that Acme Corp received 2 negative mentions in the past 30 days and summarizing the main themes as supply chain delays, operational issues affecting product rollout, customer-support response delays, customer frustration, and risk of customers switching to competitors.
expect.reason: Slack chatbot questions are answered by searching the Airtable brand-mentions history and returning the relevant count and themes to the requesting user in Slack.

tools.description: Create or update an article record in the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table.
Post a brand mention message to the specified Slack channel.
Send a direct message in Slack to the requesting team member with search results or follow-up information.

name: Brand Media Monitoring & Sentiment Triage

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Google Alerts-based RSS feed for new articles mentioning Acme Corp or specified keywords
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen article identifiers and timestamps to detect new mentions
objects.behavior: Poll the RSS feed continuously for new articles matching 'Acme Corp', 'AcmeCorp', or 'Acme products'. When a new article is detected, forward its URL, headline, and raw content to the brand-mention-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward new article URL, headline, and raw content when a new mention is detected

event_sources: external system: Google Alerts RSS feed new item

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes article content, extracts key information, classifies sentiment, and routes mentions appropriately
objects.state_description: Tracks current article being processed including extracted headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, and routing decisions
objects.behavior: When a new article payload arrives, extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source metadata. Classify sentiment as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on tone, language, and context of the article. Once analysis is complete, send the enriched record to brand-mentions-store and slack-brand-monitoring simultaneously. If sentiment is Positive, also send to slack-brand-wins. If sentiment is Negative, also send to slack-brand-escalations for approval workflow. When a weekly digest trigger arrives from the weekly-digest-scheduler, generate a comprehensive summary of trends and key articles from the prior week and send it to slack-brand-weekly-digest. When a natural language search query arrives from slack-bot-interface, query brand-mentions-store for matching records and return results to slack-bot-interface. When an error notification arrives from any peer or internal failure occurs, send an alert to slack-brand-monitoring-alerts.

peers.relationship: Send enriched article record with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment, and metadata for persistent storage
Send enriched mention with sentiment classification for all new articles
Send positive mention details when sentiment is classified as Positive
Send negative mention details requiring Communications Manager approval when sentiment is classified as Negative
Send generated weekly digest summarizing trends and key articles every Monday at 08:00 UTC
Send error alert with failure details when any part of the workflow fails

objects.role: Entry-point service object that triggers the weekly digest generation every Monday at 08:00 UTC
objects.state_description: Tracks the last scheduled trigger time to ensure no duplicate triggers
objects.behavior: At 08:00 UTC every Monday, emit a scheduled trigger event to the brand-mention-analysis peer to initiate weekly digest generation.

peers.relationship: Send weekly digest trigger every Monday at 08:00 UTC

event_sources: scheduled trigger: every Monday at 08:00 UTC

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the custom Slack bot that receives natural language search queries from team members and returns results
objects.state_description: Tracks active search queries and their requestors
objects.behavior: When a team member submits a natural language search query via the Slack bot, forward the query and requestor context to brand-mention-analysis. When search results are received from brand-mention-analysis, post them back to the requesting team member in Slack.

peers.relationship: Forward natural language search queries from team members for processing

event_sources: webhook: Slack bot slash command or mention event

objects.role: Write and read service object representing the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table used to persist and query article records
objects.state_description: Accumulates all tracked article records with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, source URL, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an enriched article record is received, write it to the Airtable Brand Mentions base, Tracked Articles table. When answering queries, call the `brand_mentions_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring Slack channel where all new brand mentions are posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all posted brand mention messages with sentiment and article details
objects.behavior: When a brand mention message is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring Slack channel including the headline, sentiment classification, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-wins Slack channel where positive brand mentions are shared
objects.state_description: Accumulates all positive mention messages posted to the channel
objects.behavior: When a positive mention message is received, post it to the #brand-wins Slack channel including the headline, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-escalations Slack channel where negative mentions are posted for Communications Manager approval
objects.state_description: Accumulates all negative mention escalation messages and their approval status
objects.behavior: When a negative mention escalation is received, post it to the #brand-escalations Slack channel with the article details, sentiment rationale, and a request for Communications Manager sign-off before any response is issued.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel where the Monday weekly digest is posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all weekly digest posts with their timestamps
objects.behavior: When a weekly digest message is received, post it to the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel including trend summaries and highlighted key articles from the prior week.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel where workflow failure alerts are sent to the Marketing Operations Administrator
objects.state_description: Accumulates all error and failure alert messages with timestamps and failure details
objects.behavior: When a workflow failure alert is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel with the failure description, affected workflow step, and timestamp so the Marketing Operations Administrator can investigate.

steps: The workflow continuously checks a Google Alerts RSS feed for newly published articles that mention the brand or configured keyword variants, such as alternate brand spellings or product-related terms. For each new article found, it captures the article link, headline, and available content for downstream processing.

When a new mention is detected, the workflow uses AI to extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source information from the article, then classifies the mention as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on the article’s tone, language, and context.
The enriched mention record is automatically saved in Airtable so the organization maintains a searchable historical record of brand coverage over time.

After analysis, every mention is posted to Slack for monitoring, and the workflow routes it based on sentiment. All mentions are shared to a general monitoring destination, while sentiment determines whether any additional Slack triage occurs.

If a mention is classified as Positive, it is immediately shared to a Slack destination for brand wins. If it is classified as Negative, it is sent to a separate Slack escalation path that includes the sentiment rationale and requests approval before any response is issued; Neutral mentions are only included in the general monitoring flow.
Every Monday at 08:00 UTC, the workflow generates a weekly AI digest covering trends and key articles from the prior week, then posts that summary to Slack for team review.

Team members can submit natural-language search questions through a Slack chatbot, and the workflow searches the Airtable record of stored mentions for relevant matches before returning the results in Slack to the requesting user.
If any part of the workflow fails or reports an error, the system sends an alert in Slack with the failure description, the affected step, and a timestamp so an administrator can investigate.

modifications.intent: For Acme product review pieces, don't make such a big deal out of the negative ones unless they really read like a broader Acme Corp problem.

events.input: A new article has appeared in the Google Alerts RSS feed matching the keyword 'Acme Corp'. Article URL: https://techcrunch.com/2024/01/15/acme-corp-launches-new-product-line. Headline: 'Acme Corp Launches Revolutionary New Product Line, Analysts React Positively'. Raw content: 'Acme Corp announced today the launch of its next-generation product line, receiving widespread praise from industry analysts. CEO Jane Holloway stated that the new lineup reflects the company's commitment to innovation. Shares rose 4% on the news. Several customers quoted in the piece described the products as game-changing.'

expect.action: A new brand mention record is created in Airtable for the TechCrunch article, including the headline, summary, notable quote(s), source details, keyword match, sentiment classification as Positive, and the article URL; the mention is posted to Slack in the general monitoring destination and also to the brand-wins Slack destination.
expect.reason: The Google Alerts RSS item matches the brand keyword, is analyzed as a positive mention, and the workflow saves the enriched record, shares it to general monitoring, and routes positive mentions to the brand-wins Slack channel.

events.input: Scheduled trigger firing at 08:00 UTC on Monday 2024-01-15 to initiate weekly brand mention digest generation for the prior week (2024-01-08 through 2024-01-14).

expect.action: A weekly AI digest covering brand mention trends and key articles from the prior week is posted to Slack for team review.
expect.reason: The Monday 08:00 UTC scheduler triggers the weekly digest generation and Slack posting for the prior week’s mentions.

events.input: Slack bot slash command received from team member Marcus Rivera, Senior Marketing Analyst (user ID: U08XR4291, channel: #brand-research, thread_ts: 1705320000.000100): '/brandbot How many negative mentions did Acme Corp receive in the past 30 days, and what were the main themes?'

expect.action: A Slack reply is posted to the requesting user with the search results from Airtable showing negative Acme Corp mentions from the past 30 days and the main themes found.
expect.reason: The Slack bot query is answered by searching the stored Airtable mention history and returning matching results in Slack.

events.input: A new article has appeared in the Google Alerts RSS feed matching the keyword 'Acme products'. Article URL: https://www.gadgetreviewer.com/2024/02/05/acme-homehub-pro-review. Headline: 'Acme HomeHub Pro Review: Feature-Rich Device Held Back by Battery Life'. Raw content: 'In this hands-on review, Gadget Reviewer praised the Acme HomeHub Pro for its fast setup, strong sound quality, and polished app experience, but criticized its short battery life and premium price. Reviewer Elena Park wrote that the product is a solid option for power users, though not the best fit for travelers. The piece focuses on the device itself and does not discuss Acme Corp leadership, finances, or company-wide concerns.'

expect.action: A new brand mention record is created in Airtable for the Gadget Reviewer article, including the headline, summary, notable quote(s), source details, keyword match, sentiment classification as Neutral, and the article URL; the mention is posted to Slack in the general monitoring destination only.
expect.reason: The article is a product review limited to the device itself, so it is stored and shared for monitoring but does not trigger positive or negative triage.

events.input: A new article has appeared in the Google Alerts RSS feed matching the keyword 'Acme products'. Article URL: https://www.consumertechdaily.com/2024/02/06/acme-soundbar-max-review. Headline: 'Acme SoundBar Max Review: Great Audio, Frustrating Remote'. Raw content: 'Consumer Tech Daily tested the Acme SoundBar Max and found that audio quality was excellent for the price, while the remote control design and menu navigation were frustrating. The review concludes that the hardware is impressive but the accessory experience needs work. The article is limited to product performance observations and does not raise concerns about Acme Corp beyond this single review.'

expect.action: A new brand mention record is created in Airtable for the Consumer Tech Daily article, including the headline, summary, notable quote(s), source details, keyword match, sentiment classification as Neutral, and the article URL; the mention is posted to Slack in the general monitoring destination only.
expect.reason: Under the active modification, a product review with mixed feedback is treated as a limited product-level mention rather than a broader company problem, so it is monitored but not escalated.

events.input: Scheduled trigger firing at 08:00 UTC on Monday 2024-02-12 to initiate weekly brand mention digest generation for the prior week (2024-02-05 through 2024-02-11).

expect.action: A weekly AI digest covering brand mention trends and key articles from the prior week is posted to Slack for team review.
expect.reason: The Monday 08:00 UTC scheduler triggers the weekly digest generation and Slack posting for the prior week’s mentions.

expect.reason: The article describes cross-line complaints and broader reputational risk, so it is stored, monitored, and escalated as a negative mention requiring approval.

tools.description: Create or update an article record in the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table.
Post a brand mention message to the specified Slack channel.
Send a direct message in Slack to the requesting team member with search results or follow-up information.

name: Brand Media Monitoring & Sentiment Triage

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Google Alerts-based RSS feed for new articles mentioning Acme Corp or specified keywords
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen article identifiers and timestamps to detect new mentions
objects.behavior: Poll the RSS feed continuously for new articles matching 'Acme Corp', 'AcmeCorp', or 'Acme products'. When a new article is detected, forward its URL, headline, and raw content to the brand-mention-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward new article URL, headline, and raw content when a new mention is detected

event_sources: external system: Google Alerts RSS feed new item

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes article content, extracts key information, classifies sentiment, and routes mentions appropriately
objects.state_description: Tracks current article being processed including extracted headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, and routing decisions
objects.behavior: When a new article payload arrives, extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source metadata. Classify sentiment as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on tone, language, and context of the article. Once analysis is complete, send the enriched record to brand-mentions-store and slack-brand-monitoring simultaneously. If sentiment is Positive, also send to slack-brand-wins. If sentiment is Negative, also send to slack-brand-escalations for approval workflow. When a weekly digest trigger arrives from the weekly-digest-scheduler, generate a comprehensive summary of trends and key articles from the prior week and send it to slack-brand-weekly-digest. When a natural language search query arrives from slack-bot-interface, query brand-mentions-store for matching records and return results to slack-bot-interface. When an error notification arrives from any peer or internal failure occurs, send an alert to slack-brand-monitoring-alerts.

peers.relationship: Send enriched article record with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment, and metadata for persistent storage
Send enriched mention with sentiment classification for all new articles
Send positive mention details when sentiment is classified as Positive
Send negative mention details requiring Communications Manager approval when sentiment is classified as Negative
Send generated weekly digest summarizing trends and key articles every Monday at 08:00 UTC
Send error alert with failure details when any part of the workflow fails

objects.role: Entry-point service object that triggers the weekly digest generation every Monday at 08:00 UTC
objects.state_description: Tracks the last scheduled trigger time to ensure no duplicate triggers
objects.behavior: At 08:00 UTC every Monday, emit a scheduled trigger event to the brand-mention-analysis peer to initiate weekly digest generation.

peers.relationship: Send weekly digest trigger every Monday at 08:00 UTC

event_sources: scheduled trigger: every Monday at 08:00 UTC

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the custom Slack bot that receives natural language search queries from team members and returns results
objects.state_description: Tracks active search queries and their requestors
objects.behavior: When a team member submits a natural language search query via the Slack bot, forward the query and requestor context to brand-mention-analysis. When search results are received from brand-mention-analysis, post them back to the requesting team member in Slack.

peers.relationship: Forward natural language search queries from team members for processing

event_sources: webhook: Slack bot slash command or mention event

objects.role: Write and read service object representing the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table used to persist and query article records
objects.state_description: Accumulates all tracked article records with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, source URL, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an enriched article record is received, write it to the Airtable Brand Mentions base, Tracked Articles table. When answering queries, call the `brand_mentions_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring Slack channel where all new brand mentions are posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all posted brand mention messages with sentiment and article details
objects.behavior: When a brand mention message is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring Slack channel including the headline, sentiment classification, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-wins Slack channel where positive brand mentions are shared
objects.state_description: Accumulates all positive mention messages posted to the channel
objects.behavior: When a positive mention message is received, post it to the #brand-wins Slack channel including the headline, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-escalations Slack channel where negative mentions are posted for Communications Manager approval
objects.state_description: Accumulates all negative mention escalation messages and their approval status
objects.behavior: When a negative mention escalation is received, post it to the #brand-escalations Slack channel with the article details, sentiment rationale, and a request for Communications Manager sign-off before any response is issued.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel where the Monday weekly digest is posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all weekly digest posts with their timestamps
objects.behavior: When a weekly digest message is received, post it to the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel including trend summaries and highlighted key articles from the prior week.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel where workflow failure alerts are sent to the Marketing Operations Administrator
objects.state_description: Accumulates all error and failure alert messages with timestamps and failure details
objects.behavior: When a workflow failure alert is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel with the failure description, affected workflow step, and timestamp so the Marketing Operations Administrator can investigate.

steps: The workflow continuously checks a Google Alerts RSS feed for newly published articles that mention the brand or configured keyword variants, such as alternate brand spellings or product-related terms. For each new article found, it captures the article link, headline, and available content for downstream processing.

When a new mention is detected, the workflow uses AI to extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source information from the article, then classifies the mention as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on the article’s tone, language, and context.
The enriched mention record is automatically saved in Airtable so the organization maintains a searchable historical record of brand coverage over time.

After analysis, every mention is posted to Slack for monitoring, and the workflow routes it based on sentiment. All mentions are shared to a general monitoring destination, while sentiment determines whether any additional Slack triage occurs.

If a mention is classified as Positive, it is immediately shared to a Slack destination for brand wins. If it is classified as Negative, it is sent to a separate Slack escalation path that includes the sentiment rationale and requests approval before any response is issued; Neutral mentions are only included in the general monitoring flow.
Every Monday at 08:00 UTC, the workflow generates a weekly AI digest covering trends and key articles from the prior week, then posts that summary to Slack for team review.

Team members can submit natural-language search questions through a Slack chatbot, and the workflow searches the Airtable record of stored mentions for relevant matches before returning the results in Slack to the requesting user.
If any part of the workflow fails or reports an error, the system sends an alert in Slack with the failure description, the affected step, and a timestamp so an administrator can investigate.

modifications.intent: For the really rough mentions, can we treat the clearly crisis-looking negative stories as something that should get flagged to the escalation side a bit more aggressively?

The story says customer complaints have increased over the last two weeks and includes interviews with two families seeking answers from the company. Crisis communications experts quoted in the piece said the issue now poses a significant reputational threat unless Acme responds swiftly and transparently.'

expect.reason: A newly detected Google Alerts mention is analyzed, stored in Airtable, and negative mentions are routed to the escalation path for review before any response is issued.

tools.description: Create or update an article record in the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table.
Post a brand mention message to the specified Slack channel.
Send a direct message in Slack to the requesting team member with search results or follow-up information.

name: Brand Media Monitoring & Sentiment Triage

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Google Alerts-based RSS feed for new articles mentioning Acme Corp or specified keywords
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen article identifiers and timestamps to detect new mentions
objects.behavior: Poll the RSS feed continuously for new articles matching 'Acme Corp', 'AcmeCorp', or 'Acme products'. When a new article is detected, forward its URL, headline, and raw content to the brand-mention-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward new article URL, headline, and raw content when a new mention is detected

event_sources: external system: Google Alerts RSS feed new item

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes article content, extracts key information, classifies sentiment, and routes mentions appropriately
objects.state_description: Tracks current article being processed including extracted headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, and routing decisions
objects.behavior: When a new article payload arrives, extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source metadata. Classify sentiment as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on tone, language, and context of the article. Once analysis is complete, send the enriched record to brand-mentions-store and slack-brand-monitoring simultaneously. If sentiment is Positive, also send to slack-brand-wins. If sentiment is Negative, also send to slack-brand-escalations for approval workflow. When a weekly digest trigger arrives from the weekly-digest-scheduler, generate a comprehensive summary of trends and key articles from the prior week and send it to slack-brand-weekly-digest. When a natural language search query arrives from slack-bot-interface, query brand-mentions-store for matching records and return results to slack-bot-interface. When an error notification arrives from any peer or internal failure occurs, send an alert to slack-brand-monitoring-alerts.

peers.relationship: Send enriched article record with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment, and metadata for persistent storage
Send enriched mention with sentiment classification for all new articles
Send positive mention details when sentiment is classified as Positive
Send negative mention details requiring Communications Manager approval when sentiment is classified as Negative
Send generated weekly digest summarizing trends and key articles every Monday at 08:00 UTC
Send error alert with failure details when any part of the workflow fails

objects.role: Entry-point service object that triggers the weekly digest generation every Monday at 08:00 UTC
objects.state_description: Tracks the last scheduled trigger time to ensure no duplicate triggers
objects.behavior: At 08:00 UTC every Monday, emit a scheduled trigger event to the brand-mention-analysis peer to initiate weekly digest generation.

peers.relationship: Send weekly digest trigger every Monday at 08:00 UTC

event_sources: scheduled trigger: every Monday at 08:00 UTC

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the custom Slack bot that receives natural language search queries from team members and returns results
objects.state_description: Tracks active search queries and their requestors
objects.behavior: When a team member submits a natural language search query via the Slack bot, forward the query and requestor context to brand-mention-analysis. When search results are received from brand-mention-analysis, post them back to the requesting team member in Slack.

peers.relationship: Forward natural language search queries from team members for processing

event_sources: webhook: Slack bot slash command or mention event

objects.role: Write and read service object representing the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table used to persist and query article records
objects.state_description: Accumulates all tracked article records with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, source URL, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an enriched article record is received, write it to the Airtable Brand Mentions base, Tracked Articles table. When answering queries, call the `brand_mentions_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring Slack channel where all new brand mentions are posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all posted brand mention messages with sentiment and article details
objects.behavior: When a brand mention message is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring Slack channel including the headline, sentiment classification, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-wins Slack channel where positive brand mentions are shared
objects.state_description: Accumulates all positive mention messages posted to the channel
objects.behavior: When a positive mention message is received, post it to the #brand-wins Slack channel including the headline, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-escalations Slack channel where negative mentions are posted for Communications Manager approval
objects.state_description: Accumulates all negative mention escalation messages and their approval status
objects.behavior: When a negative mention escalation is received, post it to the #brand-escalations Slack channel with the article details, sentiment rationale, and a request for Communications Manager sign-off before any response is issued.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel where the Monday weekly digest is posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all weekly digest posts with their timestamps
objects.behavior: When a weekly digest message is received, post it to the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel including trend summaries and highlighted key articles from the prior week.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel where workflow failure alerts are sent to the Marketing Operations Administrator
objects.state_description: Accumulates all error and failure alert messages with timestamps and failure details
objects.behavior: When a workflow failure alert is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel with the failure description, affected workflow step, and timestamp so the Marketing Operations Administrator can investigate.

steps: The workflow continuously checks a Google Alerts RSS feed for newly published articles that mention the brand or configured keyword variants, such as alternate brand spellings or product-related terms. For each new article found, it captures the article link, headline, and available content for downstream processing.

When a new mention is detected, the workflow uses AI to extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source information from the article, then classifies the mention as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on the article’s tone, language, and context.
The enriched mention record is automatically saved in Airtable so the organization maintains a searchable historical record of brand coverage over time.

After analysis, every mention is posted to Slack for monitoring, and the workflow routes it based on sentiment. All mentions are shared to a general monitoring destination, while sentiment determines whether any additional Slack triage occurs.

If a mention is classified as Positive, it is immediately shared to a Slack destination for brand wins. If it is classified as Negative, it is sent to a separate Slack escalation path that includes the sentiment rationale and requests approval before any response is issued; Neutral mentions are only included in the general monitoring flow.
Every Monday at 08:00 UTC, the workflow generates a weekly AI digest covering trends and key articles from the prior week, then posts that summary to Slack for team review.

Team members can submit natural-language search questions through a Slack chatbot, and the workflow searches the Airtable record of stored mentions for relevant matches before returning the results in Slack to the requesting user.
If any part of the workflow fails or reports an error, the system sends an alert in Slack with the failure description, the affected step, and a timestamp so an administrator can investigate.

modifications.intent: Stop sending negative press mentions to slack-brand-escalations; keep analyzing them and posting them in slack-brand-monitoring, but do not open the approval workflow for those articles anymore.

The review described the company as gaining momentum with buyers seeking easier implementation and dependable service.'

tools.description: Create or update an article record in the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table.
Post a brand mention message to the specified Slack channel.
Send a direct message in Slack to the requesting team member with search results or follow-up information.

name: Brand Media Monitoring & Sentiment Triage

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Google Alerts-based RSS feed for new articles mentioning Acme Corp or specified keywords
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen article identifiers and timestamps to detect new mentions
objects.behavior: Poll the RSS feed continuously for new articles matching 'Acme Corp', 'AcmeCorp', or 'Acme products'. When a new article is detected, forward its URL, headline, and raw content to the brand-mention-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward new article URL, headline, and raw content when a new mention is detected

event_sources: external system: Google Alerts RSS feed new item

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes article content, extracts key information, classifies sentiment, and routes mentions appropriately
objects.state_description: Tracks current article being processed including extracted headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, and routing decisions
objects.behavior: When a new article payload arrives, extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source metadata. Classify sentiment as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on tone, language, and context of the article. Once analysis is complete, send the enriched record to brand-mentions-store and slack-brand-monitoring simultaneously. If sentiment is Positive, also send to slack-brand-wins. If sentiment is Negative, also send to slack-brand-escalations for approval workflow. When a weekly digest trigger arrives from the weekly-digest-scheduler, generate a comprehensive summary of trends and key articles from the prior week and send it to slack-brand-weekly-digest. When a natural language search query arrives from slack-bot-interface, query brand-mentions-store for matching records and return results to slack-bot-interface. When an error notification arrives from any peer or internal failure occurs, send an alert to slack-brand-monitoring-alerts.

peers.relationship: Send enriched article record with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment, and metadata for persistent storage
Send enriched mention with sentiment classification for all new articles
Send positive mention details when sentiment is classified as Positive
Send negative mention details requiring Communications Manager approval when sentiment is classified as Negative
Send generated weekly digest summarizing trends and key articles every Monday at 08:00 UTC
Send error alert with failure details when any part of the workflow fails

objects.role: Entry-point service object that triggers the weekly digest generation every Monday at 08:00 UTC
objects.state_description: Tracks the last scheduled trigger time to ensure no duplicate triggers
objects.behavior: At 08:00 UTC every Monday, emit a scheduled trigger event to the brand-mention-analysis peer to initiate weekly digest generation.

peers.relationship: Send weekly digest trigger every Monday at 08:00 UTC

event_sources: scheduled trigger: every Monday at 08:00 UTC

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the custom Slack bot that receives natural language search queries from team members and returns results
objects.state_description: Tracks active search queries and their requestors
objects.behavior: When a team member submits a natural language search query via the Slack bot, forward the query and requestor context to brand-mention-analysis. When search results are received from brand-mention-analysis, post them back to the requesting team member in Slack.

peers.relationship: Forward natural language search queries from team members for processing

event_sources: webhook: Slack bot slash command or mention event

objects.role: Write and read service object representing the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table used to persist and query article records
objects.state_description: Accumulates all tracked article records with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment classification, source URL, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an enriched article record is received, write it to the Airtable Brand Mentions base, Tracked Articles table. When answering queries, call the `brand_mentions_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring Slack channel where all new brand mentions are posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all posted brand mention messages with sentiment and article details
objects.behavior: When a brand mention message is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring Slack channel including the headline, sentiment classification, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-wins Slack channel where positive brand mentions are shared
objects.state_description: Accumulates all positive mention messages posted to the channel
objects.behavior: When a positive mention message is received, post it to the #brand-wins Slack channel including the headline, summary, and source link.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-escalations Slack channel where negative mentions are posted for Communications Manager approval
objects.state_description: Accumulates all negative mention escalation messages and their approval status
objects.behavior: When a negative mention escalation is received, post it to the #brand-escalations Slack channel with the article details, sentiment rationale, and a request for Communications Manager sign-off before any response is issued.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel where the Monday weekly digest is posted
objects.state_description: Accumulates all weekly digest posts with their timestamps
objects.behavior: When a weekly digest message is received, post it to the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel including trend summaries and highlighted key articles from the prior week.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel where workflow failure alerts are sent to the Marketing Operations Administrator
objects.state_description: Accumulates all error and failure alert messages with timestamps and failure details
objects.behavior: When a workflow failure alert is received, post it to the #brand-monitoring-alerts Slack channel with the failure description, affected workflow step, and timestamp so the Marketing Operations Administrator can investigate.

steps: The workflow continuously checks a Google Alerts RSS feed for newly published articles that mention the brand or configured keyword variants, such as alternate brand spellings or product-related terms. For each new article found, it captures the article link, headline, and available content for downstream processing.

When a new mention is detected, the workflow uses AI to extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, and source information from the article, then classifies the mention as Positive, Neutral, or Negative based on the article’s tone, language, and context.
The enriched mention record is automatically saved in Airtable so the organization maintains a searchable historical record of brand coverage over time.

After analysis, every mention is posted to Slack for monitoring, and the workflow routes it based on sentiment. All mentions are shared to a general monitoring destination, while sentiment determines whether any additional Slack triage occurs.

If a mention is classified as Positive, it is immediately shared to a Slack destination for brand wins. If it is classified as Negative, it is sent to a separate Slack escalation path that includes the sentiment rationale and requests approval before any response is issued; Neutral mentions are only included in the general monitoring flow.
Every Monday at 08:00 UTC, the workflow generates a weekly AI digest covering trends and key articles from the prior week, then posts that summary to Slack for team review.

Team members can submit natural-language search questions through a Slack chatbot, and the workflow searches the Airtable record of stored mentions for relevant matches before returning the results in Slack to the requesting user.
If any part of the workflow fails or reports an error, the system sends an alert in Slack with the failure description, the affected step, and a timestamp so an administrator can investigate.

modifications.intent: Let’s make sure leadership-change coverage doesn’t just land in the usual monitoring flow — if a new article is about an Acme executive appointment, departure, or promotion, give #brand-wins a copy too, even when the tone itself isn’t especially glowing.

tools.description: Create or update an article record in the Airtable Brand Mentions base Tracked Articles table.
Post a brand mention message to the specified Slack channel.

name: Slack-ClickUp Support Automation

objects.role: Slack entry-point that receives /support slash command submissions from users in #help-desk and forwards them to the support-triage business logic object
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming support requests with user ID, request text, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a /support slash command event arrives, forward the full request payload (user ID, request text, channel, timestamp) to support-triage via peer message. When a ticket status update command arrives from a team member, forward it to support-triage.

peers.relationship: Forward support request payload when a /support slash command is received
Forward ticket status update commands from team members

event_sources: webhook: Slack /support slash command
webhook: Slack ticket status update action from team member

objects.role: Dual-role Slack write service that posts ticket notifications to #support-queue and receives status update actions from team members, forwarding them to support-triage
objects.state_description: Audit log of all messages posted to #support-queue and status update actions received
objects.behavior: Record all incoming messages and post them to the #support-queue channel. When a team member submits a status update action (e.g., marking a ticket In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved) from within #support-queue, forward the action payload to support-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward team member status update actions received in #support-queue

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action from team member in #support-queue

objects.role: Read service representing the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence, answering queries about existing solutions for common issues
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles on demand
objects.behavior: Respond to queries about known issues and solutions by searching the IT Support knowledge base. When answering queries, call the `confluence_kb_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies support requests, resolves common issues instantly, creates ClickUp tickets for complex issues, assigns priority and routing, and orchestrates all status transitions
objects.state_description: Tracks active support requests with request ID, user ID, request text, classification, priority, assigned team, ClickUp ticket ID, and current status (Open, In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved)
objects.behavior: When a support request arrives: query confluence-kb for matching solutions. If a known solution is found, send the step-by-step instructions to user-dm-notifier. If no solution is found or the issue is complex, classify the request into category (Bug, Access Request, Hardware, General Inquiry) and assign priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low) based on request content and keywords. Determine the appropriate team (Tier 1 Support, Tier 2 Engineering, or Infrastructure) based on category and current workload tracked in state. Send the ticket creation payload to clickup-tickets and simultaneously send the team notification to support-queue-channel and send an acknowledgement to user-dm-notifier. When a status update arrives (In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved): update the tracked status in state, send the status notification to user-dm-notifier, and if status is Resolved, send the resolution details to confluence-kb-updater. On Monday at 09:00 UTC, compute weekly report metrics from state (common issues, average resolution times, team performance) and send the report to support-metrics-channel.

peers.relationship: Query for matching solutions when a new support request arrives
Send ticket creation payload with category, priority, and assigned team for complex or unrecognized requests
Send team notification with ticket ID, category, priority, and context when a new ticket is created
Send instant solution instructions for resolved common issues, acknowledgement messages, and status transition notifications to the requesting user
Send resolution details and new solution content when a ticket is marked Resolved
Send weekly report with common issues, average resolution times, and team performance metrics every Monday at 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Write service that creates and updates tickets in ClickUp under the Customer Support space
objects.state_description: Audit log of all ticket creation and update payloads sent to ClickUp, including ticket ID, category, priority, assigned team, and status
objects.behavior: Record all incoming ticket creation and update requests and write them to ClickUp under the Customer Support space with the specified category, priority, assigned team, and status.

objects.role: Write service that sends direct Slack messages to requesting users with solutions, acknowledgements, and status update notifications
objects.state_description: Audit log of all direct messages sent to users, including user ID, message content, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Record all incoming message payloads and send them as direct Slack messages to the specified user in Slack.

objects.role: Write service that updates the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence with new solutions from resolved tickets
objects.state_description: Audit log of all knowledge base update payloads written to Confluence, including category, issue description, and solution content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming resolution payloads and write new solution articles to the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence under the relevant category.

objects.role: Write service that posts weekly support metrics reports to the #support-metrics Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit log of all weekly report messages posted to #support-metrics, including timestamp and report content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming report payloads and post them to the #support-metrics channel in Slack.

steps: A user submits an IT support request in Slack through a support command, and the workflow captures the request details such as the requester, message content, source conversation, and submission time for triage.
The workflow analyzes the Slack request and queries the IT support knowledge base in Confluence to determine whether the issue matches an existing documented solution.
If Confluence contains a known fix, the workflow sends the user a direct Slack message with step-by-step instructions so common issues can be resolved immediately without creating a ticket.

If no matching knowledge base solution is found or the request is judged to be complex, the workflow creates a ticket in ClickUp and categorizes it as a Bug, Access Request, Hardware issue, or General Inquiry.

For requests that become tickets, the workflow assigns a priority of Urgent, High, Medium, or Low based on the request content and keywords, then routes the work to the most appropriate team—such as frontline support, engineering, or infrastructure—based on issue type and current workload.

After routing, the workflow posts a Slack notification to the support team with the relevant ticket context, and team members can send status changes such as In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved back through Slack.

Whenever a ticket status changes, the workflow sends the requesting user a direct Slack update so they stay informed throughout the resolution process, including acknowledgement when a ticket is first created.

When a ticket is marked Resolved, the workflow sends the resolution details to Confluence so new solution articles can be added to the IT support knowledge base under the appropriate category for future reuse.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow compiles support metrics from its tracked ticket history—including common issue patterns, average resolution times, and team performance—and posts the report to Slack.

modifications.intent: If the knowledge base already has the fix and we can answer right away, we don't need the extra 'we got your request' note on top of the instructions.

I get error code 0x800704ec when trying to connect. My machine is running Windows 11 Pro, version 23H2.' User ID: U04MCHEN, channel: #help-desk, timestamp: 1717200000.000100.

expect.action: A direct Slack message is sent to Maya Chen (user ID U04MCHEN) with the published Confluence knowledge base fix for VPN error 0x800704ec on Windows 11 Pro 23H2, including step-by-step instructions to verify the error, check firewall rules, re-enable the VPN protocol in the registry, restart VPN-related Windows services, and reinstall the VPN client if needed.
expect.reason: The Slack request matches published Confluence article KB-20481, so the workflow resolves it immediately by sending the known fix instead of creating a ClickUp ticket.

events.input: Team member Jordan Lee submits a status update action from within #support-queue in Slack, marking ticket TKT-1042 (VPN connectivity issue for Maya Chen) as 'In Progress'. Action payload includes ticket ID TKT-1042, new status 'In Progress', team member ID U09JLEE, channel: #support-queue, timestamp: 1717200500.000200.

expect.action: A direct Slack update is sent to the original requester for ticket TKT-1042 stating that the ticket status is now 'In Progress'.
expect.reason: Whenever a ticket status changes through Slack, the workflow notifies the requesting user of the new status.
