name: Slack-ClickUp Support Automation

objects.role: Slack entry-point that receives /support slash command submissions from users in #help-desk and forwards them to the support-triage business logic object
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming support requests with user ID, request text, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a /support slash command event arrives, forward the full request payload (user ID, request text, channel, timestamp) to support-triage via peer message. When a ticket status update command arrives from a team member, forward it to support-triage.

peers.relationship: Forward support request payload when a /support slash command is received
Forward ticket status update commands from team members

event_sources: webhook: Slack /support slash command
webhook: Slack ticket status update action from team member

objects.role: Dual-role Slack write service that posts ticket notifications to #support-queue and receives status update actions from team members, forwarding them to support-triage
objects.state_description: Audit log of all messages posted to #support-queue and status update actions received
objects.behavior: Record all incoming messages and post them to the #support-queue channel. When a team member submits a status update action (e.g., marking a ticket In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved) from within #support-queue, forward the action payload to support-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward team member status update actions received in #support-queue

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action from team member in #support-queue

objects.role: Read service representing the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence, answering queries about existing solutions for common issues
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles on demand
objects.behavior: Respond to queries about known issues and solutions by searching the IT Support knowledge base. When answering queries, call the `confluence_kb_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies support requests, resolves common issues instantly, creates ClickUp tickets for complex issues, assigns priority and routing, and orchestrates all status transitions
objects.state_description: Tracks active support requests with request ID, user ID, request text, classification, priority, assigned team, ClickUp ticket ID, and current status (Open, In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved)
objects.behavior: When a support request arrives: query confluence-kb for matching solutions. If a known solution is found, send the step-by-step instructions to user-dm-notifier. If no solution is found or the issue is complex, classify the request into category (Bug, Access Request, Hardware, General Inquiry) and assign priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low) based on request content and keywords. Determine the appropriate team (Tier 1 Support, Tier 2 Engineering, or Infrastructure) based on category and current workload tracked in state. Send the ticket creation payload to clickup-tickets and simultaneously send the team notification to support-queue-channel and send an acknowledgement to user-dm-notifier. When a status update arrives (In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved): update the tracked status in state, send the status notification to user-dm-notifier, and if status is Resolved, send the resolution details to confluence-kb-updater. On Monday at 09:00 UTC, compute weekly report metrics from state (common issues, average resolution times, team performance) and send the report to support-metrics-channel.

peers.relationship: Query for matching solutions when a new support request arrives
Send ticket creation payload with category, priority, and assigned team for complex or unrecognized requests
Send team notification with ticket ID, category, priority, and context when a new ticket is created
Send instant solution instructions for resolved common issues, acknowledgement messages, and status transition notifications to the requesting user
Send resolution details and new solution content when a ticket is marked Resolved
Send weekly report with common issues, average resolution times, and team performance metrics every Monday at 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Write service that creates and updates tickets in ClickUp under the Customer Support space
objects.state_description: Audit log of all ticket creation and update payloads sent to ClickUp, including ticket ID, category, priority, assigned team, and status
objects.behavior: Record all incoming ticket creation and update requests and write them to ClickUp under the Customer Support space with the specified category, priority, assigned team, and status.

objects.role: Write service that sends direct Slack messages to requesting users with solutions, acknowledgements, and status update notifications
objects.state_description: Audit log of all direct messages sent to users, including user ID, message content, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Record all incoming message payloads and send them as direct Slack messages to the specified user in Slack.

objects.role: Write service that updates the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence with new solutions from resolved tickets
objects.state_description: Audit log of all knowledge base update payloads written to Confluence, including category, issue description, and solution content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming resolution payloads and write new solution articles to the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence under the relevant category.

objects.role: Write service that posts weekly support metrics reports to the #support-metrics Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit log of all weekly report messages posted to #support-metrics, including timestamp and report content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming report payloads and post them to the #support-metrics channel in Slack.

steps: A user submits an IT support request in Slack through a support command, and the workflow captures the request details such as the requester, message content, source conversation, and submission time for triage.
The workflow analyzes the Slack request and queries the IT support knowledge base in Confluence to determine whether the issue matches an existing documented solution.
If Confluence contains a known fix, the workflow sends the user a direct Slack message with step-by-step instructions so common issues can be resolved immediately without creating a ticket.

If no matching knowledge base solution is found or the request is judged to be complex, the workflow creates a ticket in ClickUp and categorizes it as a Bug, Access Request, Hardware issue, or General Inquiry.

For requests that become tickets, the workflow assigns a priority of Urgent, High, Medium, or Low based on the request content and keywords, then routes the work to the most appropriate team—such as frontline support, engineering, or infrastructure—based on issue type and current workload.

After routing, the workflow posts a Slack notification to the support team with the relevant ticket context, and team members can send status changes such as In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved back through Slack.

Whenever a ticket status changes, the workflow sends the requesting user a direct Slack update so they stay informed throughout the resolution process, including acknowledgement when a ticket is first created.

When a ticket is marked Resolved, the workflow sends the resolution details to Confluence so new solution articles can be added to the IT support knowledge base under the appropriate category for future reuse.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow compiles support metrics from its tracked ticket history—including common issue patterns, average resolution times, and team performance—and posts the report to Slack.

modifications.intent: For support requests that explicitly mention VPN error code 0x800704ec on Windows 11 Pro version 23H2, treat that exact issue as a known solution case and send only the step-by-step fix to the requesting user through user-dm-notifier instead of creating a ClickUp ticket or posting to support-queue-channel.

events.input: User Maya Chen submits a /support slash command in the #help-desk channel with the message: 'I cannot connect to the VPN after the latest Windows update. I get error code 0x800704ec when trying to connect. My machine is running Windows 11 Pro, version 23H2.' User ID: U04MCHEN, channel: #help-desk, timestamp: 1717200000.000100.

expect.action: A ClickUp ticket is created for Maya Chen’s VPN connectivity issue and categorized as Bug. The ticket is assigned Urgent priority because the request describes a work-blocking VPN failure with a specific error after a Windows update, and it is routed to Tier 2 Engineering. A Slack notification is posted in #support-queue with the new ticket details and routing context. Maya Chen receives a direct Slack message acknowledging that her support ticket was created and is being worked.
expect.reason: Under baseline behavior, the workflow checks Confluence for a known solution and, if no exact known-fix handling applies, creates and routes a ClickUp ticket with Slack notifications and requester acknowledgement.

events.input: Team member Jordan Lee submits a status update action from within #support-queue in Slack, marking ticket TKT-1042 (VPN connectivity issue for Maya Chen) as 'In Progress'. Action payload includes ticket ID TKT-1042, new status 'In Progress', team member ID U09JLEE, channel: #support-queue, timestamp: 1717200500.000200.

expect.action: The ClickUp ticket TKT-1042 is updated to status 'In Progress'. The original requester for TKT-1042 receives a direct Slack message that the VPN connectivity ticket is now In Progress.
expect.reason: When a support ticket status update is submitted from Slack, the workflow records the new status in ClickUp and notifies the requesting user by direct Slack message.

expect.action: [rerouted by M001] The requesting user receives a direct Slack message containing only the step-by-step fix from Confluence article KB-20481 for 'VPN Connection Failure with Error Code 0x800704ec on Windows 11'. [suppressed by M001] No ClickUp ticket is created for this request. [suppressed by M001] No Slack notification is posted to #support-queue for this request.
expect.reason: M001 applies because the request explicitly mentions VPN error code 0x800704ec on Windows 11 Pro version 23H2, so the workflow must treat it as a known-solution case and send only the documented fix instead of creating and routing a ticket.

Request text: "My monitor dock is not being detected after I moved desks this morning. The laptop charges but the external displays stay black. I am using a Dell WD19 dock and a Lenovo T14." Channel: #help-desk.

expect.action: A ClickUp ticket is created for Priya’s dock and external display issue and categorized as Hardware. The ticket is assigned Medium priority and routed to Infrastructure. A Slack notification is posted in #support-queue with the new ticket details and routing context. Priya receives a direct Slack message acknowledging that her support ticket was created and routed for support.
expect.reason: [M001 condition not met: the request is a hardware dock issue, not the exact Windows 11 Pro 23H2 VPN error 0x800704ec case] Under baseline behavior, hardware requests without a matching knowledge base fix become Infrastructure tickets with Slack notifications and requester acknowledgement.

events.input: Slack /support submission in #help-desk from user U15OMAR at timestamp 1717492200.000400. Thread/session ID: hd-1717492200.000400. Request text: "VPN is failing after a Windows update, but the error I see is 809, not 0x800704ec. My laptop is Windows 11 Pro version 23H2 and I have already rebooted twice." Channel: #help-desk.

expect.action: A ClickUp ticket is created for Omar’s VPN issue and categorized as Bug. The ticket is assigned High priority and routed to Tier 2 Engineering. A Slack notification is posted in #support-queue with the new ticket details and routing context. Omar receives a direct Slack message acknowledging that ticket TKT-2088 was created and is being worked.
expect.reason: [M001 condition not met: the request mentions VPN error 809, not the exact 0x800704ec issue] Under baseline behavior, a complex VPN failure without the exact known-fix match becomes a routed ClickUp ticket with requester acknowledgement.

events.input: Slack status update action submitted from within #support-queue by team member U09JLEE at timestamp 1717510800.000500. Ticket ID: TKT-2088. Thread/session ID: sq-1717510800.000500. New status: Resolved. Resolution details: "Reset the user's VPN adapter, removed the stale profile created after the Windows patch, re-imported the corporate VPN configuration, and confirmed successful connection." Original requester user ID: U15OMAR. Original issue summary: "VPN failing after Windows update with error 809 on Windows 11 Pro 23H2." Channel: #support-queue.

expect.action: The ClickUp ticket TKT-2088 is updated to status 'Resolved'. Omar receives a direct Slack message that ticket TKT-2088 has been resolved, including the resolution summary: the VPN adapter was reset, the stale profile created after the Windows patch was removed, the corporate VPN configuration was re-imported, and successful connection was confirmed. The resolution details for this resolved VPN issue are sent to Confluence for addition to the IT support knowledge base under the appropriate category.
expect.reason: When a ticket is marked Resolved, the workflow both notifies the requester of the resolution in Slack and sends the resolution details to Confluence for future knowledge base reuse; M001 does not change resolved-ticket handling for non-0x800704ec cases.

name: Intelligent Multi-Channel Message Routing

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new emails from the Support, Sales, and Billing Gmail inboxes and forwards them for processing.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-fetched timestamps per inbox to avoid duplicate ingestion.
objects.behavior: When new emails arrive in the Support, Sales, or Billing Gmail inboxes, forward each email (subject, body, sender, inbox source, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new email with full content and metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new email in Support Gmail inbox
external system: new email in Sales Gmail inbox
external system: new email in Billing Gmail inbox

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects form submissions from the Jotform contact and inquiry forms on the company website.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submission IDs to prevent duplicate processing.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives from the Jotform contact or inquiry form, forward the full submission (fields, submitter info, form name, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new form submission with full content and metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new Jotform contact form submission
external system: new Jotform inquiry form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects inbound messages from the company Facebook Page, Twitter/X mentions, and LinkedIn inbox.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-fetched message IDs per social channel to avoid duplicate ingestion.
objects.behavior: When new messages, mentions, or inbox items arrive from Facebook Page, Twitter/X, or LinkedIn, forward each message (content, sender, platform, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new social message with full content and platform metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new Facebook Page message
external system: new Twitter/X mention
external system: new LinkedIn inbox message

objects.role: Business logic object that uses GPT-4o to categorize, summarize, and prioritize each inbound message, then routes it to the appropriate team notification and records it.
objects.state_description: Holds in-flight message processing state including raw content, assigned category, two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and routing destination for each message being processed.
objects.behavior: When a message arrives from any source channel: (1) Call GPT-4o to categorize the message as exactly one of: Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing Question, or General Feedback. (2) Call GPT-4o to generate a two-sentence summary of the message. (3) Call GPT-4o to assign a priority level of High, Medium, or Low based on urgency and content. (4) Based on category, determine routing: Sales Inquiry → Sales Team (#sales-inbound), Technical Support → Technical Support Team (#support-tickets), Billing Question → Billing Team (#billing-requests), General Feedback → Customer Success Team (#customer-success). (5) Simultaneously send the enriched message record (original content, source, category, summary, priority, team, timestamp, initial status 'New') to slack-notifier and airtable-active-messages and airtable-all-messages.

peers.relationship: Send enriched message record with category, summary, priority, source, and target Slack channel name for instant team notification.
Send enriched message record to be written to the Active Messages table.
Send enriched message record to be written to the All Messages dashboard table.

objects.role: Write service that posts instant notifications to the appropriate Slack channel based on the routed message category.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all Slack notifications sent, including channel, message summary, priority, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, inspect the target channel field and post a formatted notification to the designated Slack channel: Sales Inquiry messages go to #sales-inbound, Technical Support messages go to #support-tickets, Billing Question messages go to #billing-requests, and General Feedback messages go to #customer-success. Each notification includes the two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and sender information.

objects.role: Write service that records each routed message into the Active Messages table in the Message Routing Airtable base for team status tracking.
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all message records written to the Active Messages table, including message ID, source, category, summary, priority, assigned team, and current status.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, create a new row in the Message Routing Airtable base, Active Messages table with fields: message ID, source channel, sender, category, two-sentence summary, priority, assigned team, timestamp, and initial status set to 'New'. When team members update the status field to In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved via Airtable, record the status change in the audit log.

objects.role: Write service that maintains a live dashboard in the All Messages table of the Message Routing Airtable base showing every message and its current status across all teams.
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all message records written to the All Messages table, including full message metadata and live status.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, create a new row in the Message Routing Airtable base, All Messages table with fields: message ID, source channel, sender, category, two-sentence summary, priority, assigned team, timestamp, and current status. This table serves as the live cross-team dashboard view and reflects all messages regardless of team assignment.

steps: The workflow monitors multiple Gmail inboxes for new inbound email, specifically support, sales, and billing mailboxes. For each new email, it captures the subject, message body, sender, source inbox, and timestamp so the message can enter a shared intake process.

The workflow collects new submissions from website forms in Jotform, including both contact and inquiry forms. Each submission is passed into the same intake flow with the submitted fields, submitter information, form name, and timestamp.

The workflow gathers inbound social messages from the company’s Facebook Page, Twitter/X mentions, and LinkedIn inbox. For each new message or mention, it captures the content, sender, source platform, and timestamp for downstream handling.

The workflow uses GPT-4o to assign every inbound message to exactly one business category: Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing Question, or General Feedback. This categorization is applied consistently regardless of whether the message came from Gmail, Jotform, Facebook, Twitter/X, or LinkedIn.
The workflow uses GPT-4o to generate a concise two-sentence summary of each inbound message. This gives teams a quick, standardized overview of the message before they begin work.

The workflow uses GPT-4o to assign a priority of High, Medium, or Low to each message based on its urgency and content. That priority is then included with the routed record so teams can focus on the most important items first.

Based on the assigned category, the workflow routes each message to the appropriate team: Sales Inquiry to Sales, Technical Support to Support, Billing Question to Billing, and General Feedback to Customer Success. This routing rule is deterministic and depends entirely on the AI-assigned category.

The workflow sends an immediate notification in Slack to the team responsible for the routed message. Each alert includes the two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and sender information so team members can quickly understand what needs attention.

The workflow allows team members to update the status of messages in Airtable as work progresses. Messages start with a status of New, and when users change the status to In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved, the system records that status change for tracking.

The workflow maintains a live cross-team view of all messages and their current status in Airtable. This dashboard includes every message regardless of assigned team, along with its source, sender, category, summary, priority, routing destination, timestamp, and latest status.

name: Intelligent Multi-Channel Message Routing

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new emails from the Support, Sales, and Billing Gmail inboxes and forwards them for processing.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-fetched timestamps per inbox to avoid duplicate ingestion.
objects.behavior: When new emails arrive in the Support, Sales, or Billing Gmail inboxes, forward each email (subject, body, sender, inbox source, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new email with full content and metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new email in Support Gmail inbox
external system: new email in Sales Gmail inbox
external system: new email in Billing Gmail inbox

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects form submissions from the Jotform contact and inquiry forms on the company website.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submission IDs to prevent duplicate processing.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives from the Jotform contact or inquiry form, forward the full submission (fields, submitter info, form name, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new form submission with full content and metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new Jotform contact form submission
external system: new Jotform inquiry form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects inbound messages from the company Facebook Page, Twitter/X mentions, and LinkedIn inbox.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-fetched message IDs per social channel to avoid duplicate ingestion.
objects.behavior: When new messages, mentions, or inbox items arrive from Facebook Page, Twitter/X, or LinkedIn, forward each message (content, sender, platform, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new social message with full content and platform metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new Facebook Page message
external system: new Twitter/X mention
external system: new LinkedIn inbox message

objects.role: Business logic object that uses GPT-4o to categorize, summarize, and prioritize each inbound message, then routes it to the appropriate team notification and records it.
objects.state_description: Holds in-flight message processing state including raw content, assigned category, two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and routing destination for each message being processed.
objects.behavior: When a message arrives from any source channel: (1) Call GPT-4o to categorize the message as exactly one of: Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing Question, or General Feedback. (2) Call GPT-4o to generate a two-sentence summary of the message. (3) Call GPT-4o to assign a priority level of High, Medium, or Low based on urgency and content. (4) Based on category, determine routing: Sales Inquiry → Sales Team (#sales-inbound), Technical Support → Technical Support Team (#support-tickets), Billing Question → Billing Team (#billing-requests), General Feedback → Customer Success Team (#customer-success). (5) Simultaneously send the enriched message record (original content, source, category, summary, priority, team, timestamp, initial status 'New') to slack-notifier and airtable-active-messages and airtable-all-messages.

peers.relationship: Send enriched message record with category, summary, priority, source, and target Slack channel name for instant team notification.
Send enriched message record to be written to the Active Messages table.
Send enriched message record to be written to the All Messages dashboard table.

objects.role: Write service that posts instant notifications to the appropriate Slack channel based on the routed message category.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all Slack notifications sent, including channel, message summary, priority, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, inspect the target channel field and post a formatted notification to the designated Slack channel: Sales Inquiry messages go to #sales-inbound, Technical Support messages go to #support-tickets, Billing Question messages go to #billing-requests, and General Feedback messages go to #customer-success. Each notification includes the two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and sender information.

objects.role: Write service that records each routed message into the Active Messages table in the Message Routing Airtable base for team status tracking.
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all message records written to the Active Messages table, including message ID, source, category, summary, priority, assigned team, and current status.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, create a new row in the Message Routing Airtable base, Active Messages table with fields: message ID, source channel, sender, category, two-sentence summary, priority, assigned team, timestamp, and initial status set to 'New'. When team members update the status field to In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved via Airtable, record the status change in the audit log.

objects.role: Write service that maintains a live dashboard in the All Messages table of the Message Routing Airtable base showing every message and its current status across all teams.
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all message records written to the All Messages table, including full message metadata and live status.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, create a new row in the Message Routing Airtable base, All Messages table with fields: message ID, source channel, sender, category, two-sentence summary, priority, assigned team, timestamp, and current status. This table serves as the live cross-team dashboard view and reflects all messages regardless of team assignment.

steps: The workflow monitors multiple Gmail inboxes for new inbound email, specifically support, sales, and billing mailboxes. For each new email, it captures the subject, message body, sender, source inbox, and timestamp so the message can enter a shared intake process.

The workflow collects new submissions from website forms in Jotform, including both contact and inquiry forms. Each submission is passed into the same intake flow with the submitted fields, submitter information, form name, and timestamp.

The workflow gathers inbound social messages from the company’s Facebook Page, Twitter/X mentions, and LinkedIn inbox. For each new message or mention, it captures the content, sender, source platform, and timestamp for downstream handling.

The workflow uses GPT-4o to assign every inbound message to exactly one business category: Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing Question, or General Feedback. This categorization is applied consistently regardless of whether the message came from Gmail, Jotform, Facebook, Twitter/X, or LinkedIn.
The workflow uses GPT-4o to generate a concise two-sentence summary of each inbound message. This gives teams a quick, standardized overview of the message before they begin work.

The workflow uses GPT-4o to assign a priority of High, Medium, or Low to each message based on its urgency and content. That priority is then included with the routed record so teams can focus on the most important items first.

Based on the assigned category, the workflow routes each message to the appropriate team: Sales Inquiry to Sales, Technical Support to Support, Billing Question to Billing, and General Feedback to Customer Success. This routing rule is deterministic and depends entirely on the AI-assigned category.

The workflow sends an immediate notification in Slack to the team responsible for the routed message. Each alert includes the two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and sender information so team members can quickly understand what needs attention.

The workflow allows team members to update the status of messages in Airtable as work progresses. Messages start with a status of New, and when users change the status to In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved, the system records that status change for tracking.

The workflow maintains a live cross-team view of all messages and their current status in Airtable. This dashboard includes every message regardless of assigned team, along with its source, sender, category, summary, priority, routing destination, timestamp, and latest status.

(2) Call GPT-4o to generate a two-sentence summary of the message. (3) Call GPT-4o to assign a priority level of High, Medium, or Low based on urgency and content. (4) Based on category, determine routing: Sales Inquiry → Sales Team (#sales-inbound), Technical Support → Technical Support Team (#support-tickets), Billing Question → Billing Team (#billing-requests), General Feedback → Customer Success Team (#customer-success). (5) Simultaneously send the enriched message record (original content, source, category, summary, priority, team, timestamp, initial status 'New') to slack-notifier and airtable-active-messages and airtable-all-messages.

Each notification includes the two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and sender information.

objects.role: Write service that records each routed message into the Active Messages table in the Message Routing Airtable base for team status tracking.
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all message records written to the Active Messages table, including message ID, source, category, summary, priority, assigned team, and current status.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, create a new row in the Message Routing Airtable base, Active Messages table with fields: message ID, source channel, sender, category, two-sentence summary, priority, assigned team, timestamp, and initial status set to 'New'. When team members update the status field to In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved via Airtable, record the status change in the audit log.

objects.role: Write service that maintains a live dashboard in the All Messages table of the Message Routing Airtable base showing every message and its current status across all teams.
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all message records written to the All Messages table, including full message metadata and live status.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, create a new row in the Message Routing Airtable base, All Messages table with fields: message ID, source channel, sender, category, two-sentence summary, priority, assigned team, timestamp, and current status. This table serves as the live cross-team dashboard view and reflects all messages regardless of team assignment.

steps: The workflow monitors multiple Gmail inboxes for new inbound email, specifically support, sales, and billing mailboxes. For each new email, it captures the subject, message body, sender, source inbox, and timestamp so the message can enter a shared intake process.

The workflow collects new submissions from website forms in Jotform, including both contact and inquiry forms. Each submission is passed into the same intake flow with the submitted fields, submitter information, form name, and timestamp.

The workflow gathers inbound social messages from the company’s Facebook Page, Twitter/X mentions, and LinkedIn inbox. For each new message or mention, it captures the content, sender, source platform, and timestamp for downstream handling.

The workflow uses GPT-4o to assign every inbound message to exactly one business category: Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing Question, or General Feedback. This categorization is applied consistently regardless of whether the message came from Gmail, Jotform, Facebook, Twitter/X, or LinkedIn.
The workflow uses GPT-4o to generate a concise two-sentence summary of each inbound message. This gives teams a quick, standardized overview of the message before they begin work.

The workflow uses GPT-4o to assign a priority of High, Medium, or Low to each message based on its urgency and content. That priority is then included with the routed record so teams can focus on the most important items first.

Based on the assigned category, the workflow routes each message to the appropriate team: Sales Inquiry to Sales, Technical Support to Support, Billing Question to Billing, and General Feedback to Customer Success. This routing rule is deterministic and depends entirely on the AI-assigned category.

The workflow sends an immediate notification in Slack to the team responsible for the routed message. Each alert includes the two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and sender information so team members can quickly understand what needs attention.

The workflow allows team members to update the status of messages in Airtable as work progresses. Messages start with a status of New, and when users change the status to In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved, the system records that status change for tracking.

The workflow maintains a live cross-team view of all messages and their current status in Airtable. This dashboard includes every message regardless of assigned team, along with its source, sender, category, summary, priority, routing destination, timestamp, and latest status.

tools.response_template: {{"status":"success","classification_id":"cls-{call_index:08d}","category":"actionable","subcategory":"customer-support","confidence":0.86,"reasoning":"Message references a specific product issue and requests a follow-up action, matching the \"actionable\" rubric definition.","prompt_tokens":180,"completion_tokens":40}}

tools.description: Send the inbound message content to GPT-4o to generate a two-sentence summary.
tools.response_template: {{"status":"success","summary_id":"sum-{call_index:08d}","summary":"Sender raised a concrete request needing follow-up and provided context to act on; the recipient should respond with next steps and an ETA.","prompt_tokens":210,"completion_tokens":55}}

tools.description: Send the inbound message content to GPT-4o to assign a priority level.
Post a formatted notification message to the specified Slack channel.
Create a new record in an Airtable table for the routed message.
Update an existing record in an Airtable table to reflect a changed message status or other current field values.

name: Intelligent Multi-Channel Message Routing

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new emails from the Support, Sales, and Billing Gmail inboxes and forwards them for processing.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-fetched timestamps per inbox to avoid duplicate ingestion.
objects.behavior: When new emails arrive in the Support, Sales, or Billing Gmail inboxes, forward each email (subject, body, sender, inbox source, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new email with full content and metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new email in Support Gmail inbox
external system: new email in Sales Gmail inbox
external system: new email in Billing Gmail inbox

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects form submissions from the Jotform contact and inquiry forms on the company website.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submission IDs to prevent duplicate processing.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives from the Jotform contact or inquiry form, forward the full submission (fields, submitter info, form name, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new form submission with full content and metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new Jotform contact form submission
external system: new Jotform inquiry form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects inbound messages from the company Facebook Page, Twitter/X mentions, and LinkedIn inbox.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-fetched message IDs per social channel to avoid duplicate ingestion.
objects.behavior: When new messages, mentions, or inbox items arrive from Facebook Page, Twitter/X, or LinkedIn, forward each message (content, sender, platform, timestamp) to the message-classifier peer.

peers.relationship: Forward each new social message with full content and platform metadata for classification and routing.

event_sources: external system: new Facebook Page message
external system: new Twitter/X mention
external system: new LinkedIn inbox message

objects.role: Business logic object that uses GPT-4o to categorize, summarize, and prioritize each inbound message, then routes it to the appropriate team notification and records it.
objects.state_description: Holds in-flight message processing state including raw content, assigned category, two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and routing destination for each message being processed.
objects.behavior: When a message arrives from any source channel: (1) Call GPT-4o to categorize the message as exactly one of: Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing Question, or General Feedback. (2) Call GPT-4o to generate a two-sentence summary of the message. (3) Call GPT-4o to assign a priority level of High, Medium, or Low based on urgency and content. (4) Based on category, determine routing: Sales Inquiry → Sales Team (#sales-inbound), Technical Support → Technical Support Team (#support-tickets), Billing Question → Billing Team (#billing-requests), General Feedback → Customer Success Team (#customer-success). (5) Simultaneously send the enriched message record (original content, source, category, summary, priority, team, timestamp, initial status 'New') to slack-notifier and airtable-active-messages and airtable-all-messages.

peers.relationship: Send enriched message record with category, summary, priority, source, and target Slack channel name for instant team notification.
Send enriched message record to be written to the Active Messages table.
Send enriched message record to be written to the All Messages dashboard table.

objects.role: Write service that posts instant notifications to the appropriate Slack channel based on the routed message category.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all Slack notifications sent, including channel, message summary, priority, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, inspect the target channel field and post a formatted notification to the designated Slack channel: Sales Inquiry messages go to #sales-inbound, Technical Support messages go to #support-tickets, Billing Question messages go to #billing-requests, and General Feedback messages go to #customer-success. Each notification includes the two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and sender information.

objects.role: Write service that records each routed message into the Active Messages table in the Message Routing Airtable base for team status tracking.
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all message records written to the Active Messages table, including message ID, source, category, summary, priority, assigned team, and current status.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, create a new row in the Message Routing Airtable base, Active Messages table with fields: message ID, source channel, sender, category, two-sentence summary, priority, assigned team, timestamp, and initial status set to 'New'. When team members update the status field to In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved via Airtable, record the status change in the audit log.

objects.role: Write service that maintains a live dashboard in the All Messages table of the Message Routing Airtable base showing every message and its current status across all teams.
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all message records written to the All Messages table, including full message metadata and live status.
objects.behavior: When an enriched message record arrives, create a new row in the Message Routing Airtable base, All Messages table with fields: message ID, source channel, sender, category, two-sentence summary, priority, assigned team, timestamp, and current status. This table serves as the live cross-team dashboard view and reflects all messages regardless of team assignment.

steps: The workflow monitors multiple Gmail inboxes for new inbound email, specifically support, sales, and billing mailboxes. For each new email, it captures the subject, message body, sender, source inbox, and timestamp so the message can enter a shared intake process.

The workflow collects new submissions from website forms in Jotform, including both contact and inquiry forms. Each submission is passed into the same intake flow with the submitted fields, submitter information, form name, and timestamp.

The workflow gathers inbound social messages from the company’s Facebook Page, Twitter/X mentions, and LinkedIn inbox. For each new message or mention, it captures the content, sender, source platform, and timestamp for downstream handling.

The workflow uses GPT-4o to assign every inbound message to exactly one business category: Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing Question, or General Feedback. This categorization is applied consistently regardless of whether the message came from Gmail, Jotform, Facebook, Twitter/X, or LinkedIn.
The workflow uses GPT-4o to generate a concise two-sentence summary of each inbound message. This gives teams a quick, standardized overview of the message before they begin work.

The workflow uses GPT-4o to assign a priority of High, Medium, or Low to each message based on its urgency and content. That priority is then included with the routed record so teams can focus on the most important items first.

Based on the assigned category, the workflow routes each message to the appropriate team: Sales Inquiry to Sales, Technical Support to Support, Billing Question to Billing, and General Feedback to Customer Success. This routing rule is deterministic and depends entirely on the AI-assigned category.

The workflow sends an immediate notification in Slack to the team responsible for the routed message. Each alert includes the two-sentence summary, priority level, source channel, and sender information so team members can quickly understand what needs attention.

The workflow allows team members to update the status of messages in Airtable as work progresses. Messages start with a status of New, and when users change the status to In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved, the system records that status change for tracking.

The workflow maintains a live cross-team view of all messages and their current status in Airtable. This dashboard includes every message regardless of assigned team, along with its source, sender, category, summary, priority, routing destination, timestamp, and latest status.

modifications.intent: Stop posting Slack notifications for messages that are classified as General Feedback when their priority is Low.

name: Automated Offline Conversion Tracking

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of received Closed Won opportunity conversion event payloads and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event arrives, record the payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid event is received, preserve the event payload exactly for downstream processing.

peers.relationship: Forward each received Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event for conversion extraction, deduplication, routing, retry handling, and sync tracking

event_sources: Salesforce: opportunity Closed Won conversion event

objects.role: Business logic object that knows the rules for extracting, deduplicating, routing, retrying, and confirming offline conversion syncs across ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Tracks conversion records by source opportunity, normalized conversion payloads, deduplication keys, per-platform delivery status, retry eligibility, retry schedules, attempt counts, last error by platform, and overall sync confirmation state.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won conversion event arrives, extract the conversion data needed for downstream ad platform uploads. When a conversion record is extracted, compute a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppress any record whose deduplication key has already been processed successfully or is already in progress. When a conversion record is new, create per-platform delivery intents for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When dispatching a new or retryable conversion, send to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When a platform reports a successful upload, mark that platform as synced for the conversion. When a platform reports a failed upload, record the error, increment the attempt count for that platform, and schedule a retry for 15 minutes later if retry policy allows. When a scheduled retry time arrives for a platform that is not yet synced, resend the same normalized conversion payload to that platform. When all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, mark the conversion as fully confirmed. When retry policy is exhausted for any platform, keep the conversion in a failed state with its latest error and partial sync status.

peers.relationship: Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Facebook offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Google offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable TikTok offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable LinkedIn offline conversion uploads

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Facebook Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Facebook Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Google Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Google Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Google Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to TikTok Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for TikTok Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to LinkedIn Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for LinkedIn Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Entry-point service object for inbound success and failure events from ad platform conversion upload APIs.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of platform upload result events, including platform name, conversion identity, status, error details, and receipt time.
objects.behavior: When an upload result event arrives from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads, record the result payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid upload result is received, preserve the reported status, conversion identity, platform identity, and any error details for downstream status handling.

peers.relationship: Forward each platform upload success or failure event for sync state updates and retry decisions

event_sources: Facebook Ads: conversion upload result event
Google Ads: conversion upload result event
TikTok Ads: conversion upload result event
LinkedIn Ads: conversion upload result event

objects.role: Entry-point service object for delayed retry wake-up events after the 15-minute retry interval.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of retry-due events received for scheduled conversion platform retries.
objects.behavior: When a retry-due event arrives, record the retry trigger and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid retry trigger is received, preserve the conversion identity and platform context for downstream retry evaluation.

peers.relationship: Forward each retry-due event so retry eligibility can be checked and the upload resent if still pending

event_sources: Scheduler: conversion retry due event after 15 minutes

steps: The workflow watches Salesforce for new Closed Won opportunity conversion events. When one arrives, it records the event and receipt time for audit purposes and preserves the original event payload for downstream processing if the event is valid.

For each valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event, the workflow extracts the conversion data needed for offline conversion uploads to advertising platforms. This extracted conversion data becomes the normalized record used for all downstream delivery steps.

The workflow computes a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppresses any conversion that has already been processed successfully or is already being processed. Only conversions that are new continue through the sync workflow.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Facebook Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Google Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Google Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to TikTok Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to LinkedIn Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

When Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads reports a failed upload, the workflow records the error, increments that platform’s attempt count, and schedules a retry 15 minutes later if retries are still allowed. When the retry time arrives, the workflow resends the same normalized conversion payload only for platforms that are not yet marked as synced; if retry policy is exhausted, the conversion remains failed with its latest error and any partial sync status.

As upload results arrive from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads, the workflow marks each platform as synced when its upload succeeds. Once all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, the workflow marks that conversion as fully confirmed.

modifications.intent: Starting this weekend, if a conversion platform retry comes due on Saturday or Sunday, hold it until the next business morning instead of retrying during the weekend.

events.input: Salesforce emits an opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Acme Industrial Expansion - Q2 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', account name 'Acme Industrial', contact 'James Brown', contact email 'james.brown@acmeindustrial.com', conversion timestamp '2026-05-21T14:32:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 125000.00, order ID 'ORD-99173', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-2048', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-demo-12345', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1716301920.demo67890', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.demo.445566', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.demo.778899', and stage 'Closed Won'.

expect.action: The Salesforce Closed Won event is recorded with its receipt time in the audit trail, the original Salesforce payload is preserved, a new normalized conversion record is created for opportunity OPP-78421 / order ORD-99173, and outbound offline conversion upload attempts using that same normalized payload are recorded and sent to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.
expect.reason: A new valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event is ingested, normalized, deduplicated as new, and dispatched to all four advertising platforms with audit tracking.

events.input: Facebook Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Facebook Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'fb-upload-30017', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:10Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The inbound Facebook Ads result for order ORD-99173 / opportunity OPP-78421 is recorded in the upload-results audit trail, and Facebook Ads is marked synced for that conversion.
expect.reason: A successful Facebook Ads upload result causes the system to audit the result and mark that platform complete for the conversion.

events.input: Google Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Google Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'goog-upload-44128', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:18Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The inbound Google Ads result for order ORD-99173 / opportunity OPP-78421 is recorded in the upload-results audit trail, and Google Ads is marked synced for that conversion.
expect.reason: A successful Google Ads upload result causes the system to audit the result and mark that platform complete for the conversion.

events.input: TikTok Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', upload status 'failed', error code 'RATE_LIMIT', error message 'Too many requests', platform event ID 'tt-upload-55209', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:26Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The inbound TikTok Ads failure result for order ORD-99173 / opportunity OPP-78421 is recorded in the upload-results audit trail with error code RATE_LIMIT and message 'Too many requests', TikTok Ads attempt count is incremented, and a retry for TikTok Ads is scheduled 15 minutes later.
expect.reason: A failed TikTok Ads upload with a retryable rate-limit error remains unsynced and therefore triggers retry scheduling under the baseline retry policy.

events.input: LinkedIn Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'LinkedIn Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'li-upload-66344', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:31Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The inbound LinkedIn Ads result for order ORD-99173 / opportunity OPP-78421 is recorded in the upload-results audit trail, and LinkedIn Ads is marked synced for that conversion.
expect.reason: A successful LinkedIn Ads upload result causes the system to audit the result and mark that platform complete for the conversion.

events.input: Scheduler emits a conversion retry due event after 15 minutes for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', retry attempt 1, retry due timestamp '2026-05-21T14:48:26Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: A new outbound offline conversion upload attempt for order ORD-99173 / opportunity OPP-78421 is recorded and sent to TikTok Ads using the same normalized conversion payload, because TikTok Ads is the only platform for that conversion not yet marked synced.
expect.reason: When the scheduled retry becomes due, the system resends the normalized payload only to the still-unsynced retryable platform and records that outbound attempt.

events.input: TikTok Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'tt-upload-55210', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:48:39Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The inbound TikTok Ads result for order ORD-99173 / opportunity OPP-78421 is recorded in the upload-results audit trail, TikTok Ads is marked synced for that conversion, and the conversion is marked fully confirmed across Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.
expect.reason: The final outstanding platform succeeds, so the system both records the success and marks the conversion fully confirmed once all four platforms are synced.

events.input: Scheduler emits a conversion retry due event for order ID 'ORD-88214', opportunity ID 'OPP-88214', platform 'TikTok Ads', retry attempt 1, retry due timestamp '2026-05-21T14:15:00Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-88214|ORD-88214|48250.00|2026-05-21T14:00:00Z'.

expect.action: A new outbound offline conversion upload attempt for order ORD-88214 / opportunity OPP-88214 is recorded and sent to TikTok Ads using the same normalized conversion payload for that conversion.
expect.reason: Under baseline behavior, a retry-due event immediately triggers a resend for the referenced platform when it is still unsynced and retryable.

events.input: Scheduler emits a conversion retry due event for order ID 'ORD-88277', opportunity ID 'OPP-88277', platform 'Facebook Ads', retry attempt 1, retry due timestamp '2026-05-25T09:00:00Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-88277|ORD-88277|91500.00|2026-05-25T08:45:00Z'.

expect.action: [deferred by M001] The Facebook Ads retry for order ORD-88277 / opportunity OPP-88277 is held and no outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt is produced on Sunday 2026-05-25; the retry is deferred until the next business morning.
expect.reason: M001 applies because this is a weekend retry-due event, so the normal immediate resend is delayed until the next business morning instead of firing on Sunday.

events.input: Salesforce emits an opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Northwind Renewal - EMEA', opportunity ID 'OPP-88301', account name 'Northwind Traders', contact 'Elena Petrova', contact email 'elena.petrova@northwindtraders.com', conversion timestamp '2026-05-25T09:05:00Z', currency 'EUR', conversion value 38750.00, order ID 'ORD-88301', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-3112', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-demo-88301', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1716714300.demo88301', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.demo.88301', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.demo.88301', and stage 'Closed Won'.

expect.reason: The weekend-hold modification applies only to retry-due platform retries, not to new Salesforce Closed Won conversion events, so normal ingestion and multi-platform dispatch occur. [M001 condition not met: this is not a platform retry event]

events.input: Scheduler emits a conversion retry due event for order ID 'ORD-88277', opportunity ID 'OPP-88277', platform 'Facebook Ads', retry attempt 1, retry due timestamp '2026-05-26T09:00:00Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-88277|ORD-88277|91500.00|2026-05-25T08:45:00Z'.

expect.action: [fired-on-schedule by M001] The deferred Facebook Ads retry for order ORD-88277 / opportunity OPP-88277 is now produced at 2026-05-26T09:00:00Z: a new outbound offline conversion upload attempt is recorded and sent to Facebook Ads using the same normalized conversion payload.
expect.reason: M001 held the weekend retry until the next business morning; once Monday morning arrives, the deferred resend fires normally.

events.input: Scheduler emits a conversion retry due event for order ID 'ORD-88418', opportunity ID 'OPP-88418', platform 'LinkedIn Ads', retry attempt 2, retry due timestamp '2026-05-26T11:20:00Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-88418|ORD-88418|126000.00|2026-05-26T11:05:00Z'.

expect.action: A new outbound offline conversion upload attempt for order ORD-88418 / opportunity OPP-88418 is recorded and sent to LinkedIn Ads using the same normalized conversion payload for that conversion.
expect.reason: Although M001 is active, this retry-due event occurs on Monday rather than Saturday or Sunday, so the retry is sent immediately with no weekend hold. [M001 condition not met: retry is not due on Saturday or Sunday]

tools.description: Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to Facebook Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to Google Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to TikTok Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to LinkedIn Ads in the audit trail.
Record an inbound ad platform conversion upload success or failure event, including status and error details, in the audit trail.
Record a retry-due wake-up event and receipt time in the audit trail.

name: Automated Offline Conversion Tracking

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of received Closed Won opportunity conversion event payloads and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event arrives, record the payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid event is received, preserve the event payload exactly for downstream processing.

peers.relationship: Forward each received Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event for conversion extraction, deduplication, routing, retry handling, and sync tracking

event_sources: Salesforce: opportunity Closed Won conversion event

objects.role: Business logic object that knows the rules for extracting, deduplicating, routing, retrying, and confirming offline conversion syncs across ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Tracks conversion records by source opportunity, normalized conversion payloads, deduplication keys, per-platform delivery status, retry eligibility, retry schedules, attempt counts, last error by platform, and overall sync confirmation state.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won conversion event arrives, extract the conversion data needed for downstream ad platform uploads. When a conversion record is extracted, compute a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppress any record whose deduplication key has already been processed successfully or is already in progress. When a conversion record is new, create per-platform delivery intents for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When dispatching a new or retryable conversion, send to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When a platform reports a successful upload, mark that platform as synced for the conversion. When a platform reports a failed upload, record the error, increment the attempt count for that platform, and schedule a retry for 15 minutes later if retry policy allows. When a scheduled retry time arrives for a platform that is not yet synced, resend the same normalized conversion payload to that platform. When all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, mark the conversion as fully confirmed. When retry policy is exhausted for any platform, keep the conversion in a failed state with its latest error and partial sync status.

peers.relationship: Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Facebook offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Google offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable TikTok offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable LinkedIn offline conversion uploads

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Facebook Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Facebook Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Google Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Google Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Google Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to TikTok Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for TikTok Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to LinkedIn Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for LinkedIn Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Entry-point service object for inbound success and failure events from ad platform conversion upload APIs.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of platform upload result events, including platform name, conversion identity, status, error details, and receipt time.
objects.behavior: When an upload result event arrives from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads, record the result payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid upload result is received, preserve the reported status, conversion identity, platform identity, and any error details for downstream status handling.

peers.relationship: Forward each platform upload success or failure event for sync state updates and retry decisions

event_sources: Facebook Ads: conversion upload result event
Google Ads: conversion upload result event
TikTok Ads: conversion upload result event
LinkedIn Ads: conversion upload result event

objects.role: Entry-point service object for delayed retry wake-up events after the 15-minute retry interval.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of retry-due events received for scheduled conversion platform retries.
objects.behavior: When a retry-due event arrives, record the retry trigger and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid retry trigger is received, preserve the conversion identity and platform context for downstream retry evaluation.

peers.relationship: Forward each retry-due event so retry eligibility can be checked and the upload resent if still pending

event_sources: Scheduler: conversion retry due event after 15 minutes

steps: The workflow watches Salesforce for new Closed Won opportunity conversion events. When one arrives, it records the event and receipt time for audit purposes and preserves the original event payload for downstream processing if the event is valid.

For each valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event, the workflow extracts the conversion data needed for offline conversion uploads to advertising platforms. This extracted conversion data becomes the normalized record used for all downstream delivery steps.

The workflow computes a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppresses any conversion that has already been processed successfully or is already being processed. Only conversions that are new continue through the sync workflow.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Facebook Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Google Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Google Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to TikTok Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to LinkedIn Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

When Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads reports a failed upload, the workflow records the error, increments that platform’s attempt count, and schedules a retry 15 minutes later if retries are still allowed. When the retry time arrives, the workflow resends the same normalized conversion payload only for platforms that are not yet marked as synced; if retry policy is exhausted, the conversion remains failed with its latest error and any partial sync status.

As upload results arrive from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads, the workflow marks each platform as synced when its upload succeeds. Once all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, the workflow marks that conversion as fully confirmed.

modifications.intent: For any given conversion and ad platform, if that platform has already come back successful, ignore any later upload-result events that repeat the same conversion-platform outcome instead of reopening or reprocessing it.

events.input: Salesforce emits an opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Acme Industrial Expansion - Q2 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', account name 'Acme Industrial', contact 'James Brown', contact email 'james.brown@acmeindustrial.com', conversion timestamp '2026-05-21T14:32:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 125000.00, order ID 'ORD-99173', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-2048', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-demo-12345', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1716301920.demo67890', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.demo.445566', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.demo.778899', and stage 'Closed Won'.

expect.action: A new Salesforce Closed Won conversion event is recorded in the audit trail with its receipt time, and the original Salesforce payload is preserved. A normalized conversion record for opportunity OPP-78421 / order ORD-99173 is created for downstream delivery. Because this conversion is new, one offline conversion upload attempt using that normalized payload is sent to Facebook Ads, one to Google Ads, one to TikTok Ads, and one to LinkedIn Ads, and each outbound upload attempt is recorded in the audit trail.
expect.reason: A valid new Salesforce Closed Won event is ingested, normalized, deduplicated as not previously processed, and dispatched to all four ad platforms.

events.input: Facebook Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Facebook Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'fb-upload-30017', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:10Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The Facebook Ads upload result for opportunity OPP-78421 / order ORD-99173 is recorded in the audit trail as a success, and Facebook Ads is marked synced for that conversion. The conversion is not yet marked fully confirmed.
expect.reason: A platform success result updates that platform's sync status, but full confirmation requires success from all four ad platforms.
