tools.description: Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to Facebook Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to Google Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to TikTok Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to LinkedIn Ads in the audit trail.
Record an inbound ad platform conversion upload success or failure event, including status and error details, in the audit trail.
Record a retry-due wake-up event and receipt time in the audit trail.

name: Automated Offline Conversion Tracking

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of received Closed Won opportunity conversion event payloads and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event arrives, record the payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid event is received, preserve the event payload exactly for downstream processing.

peers.relationship: Forward each received Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event for conversion extraction, deduplication, routing, retry handling, and sync tracking

event_sources: Salesforce: opportunity Closed Won conversion event

objects.role: Business logic object that knows the rules for extracting, deduplicating, routing, retrying, and confirming offline conversion syncs across ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Tracks conversion records by source opportunity, normalized conversion payloads, deduplication keys, per-platform delivery status, retry eligibility, retry schedules, attempt counts, last error by platform, and overall sync confirmation state.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won conversion event arrives, extract the conversion data needed for downstream ad platform uploads. When a conversion record is extracted, compute a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppress any record whose deduplication key has already been processed successfully or is already in progress. When a conversion record is new, create per-platform delivery intents for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When dispatching a new or retryable conversion, send to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When a platform reports a successful upload, mark that platform as synced for the conversion. When a platform reports a failed upload, record the error, increment the attempt count for that platform, and schedule a retry for 15 minutes later if retry policy allows. When a scheduled retry time arrives for a platform that is not yet synced, resend the same normalized conversion payload to that platform. When all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, mark the conversion as fully confirmed. When retry policy is exhausted for any platform, keep the conversion in a failed state with its latest error and partial sync status.

peers.relationship: Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Facebook offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Google offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable TikTok offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable LinkedIn offline conversion uploads

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Facebook Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Facebook Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Google Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Google Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Google Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to TikTok Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for TikTok Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to LinkedIn Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for LinkedIn Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Entry-point service object for inbound success and failure events from ad platform conversion upload APIs.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of platform upload result events, including platform name, conversion identity, status, error details, and receipt time.
objects.behavior: When an upload result event arrives from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads, record the result payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid upload result is received, preserve the reported status, conversion identity, platform identity, and any error details for downstream status handling.

peers.relationship: Forward each platform upload success or failure event for sync state updates and retry decisions

event_sources: Facebook Ads: conversion upload result event
Google Ads: conversion upload result event
TikTok Ads: conversion upload result event
LinkedIn Ads: conversion upload result event

objects.role: Entry-point service object for delayed retry wake-up events after the 15-minute retry interval.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of retry-due events received for scheduled conversion platform retries.
objects.behavior: When a retry-due event arrives, record the retry trigger and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid retry trigger is received, preserve the conversion identity and platform context for downstream retry evaluation.

peers.relationship: Forward each retry-due event so retry eligibility can be checked and the upload resent if still pending

event_sources: Scheduler: conversion retry due event after 15 minutes

steps: The workflow watches Salesforce for new Closed Won opportunity conversion events. When one arrives, it records the event and receipt time for audit purposes and preserves the original event payload for downstream processing if the event is valid.

For each valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event, the workflow extracts the conversion data needed for offline conversion uploads to advertising platforms. This extracted conversion data becomes the normalized record used for all downstream delivery steps.

The workflow computes a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppresses any conversion that has already been processed successfully or is already being processed. Only conversions that are new continue through the sync workflow.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Facebook Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Google Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Google Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to TikTok Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to LinkedIn Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

When Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads reports a failed upload, the workflow records the error, increments that platform’s attempt count, and schedules a retry 15 minutes later if retries are still allowed. When the retry time arrives, the workflow resends the same normalized conversion payload only for platforms that are not yet marked as synced; if retry policy is exhausted, the conversion remains failed with its latest error and any partial sync status.

As upload results arrive from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads, the workflow marks each platform as synced when its upload succeeds. Once all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, the workflow marks that conversion as fully confirmed.

modifications.intent: Change deduplication handling so a Salesforce Closed Won conversion event is suppressed only when the same deduplication key has already reached fully confirmed status across Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. If the same deduplication key is still in progress, partially synced, retrying, or failed on any platform, do not suppress the repeated event and create a fresh dispatch attempt to all four platforms using the same normalized conversion payload.

events.input: Salesforce emits an opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Acme Industrial Expansion - Q2 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', account name 'Acme Industrial', contact 'James Brown', contact email 'james.brown@acmeindustrial.com', conversion timestamp '2026-05-21T14:32:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 125000.00, order ID 'ORD-99173', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-2048', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-demo-12345', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1716301920.demo67890', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.demo.445566', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.demo.778899', and stage 'Closed Won'.

expect.action: The Salesforce Closed Won event is recorded with its receipt time in the audit trail, the original Salesforce payload is preserved, a normalized offline conversion record is created for opportunity OPP-78421 / order ORD-99173, and one offline conversion upload attempt using that normalized payload is sent and audit-recorded for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.
expect.reason: A new valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event is ingested, normalized, not suppressed as a duplicate, and dispatched to all four advertising platforms under the baseline sync workflow.

events.input: Facebook Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Facebook Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'fb-upload-30017', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:10Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The Facebook Ads upload result for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail, and the conversion’s Facebook Ads sync status is marked successful.
expect.reason: A successful Facebook Ads upload result causes the system to audit the result and mark that platform as synced for the conversion, but not fully confirm the conversion until all four platforms succeed.

events.input: Google Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Google Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'goog-upload-44128', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:18Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The Google Ads upload result for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail, and the conversion’s Google Ads sync status is marked successful.
expect.reason: A successful Google Ads upload result causes the system to audit the result and mark that platform as synced for the conversion, but not fully confirm the conversion until all four platforms succeed.

events.input: TikTok Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', upload status 'failed', error code 'RATE_LIMIT', error message 'Too many requests', platform event ID 'tt-upload-55209', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:26Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The TikTok Ads failed upload result for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail with error code RATE_LIMIT and message 'Too many requests', TikTok Ads attempt count is incremented, and a retry for TikTok Ads is scheduled 15 minutes later while the other platform sync statuses remain unchanged.
expect.reason: A retryable TikTok Ads failure is audited and scheduled for retry because the platform is not yet synced and the retry policy still allows another attempt.

events.input: LinkedIn Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'LinkedIn Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'li-upload-66344', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:31Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The LinkedIn Ads upload result for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail, and the conversion’s LinkedIn Ads sync status is marked successful.
expect.reason: A successful LinkedIn Ads upload result causes the system to audit the result and mark that platform as synced for the conversion, but not fully confirm the conversion until TikTok Ads also succeeds.

events.input: Scheduler emits a conversion retry due event after 15 minutes for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', retry attempt 1, retry due timestamp '2026-05-21T14:48:26Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: When the scheduled retry becomes due for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z, the system resends the same normalized conversion payload to TikTok Ads only, and that outbound TikTok Ads retry attempt is recorded in the audit trail.
expect.reason: A retry-due event triggers resend only for the unsynced failed platform, not for platforms already marked synced.

events.input: TikTok Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'tt-upload-55210', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:48:39Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The TikTok Ads upload result for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail, the conversion’s TikTok Ads sync status is marked successful, and the conversion is marked fully confirmed because Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads are all now synced.
expect.reason: The final outstanding platform succeeded, so the system both records the success and marks the conversion fully confirmed.

events.input: Salesforce emits an opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Northwind Logistics Renewal - April 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-81011', account name 'Northwind Logistics', contact 'Erin Walsh', contact email 'erin.walsh@northwindlogistics.com', conversion timestamp '2026-04-07T09:14:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 48250.00, order ID 'ORD-12011', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-3101', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-northwind-12011', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1712558040.northwind12011', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.northwind.12011', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.northwind.12011', and stage 'Closed Won'.

expect.action: The Salesforce Closed Won event is recorded with its receipt time in the audit trail, the original Salesforce payload is preserved, a normalized offline conversion record is created for opportunity OPP-81011 / order ORD-12011, and one offline conversion upload attempt using that normalized payload is sent and audit-recorded for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.
expect.reason: A new valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event is ingested, normalized, not suppressed as a duplicate, and dispatched to all four advertising platforms under the baseline sync workflow.

events.input: Salesforce emits a repeated opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'BluePeak Manufacturing Expansion - April 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-81077', account name 'BluePeak Manufacturing', contact 'Lena Ortiz', contact email 'lena.ortiz@bluepeakmfg.com', conversion timestamp '2026-04-07T11:05:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 96300.00, order ID 'ORD-12077', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-3114', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-bluepeak-12077', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1712564700.bluepeak12077', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.bluepeak.12077', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.bluepeak.12077', and stage 'Closed Won'. This repeated event is for the same deduplication key as an earlier BluePeak Closed Won event that is not fully confirmed because TikTok Ads is still pending retry after a failed upload.

expect.action: The repeated Salesforce Closed Won event is recorded with its receipt time in the audit trail, the original Salesforce payload is preserved, and [adjusted by M001] because the same deduplication key is not yet fully confirmed, the event is not suppressed: a fresh normalized conversion dispatch is produced for opportunity OPP-81077 / order ORD-12077 and one new offline conversion upload attempt using the same normalized payload is sent and audit-recorded for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.
expect.reason: M001 changes deduplication so repeated Salesforce Closed Won events are suppressed only after full confirmation across all four ad platforms; a still-pending TikTok retry means this repeated event must be dispatched again.

events.input: LinkedIn Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-44519', opportunity ID 'OPP-84519', platform 'LinkedIn Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'li-upload-84519', received timestamp '2026-04-07T13:22:00Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-84519|ORD-44519|71500.00|2026-04-07T12:58:00Z'.

expect.action: The LinkedIn Ads upload result for deduplication key OPP-84519|ORD-44519|71500.00|2026-04-07T12:58:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail, and the conversion’s LinkedIn Ads sync status is marked successful.
expect.reason: [M001 produces no observable difference on this input] A successful LinkedIn Ads upload result still only updates audit history and LinkedIn sync status for that conversion.

events.input: Salesforce emits a repeated opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Harborview Medical Equipment Upgrade - April 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-81103', account name 'Harborview Medical', contact 'Nina Patel', contact email 'nina.patel@harborviewmedical.com', conversion timestamp '2026-04-07T14:10:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 154200.00, order ID 'ORD-12103', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-3120', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-harborview-12103', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1712571000.harborview12103', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.harborview.12103', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.harborview.12103', and stage 'Closed Won'. This repeated event is for the same deduplication key as an earlier Harborview Closed Won event that has already been fully confirmed with successful uploads recorded for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.

expect.action: The repeated Salesforce Closed Won event is recorded with its receipt time in the audit trail, the original Salesforce payload is preserved, and [suppressed by M001] no new conversion dispatch or ad-platform upload attempts are produced for opportunity OPP-81103 / order ORD-12103 because the same deduplication key has already been fully confirmed across Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.
expect.reason: M001 suppresses repeated Salesforce Closed Won events only when the matching deduplication key is already fully confirmed on all four platforms, which is true for this Harborview conversion.

events.input: Salesforce emits a repeated opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Summit Energy Services Rollout - April 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-81145', account name 'Summit Energy Services', contact 'Owen Price', contact email 'owen.price@summitenergy.com', conversion timestamp '2026-04-07T15:05:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 88750.00, order ID 'ORD-12145', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-3132', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-summit-12145', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1712574300.summit12145', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.summit.12145', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.summit.12145', and stage 'Closed Won'. This repeated event is for the same deduplication key as an earlier Summit Closed Won event that remains partially synced because Facebook Ads and Google Ads succeeded, LinkedIn Ads succeeded, and TikTok Ads is still in failed state after retry policy was exhausted.

expect.action: The repeated Salesforce Closed Won event is recorded with its receipt time in the audit trail, the original Salesforce payload is preserved, and [adjusted by M001] because the same deduplication key is not fully confirmed, the event is not suppressed: a fresh normalized conversion dispatch is produced for opportunity OPP-81145 / order ORD-12145 and one new offline conversion upload attempt using the same normalized payload is sent and audit-recorded for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.
expect.reason: M001 requires re-dispatch of repeated events when the prior conversion is only partially synced or failed on any platform; this Summit conversion is not fully confirmed because TikTok Ads remains failed after retries were exhausted.

name: Automated Offline Conversion Tracking

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of received Closed Won opportunity conversion event payloads and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event arrives, record the payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid event is received, preserve the event payload exactly for downstream processing.

peers.relationship: Forward each received Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event for conversion extraction, deduplication, routing, retry handling, and sync tracking

event_sources: Salesforce: opportunity Closed Won conversion event

objects.role: Business logic object that knows the rules for extracting, deduplicating, routing, retrying, and confirming offline conversion syncs across ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Tracks conversion records by source opportunity, normalized conversion payloads, deduplication keys, per-platform delivery status, retry eligibility, retry schedules, attempt counts, last error by platform, and overall sync confirmation state.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won conversion event arrives, extract the conversion data needed for downstream ad platform uploads. When a conversion record is extracted, compute a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppress any record whose deduplication key has already been processed successfully or is already in progress. When a conversion record is new, create per-platform delivery intents for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When dispatching a new or retryable conversion, send to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When a platform reports a successful upload, mark that platform as synced for the conversion. When a platform reports a failed upload, record the error, increment the attempt count for that platform, and schedule a retry for 15 minutes later if retry policy allows. When a scheduled retry time arrives for a platform that is not yet synced, resend the same normalized conversion payload to that platform. When all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, mark the conversion as fully confirmed. When retry policy is exhausted for any platform, keep the conversion in a failed state with its latest error and partial sync status.

event_sources: Scheduler: conversion retry due event after 15 minutes

steps: The workflow watches Salesforce for new Closed Won opportunity conversion events. When one arrives, it records the event and receipt time for audit purposes and preserves the original event payload for downstream processing if the event is valid.

For each valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event, the workflow extracts the conversion data needed for offline conversion uploads to advertising platforms. This extracted conversion data becomes the normalized record used for all downstream delivery steps.

The workflow computes a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppresses any conversion that has already been processed successfully or is already being processed. Only conversions that are new continue through the sync workflow.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Facebook Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Google Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Google Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to TikTok Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to LinkedIn Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

When Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads reports a failed upload, the workflow records the error, increments that platform’s attempt count, and schedules a retry 15 minutes later if retries are still allowed. When the retry time arrives, the workflow resends the same normalized conversion payload only for platforms that are not yet marked as synced; if retry policy is exhausted, the conversion remains failed with its latest error and any partial sync status.

As upload results arrive from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads, the workflow marks each platform as synced when its upload succeeds. Once all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, the workflow marks that conversion as fully confirmed.

modifications.intent: We need to stop treating one of these as done just because part of it got through — only consider the dedup key settled once the conversion is fully confirmed across Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.

events.input: Salesforce emits an opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Acme Industrial Expansion - Q2 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', account name 'Acme Industrial', contact 'James Brown', contact email 'james.brown@acmeindustrial.com', conversion timestamp '2026-05-21T14:32:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 125000.00, order ID 'ORD-99173', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-2048', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-demo-12345', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1716301920.demo67890', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.demo.445566', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.demo.778899', and stage 'Closed Won'.

expect.action: The Salesforce Closed Won event is recorded with its receipt time in the audit trail, the original Salesforce payload is preserved, a new normalized offline-conversion record for opportunity OPP-78421 / order ORD-99173 is created, and the same conversion is sent for offline upload to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads with an outbound audit record for each platform upload attempt.
expect.reason: A new valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event is ingested, normalized, not suppressed as a duplicate, and dispatched to all four advertising platforms.

tools.description: Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to Facebook Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to Google Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to TikTok Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to LinkedIn Ads in the audit trail.
Record an inbound ad platform conversion upload success or failure event, including status and error details, in the audit trail.
Record a retry-due wake-up event and receipt time in the audit trail.

name: Automated Offline Conversion Tracking

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of received Closed Won opportunity conversion event payloads and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event arrives, record the payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid event is received, preserve the event payload exactly for downstream processing.

peers.relationship: Forward each received Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event for conversion extraction, deduplication, routing, retry handling, and sync tracking

event_sources: Salesforce: opportunity Closed Won conversion event

objects.role: Business logic object that knows the rules for extracting, deduplicating, routing, retrying, and confirming offline conversion syncs across ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Tracks conversion records by source opportunity, normalized conversion payloads, deduplication keys, per-platform delivery status, retry eligibility, retry schedules, attempt counts, last error by platform, and overall sync confirmation state.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won conversion event arrives, extract the conversion data needed for downstream ad platform uploads. When a conversion record is extracted, compute a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppress any record whose deduplication key has already been processed successfully or is already in progress. When a conversion record is new, create per-platform delivery intents for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When dispatching a new or retryable conversion, send to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When a platform reports a successful upload, mark that platform as synced for the conversion. When a platform reports a failed upload, record the error, increment the attempt count for that platform, and schedule a retry for 15 minutes later if retry policy allows. When a scheduled retry time arrives for a platform that is not yet synced, resend the same normalized conversion payload to that platform. When all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, mark the conversion as fully confirmed. When retry policy is exhausted for any platform, keep the conversion in a failed state with its latest error and partial sync status.

peers.relationship: Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Facebook offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Google offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable TikTok offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable LinkedIn offline conversion uploads

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Facebook Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Facebook Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Google Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Google Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Google Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to TikTok Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for TikTok Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to LinkedIn Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for LinkedIn Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Entry-point service object for inbound success and failure events from ad platform conversion upload APIs.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of platform upload result events, including platform name, conversion identity, status, error details, and receipt time.
objects.behavior: When an upload result event arrives from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads, record the result payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid upload result is received, preserve the reported status, conversion identity, platform identity, and any error details for downstream status handling.

peers.relationship: Forward each platform upload success or failure event for sync state updates and retry decisions

event_sources: Facebook Ads: conversion upload result event
Google Ads: conversion upload result event
TikTok Ads: conversion upload result event
LinkedIn Ads: conversion upload result event

objects.role: Entry-point service object for delayed retry wake-up events after the 15-minute retry interval.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of retry-due events received for scheduled conversion platform retries.
objects.behavior: When a retry-due event arrives, record the retry trigger and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid retry trigger is received, preserve the conversion identity and platform context for downstream retry evaluation.

peers.relationship: Forward each retry-due event so retry eligibility can be checked and the upload resent if still pending

event_sources: Scheduler: conversion retry due event after 15 minutes

steps: The workflow watches Salesforce for new Closed Won opportunity conversion events. When one arrives, it records the event and receipt time for audit purposes and preserves the original event payload for downstream processing if the event is valid.

For each valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event, the workflow extracts the conversion data needed for offline conversion uploads to advertising platforms. This extracted conversion data becomes the normalized record used for all downstream delivery steps.

The workflow computes a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppresses any conversion that has already been processed successfully or is already being processed. Only conversions that are new continue through the sync workflow.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Facebook Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Google Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Google Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to TikTok Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to LinkedIn Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

When Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads reports a failed upload, the workflow records the error, increments that platform’s attempt count, and schedules a retry 15 minutes later if retries are still allowed. When the retry time arrives, the workflow resends the same normalized conversion payload only for platforms that are not yet marked as synced; if retry policy is exhausted, the conversion remains failed with its latest error and any partial sync status.

As upload results arrive from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads, the workflow marks each platform as synced when its upload succeeds. Once all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, the workflow marks that conversion as fully confirmed.

modifications.intent: Let's stop bothering with LinkedIn on these offline conversion pushes for now.

events.input: Salesforce emits an opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Acme Industrial Expansion - Q2 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', account name 'Acme Industrial', contact 'James Brown', contact email 'james.brown@acmeindustrial.com', conversion timestamp '2026-05-21T14:32:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 125000.00, order ID 'ORD-99173', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-2048', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-demo-12345', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1716301920.demo67890', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.demo.445566', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.demo.778899', and stage 'Closed Won'.

expect.action: A new Salesforce Closed Won conversion event is recorded in the external audit trail with its receipt time, and the original Salesforce payload is preserved for audit. A new conversion processing record is created in the external conversion state store for this opportunity/order as a normalized offline conversion. Outbound offline conversion upload attempts are recorded in the audit trail for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads, and the normalized conversion is sent to all four advertising platforms.
expect.reason: A valid new Salesforce Closed Won conversion is normalized, not suppressed as a duplicate, and delivered to every configured ad platform under the baseline workflow.

events.input: Facebook Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Facebook Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'fb-upload-30017', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:10Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The conversion’s Facebook Ads status is updated to synced in the external conversion state store, and the inbound Facebook Ads success result is recorded in the external upload results audit log.
expect.reason: A Facebook Ads success result marks that platform as completed for the conversion, but full confirmation requires all configured platforms to be synced.

events.input: Google Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Google Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'goog-upload-44128', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:18Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The conversion’s Google Ads status is updated to synced in the external conversion state store, and the inbound Google Ads success result is recorded in the external upload results audit log.
expect.reason: A Google Ads success result marks that platform as completed for the conversion, but full confirmation requires all configured platforms to be synced.

events.input: TikTok Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', upload status 'failed', error code 'RATE_LIMIT', error message 'Too many requests', platform event ID 'tt-upload-55209', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:26Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The TikTok Ads failure result is recorded in the external upload results audit log with the reported error. The conversion’s TikTok Ads attempt count and latest error are updated in the external conversion state store, and a retry is scheduled for 15 minutes later.
expect.reason: A retryable TikTok Ads upload failure for a platform that is not yet synced must be recorded and retried under the retry policy.

events.input: LinkedIn Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'LinkedIn Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'li-upload-66344', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:31Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The conversion’s LinkedIn Ads status is updated to synced in the external conversion state store, and the inbound LinkedIn Ads success result is recorded in the external upload results audit log.
expect.reason: A LinkedIn Ads success result marks that platform as completed for the conversion, but full confirmation requires all configured platforms to be synced.

events.input: Scheduler emits a conversion retry due event after 15 minutes for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', retry attempt 1, retry due timestamp '2026-05-21T14:48:26Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: A new outbound TikTok Ads offline conversion upload attempt is recorded in the audit trail, and the same normalized conversion payload is resent to TikTok Ads.
expect.reason: When the scheduled retry time arrives for a retryable failed platform that is still unsynced, the workflow resends the conversion only to that platform.

events.input: TikTok Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'tt-upload-55210', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:48:39Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

tools.description: Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to Facebook Ads in the audit trail.
Record an outbound offline conversion upload attempt sent to Google Ads in the audit trail.

name: Automated Offline Conversion Tracking

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of received Closed Won opportunity conversion event payloads and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event arrives, record the payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid event is received, preserve the event payload exactly for downstream processing.

peers.relationship: Forward each received Salesforce Closed Won opportunity conversion event for conversion extraction, deduplication, routing, retry handling, and sync tracking

event_sources: Salesforce: opportunity Closed Won conversion event

objects.role: Business logic object that knows the rules for extracting, deduplicating, routing, retrying, and confirming offline conversion syncs across ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Tracks conversion records by source opportunity, normalized conversion payloads, deduplication keys, per-platform delivery status, retry eligibility, retry schedules, attempt counts, last error by platform, and overall sync confirmation state.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce Closed Won conversion event arrives, extract the conversion data needed for downstream ad platform uploads. When a conversion record is extracted, compute a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppress any record whose deduplication key has already been processed successfully or is already in progress. When a conversion record is new, create per-platform delivery intents for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When dispatching a new or retryable conversion, send to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads. When a platform reports a successful upload, mark that platform as synced for the conversion. When a platform reports a failed upload, record the error, increment the attempt count for that platform, and schedule a retry for 15 minutes later if retry policy allows. When a scheduled retry time arrives for a platform that is not yet synced, resend the same normalized conversion payload to that platform. When all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, mark the conversion as fully confirmed. When retry policy is exhausted for any platform, keep the conversion in a failed state with its latest error and partial sync status.

peers.relationship: Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Facebook offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable Google offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable TikTok offline conversion uploads
Send normalized conversion payloads for new and retryable LinkedIn offline conversion uploads

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Facebook Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Facebook Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to Google Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for Google Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound Google Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to TikTok Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for TikTok Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object for recording outbound offline conversion uploads sent to LinkedIn Ads.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of conversion payloads received for LinkedIn Ads upload attempts, including timestamps and delivery outcomes received by the service.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload is received, record the outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt in the audit trail.

objects.role: Entry-point service object for inbound success and failure events from ad platform conversion upload APIs.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of platform upload result events, including platform name, conversion identity, status, error details, and receipt time.
objects.behavior: When an upload result event arrives from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads, record the result payload and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid upload result is received, preserve the reported status, conversion identity, platform identity, and any error details for downstream status handling.

peers.relationship: Forward each platform upload success or failure event for sync state updates and retry decisions

event_sources: Facebook Ads: conversion upload result event
Google Ads: conversion upload result event
TikTok Ads: conversion upload result event
LinkedIn Ads: conversion upload result event

objects.role: Entry-point service object for delayed retry wake-up events after the 15-minute retry interval.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of retry-due events received for scheduled conversion platform retries.
objects.behavior: When a retry-due event arrives, record the retry trigger and receipt time in the audit trail. When a valid retry trigger is received, preserve the conversion identity and platform context for downstream retry evaluation.

peers.relationship: Forward each retry-due event so retry eligibility can be checked and the upload resent if still pending

event_sources: Scheduler: conversion retry due event after 15 minutes

steps: The workflow watches Salesforce for new Closed Won opportunity conversion events. When one arrives, it records the event and receipt time for audit purposes and preserves the original event payload for downstream processing if the event is valid.

For each valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event, the workflow extracts the conversion data needed for offline conversion uploads to advertising platforms. This extracted conversion data becomes the normalized record used for all downstream delivery steps.

The workflow computes a stable deduplication key from the source conversion identity and suppresses any conversion that has already been processed successfully or is already being processed. Only conversions that are new continue through the sync workflow.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Facebook Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Facebook Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to Google Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound Google Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to TikTok Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound TikTok Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

For each new or retryable conversion, the workflow sends the same normalized conversion payload to LinkedIn Ads as an offline conversion upload. Each outbound LinkedIn Ads upload attempt is recorded for audit tracking.

When Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Ads reports a failed upload, the workflow records the error, increments that platform’s attempt count, and schedules a retry 15 minutes later if retries are still allowed. When the retry time arrives, the workflow resends the same normalized conversion payload only for platforms that are not yet marked as synced; if retry policy is exhausted, the conversion remains failed with its latest error and any partial sync status.

As upload results arrive from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads, the workflow marks each platform as synced when its upload succeeds. Once all four platforms are marked synced for the same conversion, the workflow marks that conversion as fully confirmed.

modifications.intent: For Salesforce Closed Won conversion events received on or after W02-1T10:30, keep the existing deduplication behavior for Facebook Ads, Google Ads, and TikTok Ads, but if the same deduplication key was already processed successfully or is already in progress, still create and dispatch a LinkedIn Ads delivery intent when the incoming Salesforce event contains a LinkedIn click ID and LinkedIn Ads is not yet marked synced for that conversion.

events.input: Salesforce emits an opportunity Closed Won conversion event for opportunity 'Acme Industrial Expansion - Q2 2026' with opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', account name 'Acme Industrial', contact 'James Brown', contact email 'james.brown@acmeindustrial.com', conversion timestamp '2026-05-21T14:32:00Z', currency 'USD', conversion value 125000.00, order ID 'ORD-99173', Salesforce campaign ID 'CAM-2048', click ID 'gclid=EAIaIQobChMI-demo-12345', Facebook click ID 'fbclid=fb.1.1716301920.demo67890', TikTok click ID 'ttclid=tt.demo.445566', LinkedIn click ID 'li_fat_id=li.demo.778899', and stage 'Closed Won'.

expect.action: The Salesforce Closed Won event is recorded with its receipt time in the audit trail, the original Salesforce payload is preserved for downstream use, a normalized offline conversion record is created for opportunity OPP-78421 / order ORD-99173 with deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z, and outbound offline conversion upload attempts using that same normalized payload are recorded and dispatched to Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.
expect.reason: A new valid Salesforce Closed Won conversion event is ingested, normalized, not suppressed as a duplicate, and sent to all four advertising platforms under baseline behavior.

events.input: Facebook Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Facebook Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'fb-upload-30017', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:10Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The Facebook Ads upload result for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail, and Facebook Ads is marked synced for that conversion.
expect.reason: A successful Facebook Ads upload result causes the system to persist the success and mark that platform complete for the conversion.

events.input: Google Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'Google Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'goog-upload-44128', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:18Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

events.input: TikTok Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', upload status 'failed', error code 'RATE_LIMIT', error message 'Too many requests', platform event ID 'tt-upload-55209', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:26Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The TikTok Ads failed upload result for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail with error code RATE_LIMIT and message 'Too many requests', TikTok Ads attempt count is incremented, and a retry for TikTok Ads is scheduled 15 minutes later for the same normalized conversion payload.
expect.reason: A retryable TikTok Ads failure leaves TikTok unsynced, records the error, and schedules the next allowed retry under the retry policy.

events.input: LinkedIn Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'LinkedIn Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'li-upload-66344', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:33:31Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.reason: A successful LinkedIn Ads upload result causes the system to persist the success and mark that platform complete for the conversion.

events.input: Scheduler emits a conversion retry due event after 15 minutes for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', retry attempt 1, retry due timestamp '2026-05-21T14:48:26Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: When the scheduled retry becomes due for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z, the system records and dispatches a new outbound TikTok Ads offline conversion upload attempt for the same normalized conversion payload; no new Facebook Ads, Google Ads, or LinkedIn Ads upload is sent at this time.
expect.reason: A retry-due event resends the conversion only to the still-unsynced platform named in the retry context.

events.input: TikTok Ads emits a conversion upload result event for order ID 'ORD-99173', opportunity ID 'OPP-78421', platform 'TikTok Ads', upload status 'success', platform event ID 'tt-upload-55210', received timestamp '2026-05-21T14:48:39Z', and deduplication key 'OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z'.

expect.action: The TikTok Ads upload result for deduplication key OPP-78421|ORD-99173|125000.00|2026-05-21T14:32:00Z is recorded in the platform results audit trail, TikTok Ads is marked synced for that conversion, and the conversion is marked fully confirmed because all four advertising platforms are now synced.
expect.reason: The final outstanding platform success completes platform sync and promotes the conversion to fully confirmed.

name: Employee Onboarding Manager

objects.role: Service object representing Workday new-employee registration events and forwarding each new-hire payload into the automation.
objects.state_description: Stateless event ingress for new employee registration events; does not retain business data beyond transient delivery context.
objects.behavior: When a new employee is registered in Workday, accept the event payload containing name, role, team, work email, start date, and birthday, and treat it as the authoritative onboarding trigger for this automation. Then forward the event to onboarding-rules.

peers.relationship: Forward new-hire registration details when a Workday new employee event arrives

event_sources: workday: employee registered

objects.role: Write service for creating Google Calendar onboarding events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of calendar event creation requests received, including event title, attendees, date, and team context.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding event creation request arrives, record the request and create the Google Calendar event with the new employee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager invited.

objects.role: Write service for adding employees to the company's internal Slack directory.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of directory add requests received, including employee identity, team, role, and work email.
objects.behavior: When an employee directory add request arrives, record the request and add the employee to the company's internal directory in Slack.

objects.role: Write service for posting onboarding announcements to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of Slack posting requests received, including channel, message body, and employee details.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding announcement request arrives, record the request and post to the #product-marketing channel introducing the new hire.

objects.role: Write service for sending onboarding and reminder emails.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of outbound email requests received, including recipient list, subject, template type, and employee context.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding checklist email request arrives, record the request and send the checklist email to the new hire with onboarding tasks and important links. When a birthday reminder email request arrives, record the request and send the reminder email to the Product Marketing team. When a work anniversary reminder email request arrives, record the request and send the reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

objects.role: Write service for storing annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedules and emitting due reminder events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of annual reminder schedules keyed by employee work email, with birthday date, work anniversary date, team, and reminder types to emit each year.
objects.behavior: When an annual reminder schedule request arrives, record the employee birthday and work anniversary schedule for recurring yearly reminders. When a birthday reminder becomes due, forward it to onboarding-rules via peer message. When a work anniversary reminder becomes due, forward it to onboarding-rules via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward employee reminder details when a scheduled birthday reminder becomes due
Forward employee reminder details when a scheduled work anniversary reminder becomes due

event_sources: scheduler: birthday reminder due
scheduler: work anniversary reminder due

objects.role: Business logic object that applies employee onboarding and milestone reminder rules for Product Marketing hires.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight onboarding and reminder decisions for employees, including whether onboarding outputs and annual reminder schedules have been derived from the incoming employee facts.
objects.behavior: When a new-hire registration arrives for an employee on the Product Marketing team, derive the onboarding calendar event details, Slack directory entry details, #product-marketing announcement content, onboarding checklist email content, and annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedule from the employee profile. When a new-hire registration arrives for an employee not on the Product Marketing team, do not emit Product Marketing team announcement or Product Marketing reminder actions. When a birthday reminder due message arrives, prepare a Product Marketing team birthday reminder email for that employee. When a work anniversary reminder due message arrives, prepare a Product Marketing team work anniversary reminder email for that employee.

peers.relationship: Send onboarding event details when a Product Marketing new-hire registration is processed
Send employee directory details when a new-hire registration is processed
Send onboarding announcement content when a Product Marketing new-hire registration is processed
Send onboarding checklist email details when a new-hire registration is processed, and send Product Marketing reminder email details when birthday or work anniversary reminders are due
Send annual birthday and work anniversary schedule details when a new-hire registration is processed

steps: When a new employee is registered in Workday, the workflow uses that registration as the authoritative trigger for onboarding and reads the employee's name, role, team, work email, start date, and birthday from the Workday profile.
For a new hire on the Product Marketing team, the workflow creates an onboarding event in Google Calendar and invites the new employee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager.
The workflow adds the new employee to the company's internal directory in Slack as part of onboarding.
If the new hire is on the Product Marketing team, the workflow posts an introduction announcement for that employee in Slack to the team's channel.
The workflow sends an onboarding checklist email to the new hire using their work email address, including onboarding tasks and important links.
The workflow stores the employee's birthday as a recurring annual reminder and, when that date comes due each year, sends a birthday reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

The workflow stores the employee's start date as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder and, when that date comes due each year, sends a work anniversary reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

modifications.intent: For the rest of this week, if a Product Marketing hire is starting on Friday, just hold off on the usual onboarding stuff for now.

events.input: Workday emits an employee registered event for Alex Rivera, Product Marketing Manager on the Product Marketing team, work email alex.rivera@company.com, start date 2026-06-15, birthday 1992-09-08.

expect.action: A Google Calendar onboarding event is created for Alex Rivera and invitations are sent to Alex Rivera, the Product Marketing team, and hiring manager Elena Rodriguez. Alex Rivera is added to the company’s internal directory in Slack. A Slack introduction announcement for Alex Rivera is posted in the Product Marketing team channel. An onboarding checklist email is sent to alex.rivera@company.com with onboarding tasks and important links. Alex Rivera’s birthday is stored as a recurring annual reminder. Alex Rivera’s start date is stored as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder.
expect.reason: A new Workday registration for a Product Marketing hire triggers onboarding, team announcement, email checklist, and creation of recurring birthday and work anniversary reminders in Google Calendar, Slack, email, and the reminder store.

events.input: On Alex Rivera's birthday reminder date, the scheduler emits a birthday reminder due event for Alex Rivera of the Product Marketing team at alex.rivera@company.com.

expect.action: A birthday reminder email for Alex Rivera is sent to the Product Marketing team.
expect.reason: When a stored birthday reminder comes due for a Product Marketing employee, the workflow sends a birthday reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

events.input: On Alex Rivera's work anniversary reminder date, the scheduler emits a work anniversary reminder due event for Alex Rivera of the Product Marketing team at alex.rivera@company.com, based on start date 2026-06-15.

expect.action: A work anniversary reminder email for Alex Rivera is sent to the Product Marketing team.
expect.reason: When a stored work anniversary reminder comes due for a Product Marketing employee, the workflow sends a work anniversary reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

events.input: Workday new employee registered: name Jordan Lee; role Product Marketing Coordinator; team Product Marketing; work email jordan.lee@company.com; start date 2026-06-19; birthday 1996-04-14.

expect.reason: M001 applies because Jordan Lee is a Product Marketing hire starting on Friday, and for the rest of this week the usual onboarding actions are held back for that case.

expect.action: Marcus Chen is added to the company’s internal directory in Slack. An onboarding checklist email is sent to marcus.chen@company.com with onboarding tasks and important links. name: Employee Onboarding Manager

objects.role: Service object representing Workday new-employee registration events and forwarding each new-hire payload into the automation.
objects.state_description: Stateless event ingress for new employee registration events; does not retain business data beyond transient delivery context.
objects.behavior: When a new employee is registered in Workday, accept the event payload containing name, role, team, work email, start date, and birthday, and treat it as the authoritative onboarding trigger for this automation. Then forward the event to onboarding-rules.

peers.relationship: Forward new-hire registration details when a Workday new employee event arrives

event_sources: workday: employee registered

objects.role: Write service for creating Google Calendar onboarding events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of calendar event creation requests received, including event title, attendees, date, and team context.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding event creation request arrives, record the request and create the Google Calendar event with the new employee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager invited.

objects.role: Write service for adding employees to the company's internal Slack directory.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of directory add requests received, including employee identity, team, role, and work email.
objects.behavior: When an employee directory add request arrives, record the request and add the employee to the company's internal directory in Slack.

objects.role: Write service for posting onboarding announcements to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of Slack posting requests received, including channel, message body, and employee details.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding announcement request arrives, record the request and post to the #product-marketing channel introducing the new hire.

objects.role: Write service for sending onboarding and reminder emails.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of outbound email requests received, including recipient list, subject, template type, and employee context.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding checklist email request arrives, record the request and send the checklist email to the new hire with onboarding tasks and important links. When a birthday reminder email request arrives, record the request and send the reminder email to the Product Marketing team. When a work anniversary reminder email request arrives, record the request and send the reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

objects.role: Write service for storing annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedules and emitting due reminder events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of annual reminder schedules keyed by employee work email, with birthday date, work anniversary date, team, and reminder types to emit each year.
objects.behavior: When an annual reminder schedule request arrives, record the employee birthday and work anniversary schedule for recurring yearly reminders. When a birthday reminder becomes due, forward it to onboarding-rules via peer message. When a work anniversary reminder becomes due, forward it to onboarding-rules via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward employee reminder details when a scheduled birthday reminder becomes due
Forward employee reminder details when a scheduled work anniversary reminder becomes due

event_sources: scheduler: birthday reminder due
scheduler: work anniversary reminder due

objects.role: Business logic object that applies employee onboarding and milestone reminder rules for Product Marketing hires.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight onboarding and reminder decisions for employees, including whether onboarding outputs and annual reminder schedules have been derived from the incoming employee facts.
objects.behavior: When a new-hire registration arrives for an employee on the Product Marketing team, derive the onboarding calendar event details, Slack directory entry details, #product-marketing announcement content, onboarding checklist email content, and annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedule from the employee profile. When a new-hire registration arrives for an employee not on the Product Marketing team, do not emit Product Marketing team announcement or Product Marketing reminder actions. When a birthday reminder due message arrives, prepare a Product Marketing team birthday reminder email for that employee. When a work anniversary reminder due message arrives, prepare a Product Marketing team work anniversary reminder email for that employee.

peers.relationship: Send onboarding event details when a Product Marketing new-hire registration is processed
Send employee directory details when a new-hire registration is processed
Send onboarding announcement content when a Product Marketing new-hire registration is processed
Send onboarding checklist email details when a new-hire registration is processed, and send Product Marketing reminder email details when birthday or work anniversary reminders are due
Send annual birthday and work anniversary schedule details when a new-hire registration is processed

steps: When a new employee is registered in Workday, the workflow uses that registration as the authoritative trigger for onboarding and reads the employee's name, role, team, work email, start date, and birthday from the Workday profile.
For a new hire on the Product Marketing team, the workflow creates an onboarding event in Google Calendar and invites the new employee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager.
The workflow adds the new employee to the company's internal directory in Slack as part of onboarding.
If the new hire is on the Product Marketing team, the workflow posts an introduction announcement for that employee in Slack to the team's channel.
The workflow sends an onboarding checklist email to the new hire using their work email address, including onboarding tasks and important links.
The workflow stores the employee's birthday as a recurring annual reminder and, when that date comes due each year, sends a birthday reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

The workflow stores the employee's start date as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder and, when that date comes due each year, sends a work anniversary reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

modifications.intent: For Product Marketing new hires, only run the onboarding actions when the start date is still upcoming; if Workday sends someone whose start date has already passed, skip the onboarding event, Slack directory add, Slack announcement, checklist email, and annual reminder scheduling.

events.input: Workday emits an employee registered event for Alex Rivera, Product Marketing Manager on the Product Marketing team, work email alex.rivera@company.com, start date 2026-06-15, birthday 1992-09-08.

expect.action: A Google Calendar onboarding event is created for Alex Rivera and invitations are sent to Alex Rivera, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager. Alex Rivera is added to the company's internal directory in Slack. An introduction announcement for Alex Rivera is posted in the Product Marketing Slack channel. An onboarding checklist email is sent to alex.rivera@company.com with onboarding tasks and important links. Alex Rivera's birthday is stored as a recurring annual reminder, and Alex Rivera's start date is stored as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder.
expect.reason: Under the baseline onboarding workflow, a new Workday registration for a Product Marketing hire triggers calendar setup, Slack onboarding actions, checklist email delivery, and scheduling of annual birthday and work anniversary reminders.

events.input: On Alex Rivera's birthday reminder date, the scheduler emits a birthday reminder due event for Alex Rivera of the Product Marketing team at alex.rivera@company.com.

expect.action: A birthday reminder email is sent to the Product Marketing team announcing Alex Rivera's birthday.
expect.reason: When a stored birthday reminder comes due, the workflow sends a birthday reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

events.input: On Alex Rivera's work anniversary reminder date, the scheduler emits a work anniversary reminder due event for Alex Rivera of the Product Marketing team at alex.rivera@company.com, based on start date 2026-06-15.

expect.action: A work anniversary reminder email is sent to the Product Marketing team announcing Alex Rivera's work anniversary.
expect.reason: When a stored work anniversary reminder comes due, the workflow sends a work anniversary reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

expect.action: A Google Calendar onboarding event is created for Jordan Lee and invitations are sent to Jordan Lee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager. Jordan Lee is added to the company's internal directory in Slack. An introduction announcement for Jordan Lee is posted in the Product Marketing Slack channel. An onboarding checklist email is sent to jordan.lee@company.com with onboarding tasks and important links. Jordan Lee's birthday is stored as a recurring annual reminder, and Jordan Lee's start date is stored as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder.
expect.reason: Under the baseline onboarding workflow, a new Workday registration for a Product Marketing hire triggers calendar setup, Slack onboarding actions, checklist email delivery, and scheduling of annual birthday and work anniversary reminders.

events.input: Workday new employee registered event: employee_name=Priya Nair; role=Product Marketing Analyst; team=Product Marketing; work_email=priya.nair@company.com; start_date=2026-06-10; birthday=1993-04-19; workday_event_id=wd-emp-10022.

expect.action: [suppressed by M001] No Google Calendar onboarding event is created for Priya Nair, no Slack directory entry is added, no Product Marketing Slack introduction announcement is posted, no onboarding checklist email is sent to priya.nair@company.com, and no annual birthday or work anniversary reminders are scheduled.
expect.reason: M001 applies because this is a Product Marketing new hire whose Workday registration arrived after the start date had already passed, so all onboarding actions and annual reminder scheduling are skipped.

events.input: Workday new employee registered event: employee_name=Elena Gomez; role=Product Marketing Manager; team=Product Marketing; work_email=elena.gomez@company.com; start_date=2026-07-06; birthday=1991-12-14; workday_event_id=wd-emp-10023.

expect.action: A Google Calendar onboarding event is created for Elena Gomez and invitations are sent to Elena Gomez, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager. Elena Gomez is added to the company's internal directory in Slack. An introduction announcement for Elena Gomez is posted in the Product Marketing Slack channel. An onboarding checklist email is sent to elena.gomez@company.com with onboarding tasks and important links. Elena Gomez's birthday is stored as a recurring annual reminder, and Elena Gomez's start date is stored as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder. [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]
expect.reason: Because Elena Gomez is a Product Marketing new hire whose start date is still upcoming, M001 does not change the baseline onboarding behavior.

events.input: Birthday reminder due event: employee_name=Alex Rivera; team=Product Marketing; work_email=alex.rivera@company.com; reminder_type=birthday; birthday=1992-09-08; scheduler_event_id=anniv-20031.

expect.action: A birthday reminder email is sent to the Product Marketing team announcing Alex Rivera's birthday. [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]
expect.reason: M001 only changes onboarding-time handling for Product Marketing new hires with past start dates and does not alter birthday reminder emails when a scheduled reminder becomes due.

events.input: Work anniversary reminder due event: employee_name=Sam Chen; team=Product Marketing; work_email=sam.chen@company.com; reminder_type=work_anniversary; start_date=2026-06-15; scheduler_event_id=anniv-20032.

expect.action: A work anniversary reminder email is sent to the Product Marketing team announcing Sam Chen's work anniversary. [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]
expect.reason: M001 only changes onboarding-time handling for Product Marketing new hires with past start dates and does not alter work anniversary reminder emails when a scheduled reminder becomes due.
