name: Employee Onboarding Manager

objects.role: Service object representing Workday new-employee registration events and forwarding each new-hire payload into the automation.
objects.state_description: Stateless event ingress for new employee registration events; does not retain business data beyond transient delivery context.
objects.behavior: When a new employee is registered in Workday, accept the event payload containing name, role, team, work email, start date, and birthday, and treat it as the authoritative onboarding trigger for this automation. Then forward the event to onboarding-rules.

peers.relationship: Forward new-hire registration details when a Workday new employee event arrives

event_sources: workday: employee registered

objects.role: Write service for creating Google Calendar onboarding events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of calendar event creation requests received, including event title, attendees, date, and team context.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding event creation request arrives, record the request and create the Google Calendar event with the new employee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager invited.

objects.role: Write service for adding employees to the company's internal Slack directory.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of directory add requests received, including employee identity, team, role, and work email.
objects.behavior: When an employee directory add request arrives, record the request and add the employee to the company's internal directory in Slack.

objects.role: Write service for posting onboarding announcements to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of Slack posting requests received, including channel, message body, and employee details.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding announcement request arrives, record the request and post to the #product-marketing channel introducing the new hire.

objects.role: Write service for sending onboarding and reminder emails.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of outbound email requests received, including recipient list, subject, template type, and employee context.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding checklist email request arrives, record the request and send the checklist email to the new hire with onboarding tasks and important links. When a birthday reminder email request arrives, record the request and send the reminder email to the Product Marketing team. When a work anniversary reminder email request arrives, record the request and send the reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

objects.role: Write service for storing annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedules and emitting due reminder events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of annual reminder schedules keyed by employee work email, with birthday date, work anniversary date, team, and reminder types to emit each year.
objects.behavior: When an annual reminder schedule request arrives, record the employee birthday and work anniversary schedule for recurring yearly reminders. When a birthday reminder becomes due, forward it to onboarding-rules via peer message. When a work anniversary reminder becomes due, forward it to onboarding-rules via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward employee reminder details when a scheduled birthday reminder becomes due
Forward employee reminder details when a scheduled work anniversary reminder becomes due

event_sources: scheduler: birthday reminder due
scheduler: work anniversary reminder due

objects.role: Business logic object that applies employee onboarding and milestone reminder rules for Product Marketing hires.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight onboarding and reminder decisions for employees, including whether onboarding outputs and annual reminder schedules have been derived from the incoming employee facts.
objects.behavior: When a new-hire registration arrives for an employee on the Product Marketing team, derive the onboarding calendar event details, Slack directory entry details, #product-marketing announcement content, onboarding checklist email content, and annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedule from the employee profile. When a new-hire registration arrives for an employee not on the Product Marketing team, do not emit Product Marketing team announcement or Product Marketing reminder actions. When a birthday reminder due message arrives, prepare a Product Marketing team birthday reminder email for that employee. When a work anniversary reminder due message arrives, prepare a Product Marketing team work anniversary reminder email for that employee.

peers.relationship: Send onboarding event details when a Product Marketing new-hire registration is processed
Send employee directory details when a new-hire registration is processed
Send onboarding announcement content when a Product Marketing new-hire registration is processed
Send onboarding checklist email details when a new-hire registration is processed, and send Product Marketing reminder email details when birthday or work anniversary reminders are due
Send annual birthday and work anniversary schedule details when a new-hire registration is processed

steps: When a new employee is registered in Workday, the workflow uses that registration as the authoritative trigger for onboarding and reads the employee's name, role, team, work email, start date, and birthday from the Workday profile.
For a new hire on the Product Marketing team, the workflow creates an onboarding event in Google Calendar and invites the new employee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager.
The workflow adds the new employee to the company's internal directory in Slack as part of onboarding.
If the new hire is on the Product Marketing team, the workflow posts an introduction announcement for that employee in Slack to the team's channel.
The workflow sends an onboarding checklist email to the new hire using their work email address, including onboarding tasks and important links.
The workflow stores the employee's birthday as a recurring annual reminder and, when that date comes due each year, sends a birthday reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

The workflow stores the employee's start date as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder and, when that date comes due each year, sends a work anniversary reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

modifications.intent: Treat Jordan Lee, work email jordan.lee@company.com, as an exception: when a Workday new-hire registration arrives for Jordan Lee with team listed as Sales, still process the employee with the full Product Marketing onboarding flow by sending the onboarding calendar event, Slack directory entry, #product-marketing announcement, onboarding checklist email, and annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedule exactly as you would for a Product Marketing team hire.

events.input: Workday emits an employee registered event for Alex Rivera, Product Marketing Manager on the Product Marketing team, work email alex.rivera@company.com, start date 2026-06-15, birthday 1992-09-08.

expect.action: A Google Calendar onboarding event is created for Alex Rivera and invitations are sent to Alex Rivera, the Product Marketing team, and Alex Rivera's hiring manager. Alex Rivera is added to the company's internal directory in Slack. An introduction announcement for Alex Rivera is posted in the Product Marketing Slack channel. An onboarding checklist email is sent to alex.rivera@company.com with onboarding tasks and important links. A recurring annual birthday reminder for Alex Rivera is stored, and a recurring annual work anniversary reminder based on start date 2026-06-15 is stored.
expect.reason: Because Workday registered a new hire on the Product Marketing team, the workflow performs the full Product Marketing onboarding flow and schedules annual birthday and work anniversary reminders.

events.input: On Alex Rivera's birthday reminder date, the scheduler emits a birthday reminder due event for Alex Rivera of the Product Marketing team at alex.rivera@company.com.

expect.action: A birthday reminder email for Alex Rivera is sent to the Product Marketing team.

events.input: On Alex Rivera's work anniversary reminder date, the scheduler emits a work anniversary reminder due event for Alex Rivera of the Product Marketing team at alex.rivera@company.com, based on start date 2026-06-15.

expect.action: A work anniversary reminder email for Alex Rivera is sent to the Product Marketing team.
expect.reason: When a stored work anniversary reminder comes due for an employee on the Product Marketing team, the workflow emails the team a work anniversary reminder.

name: Employee Onboarding Manager

objects.role: Service object representing Workday new-employee registration events and forwarding each new-hire payload into the automation.
objects.state_description: Stateless event ingress for new employee registration events; does not retain business data beyond transient delivery context.
objects.behavior: When a new employee is registered in Workday, accept the event payload containing name, role, team, work email, start date, and birthday, and treat it as the authoritative onboarding trigger for this automation. Then forward the event to onboarding-rules.

peers.relationship: Forward new-hire registration details when a Workday new employee event arrives

event_sources: workday: employee registered

objects.role: Write service for creating Google Calendar onboarding events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of calendar event creation requests received, including event title, attendees, date, and team context.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding event creation request arrives, record the request and create the Google Calendar event with the new employee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager invited.

objects.role: Write service for adding employees to the company's internal Slack directory.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of directory add requests received, including employee identity, team, role, and work email.
objects.behavior: When an employee directory add request arrives, record the request and add the employee to the company's internal directory in Slack.

objects.role: Write service for posting onboarding announcements to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of Slack posting requests received, including channel, message body, and employee details.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding announcement request arrives, record the request and post to the #product-marketing channel introducing the new hire.

objects.role: Write service for sending onboarding and reminder emails.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of outbound email requests received, including recipient list, subject, template type, and employee context.
objects.behavior: When an onboarding checklist email request arrives, record the request and send the checklist email to the new hire with onboarding tasks and important links. When a birthday reminder email request arrives, record the request and send the reminder email to the Product Marketing team. When a work anniversary reminder email request arrives, record the request and send the reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

objects.role: Write service for storing annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedules and emitting due reminder events.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of annual reminder schedules keyed by employee work email, with birthday date, work anniversary date, team, and reminder types to emit each year.
objects.behavior: When an annual reminder schedule request arrives, record the employee birthday and work anniversary schedule for recurring yearly reminders. When a birthday reminder becomes due, forward it to onboarding-rules via peer message. When a work anniversary reminder becomes due, forward it to onboarding-rules via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward employee reminder details when a scheduled birthday reminder becomes due
Forward employee reminder details when a scheduled work anniversary reminder becomes due

event_sources: scheduler: birthday reminder due
scheduler: work anniversary reminder due

objects.role: Business logic object that applies employee onboarding and milestone reminder rules for Product Marketing hires.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight onboarding and reminder decisions for employees, including whether onboarding outputs and annual reminder schedules have been derived from the incoming employee facts.
objects.behavior: When a new-hire registration arrives for an employee on the Product Marketing team, derive the onboarding calendar event details, Slack directory entry details, #product-marketing announcement content, onboarding checklist email content, and annual birthday and work anniversary reminder schedule from the employee profile. When a new-hire registration arrives for an employee not on the Product Marketing team, do not emit Product Marketing team announcement or Product Marketing reminder actions. When a birthday reminder due message arrives, prepare a Product Marketing team birthday reminder email for that employee. When a work anniversary reminder due message arrives, prepare a Product Marketing team work anniversary reminder email for that employee.

peers.relationship: Send onboarding event details when a Product Marketing new-hire registration is processed
Send employee directory details when a new-hire registration is processed
Send onboarding announcement content when a Product Marketing new-hire registration is processed
Send onboarding checklist email details when a new-hire registration is processed, and send Product Marketing reminder email details when birthday or work anniversary reminders are due
Send annual birthday and work anniversary schedule details when a new-hire registration is processed

steps: When a new employee is registered in Workday, the workflow uses that registration as the authoritative trigger for onboarding and reads the employee's name, role, team, work email, start date, and birthday from the Workday profile.
For a new hire on the Product Marketing team, the workflow creates an onboarding event in Google Calendar and invites the new employee, the Product Marketing team, and the hiring manager.
The workflow adds the new employee to the company's internal directory in Slack as part of onboarding.
If the new hire is on the Product Marketing team, the workflow posts an introduction announcement for that employee in Slack to the team's channel.
The workflow sends an onboarding checklist email to the new hire using their work email address, including onboarding tasks and important links.
The workflow stores the employee's birthday as a recurring annual reminder and, when that date comes due each year, sends a birthday reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

The workflow stores the employee's start date as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder and, when that date comes due each year, sends a work anniversary reminder email to the Product Marketing team.

modifications.intent: Let's skip those anniversary reminder emails for Product Marketing hires for now.

events.input: Workday emits an employee registered event for Alex Rivera, Product Marketing Manager on the Product Marketing team, work email alex.rivera@company.com, start date 2026-06-15, birthday 1992-09-08.

expect.action: A Google Calendar onboarding event is created for Alex Rivera and invitations are sent to Alex Rivera, the Product Marketing team, and hiring manager Elena Rodriguez. Alex Rivera is added to the company’s Slack directory. An introduction announcement for Alex Rivera is posted in the Product Marketing Slack channel. An onboarding checklist email with onboarding tasks and important links is sent to alex.rivera@company.com. Alex Rivera’s birthday is stored as a recurring annual reminder. Alex Rivera’s start date of 2026-06-15 is stored as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder.
expect.reason: A new Workday registration for a Product Marketing hire triggers the full onboarding flow, including team-specific calendar and Slack actions plus recurring birthday and work anniversary reminder setup.

events.input: On Alex Rivera's birthday reminder date, the scheduler emits a birthday reminder due event for Alex Rivera of the Product Marketing team at alex.rivera@company.com.

expect.action: A birthday reminder email for Alex Rivera is sent to the Product Marketing team.
expect.reason: When a stored birthday reminder comes due for a Product Marketing employee, the workflow sends a birthday reminder email to that team.

events.input: On Alex Rivera's work anniversary reminder date, the scheduler emits a work anniversary reminder due event for Alex Rivera of the Product Marketing team at alex.rivera@company.com, based on start date 2026-06-15.

expect.action: A work anniversary reminder email for Alex Rivera is sent to the Product Marketing team.
expect.reason: When a stored work anniversary reminder comes due for a Product Marketing employee, the workflow sends a work anniversary reminder email to that team.

events.input: Workday new employee registered event: employee_name=Jordan Lee; role=Product Marketing Specialist; team=Product Marketing; work_email=jordan.lee@company.com; start_date=2026-07-06; birthday=1994-11-21.

expect.action: A Google Calendar onboarding event is created for Jordan Lee and invitations are sent to Jordan Lee, the Product Marketing team, and hiring manager Maya Patel. Jordan Lee is added to the company’s Slack directory. An introduction announcement for Jordan Lee is posted in the Product Marketing Slack channel. An onboarding checklist email with onboarding tasks and important links is sent to jordan.lee@company.com. Jordan Lee’s birthday is stored as a recurring annual reminder. Jordan Lee’s start date of 2026-07-06 is stored as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder.
expect.reason: A new Workday registration for a Product Marketing hire triggers the full onboarding flow, including team-specific calendar and Slack actions plus recurring birthday and work anniversary reminder setup.

events.input: Birthday reminder due event: employee_name=Priya Nair; team=Product Marketing; work_email=priya.nair@company.com; reminder_type=birthday; birthday=1991-04-12.

expect.action: A birthday reminder email for Priya Nair is sent to the Product Marketing team.
expect.reason: [M001 condition not met: the modification skips only work anniversary reminder emails for Product Marketing hires, but this event is a birthday reminder due event]

events.input: Workday new employee registered event: employee_name=Marcus Chen; role=Sales Operations Analyst; team=Sales Operations; work_email=marcus.chen@company.com; start_date=2026-07-13; birthday=1990-02-03.

expect.action: Marcus Chen is added to the company’s Slack directory. An onboarding checklist email with onboarding tasks and important links is sent to marcus.chen@company.com. Marcus Chen’s birthday is stored as a recurring annual reminder. Marcus Chen’s start date of 2026-07-13 is stored as a recurring annual work anniversary reminder.
expect.reason: A new Workday registration for a non-Product-Marketing hire triggers standard onboarding actions, but not the Product Marketing-only calendar event or team Slack announcement.

events.input: Work anniversary reminder due event: employee_name=Elena Gomez; team=Product Marketing; work_email=elena.gomez@company.com; reminder_type=work_anniversary; start_date=2026-06-15; work_anniversary_date=2027-06-15.

expect.action: [suppressed by M001] A work anniversary reminder email for Elena Gomez is not sent to the Product Marketing team.
expect.reason: M001 applies to Product Marketing work anniversary reminders and explicitly skips those reminder emails going forward.

events.input: Birthday reminder due event: employee_name=Noah Patel; team=Finance; work_email=noah.patel@company.com; reminder_type=birthday; birthday=1988-09-30.

expect.action: A birthday reminder email for Noah Patel is sent to the Finance team.
expect.reason: [M001 condition not met: the modification skips only work anniversary reminder emails for Product Marketing hires, but this event is a birthday reminder for a Finance employee]

tools.description: Create a Google Calendar onboarding event and invite the specified attendees.
Add an employee record to the company's internal Slack directory.
Post an onboarding announcement message to a Slack channel.
Send an onboarding or reminder email to the specified recipient.
Store a recurring yearly reminder schedule for an employee's birthday and work anniversary.

Retrieve reference data from org directory data. Employee directory and org chart data containing user identities, team membership, manager relationships, and distribution list or group email information needed to identify the hiring manager and Product Marketing recipients.

name: Out-of-Office Task Tracking System

objects.role: Dual-role service object for the Retool app that receives out-of-office status changes and return-session task updates, and presents task data to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Retool events such as out-of-office activation, return access, and task status updates initiated in the app; does not store authoritative task records.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event arrives, treat the submitted absence context as the workflow trigger. When a return-session task status update event arrives, treat the submitted task identifier and new status as a user action to be processed. When a return access event arrives, treat it as a request to load current tasks for display. When out-of-office activation, return access, or task status update arrives, forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Retool out-of-office activation, return access, and task status update events when submitted in the app

event_sources: retool app: out-of-office activation submitted
retool app: return access requested
retool app: task status update submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Slack emoji reaction events from messages in #revops-requests during an active out-of-office period.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Slack reaction events received from #revops-requests.
objects.behavior: When a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, capture the message reference, reactor, emoji, channel, and requester context included in the event and forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction events from #revops-requests for task creation during out-of-office periods

event_sources: slack: reaction added in #revops-requests

objects.role: Read service object for the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table, providing task lookup and retrieval for current out-of-office tracking data.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime state; task data is retrieved on demand from the Airtable Tasks table.
objects.behavior: When asked for tasks by status, assignee, message reference, requester, or out-of-office session, return matching records from the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked for all accumulated tasks for return review, return the current task list with identifiers, source message references, requester details, reaction metadata, and statuses. When asked for a specific task, return the matching task record. When answering queries, call the `airtable-tasks_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object for creating and updating task records in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of task create and task update write requests sent to Airtable.
objects.behavior: When asked to create a task, record the provided message reference, requester, reaction metadata, out-of-office session context, and initial status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked to update a task, record the provided task identifier and new status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.

objects.role: Write service object for posting Slack notifications to #revops-team and #revops-requests.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound Slack messages posted by the automation.
objects.behavior: When asked to announce out-of-office activation, post to the #revops-team channel with the absence notice and task-tracking activation context. When asked to notify a requester of task status, post to the #revops-requests channel with the original request reference, updated status, and requester-facing context.

objects.role: Write service object for sending task lists and task-update results back to the Retool app UI for display.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound UI payloads sent to Retool for rendering task lists and status refreshes.
objects.behavior: When asked to display accumulated tasks, send the current task list with identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses to the Retool app UI. When asked to refresh a task after status change, send the updated task state to the Retool app UI.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies the rules for activating out-of-office tracking, converting Slack reactions into tracked tasks, and handling return-time task review and status updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks whether out-of-office tracking is active, the current absence session context, and correlations between Slack request messages, Airtable task records, and requester notification state.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event is received, mark tracking active for the submitted absence session and prepare notifications and task capture for that session. When tracking is active and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, interpret the reaction as a task/request to capture while the user is away, derive a task record from the message and reaction context, and assign initial status New. When tracking is inactive and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives, ignore it for task creation. When a return access event is received, retrieve the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent absence session and prepare them for display in Retool. When a task status update event is received, allow only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done; reject any other status. When a valid task status update is received, prepare the requester notification context tied to the original Slack request. Send to slack-notifications and airtable-task-writes for out-of-office activation. Send to airtable-task-writes when a new task should be created from a Slack reaction. Send to airtable-tasks and retool-task-view when return access requires task display. Send to airtable-task-writes, airtable-tasks, slack-notifications, and retool-task-view when a valid task status update is submitted.

peers.relationship: Send out-of-office announcement requests for #revops-team and requester status notifications for #revops-requests
Send task create requests for captured Slack reactions and task update requests for status changes
Request current task records for return review and task refresh context
Send task lists and refreshed task state for display in the Retool app

steps: When a user marks themselves out of office in the Retool interface, the workflow starts an absence-tracking session using the submitted absence context. It then sends a Slack announcement to the team and enables capture of incoming requests for that out-of-office period.

While that out-of-office session is active, the workflow listens for emoji reactions added to messages in Slack request conversations. If tracking is not active, those reactions are ignored and no task is created.

For each qualifying Slack reaction received during the active absence period, the workflow turns the message and reaction context into a tracked task in Airtable. It stores the original message reference, requester details, reaction metadata, the related out-of-office session, and sets the initial task status to New.

When the user returns and opens the Retool interface, the workflow retrieves the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent out-of-office session from Airtable. It displays the current list with task identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses.

As the user works through the backlog in Retool, they can update each task's status directly in the interface. The workflow accepts only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done, and rejects any other value.

Whenever a valid task status change is submitted, the workflow updates the task in Airtable and sends a Slack notification tied to the original request so the requester is informed of the new status. It also refreshes the task display in Retool with the updated state.

The workflow can also be viewed in Canvas as a visual map of how Retool, Slack, and Airtable interact across out-of-office activation, task capture, return review, and requester notifications. This step is for visualization only and does not change workflow data or behavior.

modifications.intent: While I'm out, only treat the more obvious follow-up reactions in #revops-requests as tasks — basically keep it to eyes reactions for capture and leave the other emoji alone for now.

tools.description: Post a notification message to a Slack channel for out-of-office activation or requester task status updates.
Create a new task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with the provided request, reaction, session, and initial status data.
Update an existing task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with a new status and related task details.
Send accumulated task lists or updated task state back to the Retool app UI for display.

name: Out-of-Office Task Tracking System

objects.role: Dual-role service object for the Retool app that receives out-of-office status changes and return-session task updates, and presents task data to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Retool events such as out-of-office activation, return access, and task status updates initiated in the app; does not store authoritative task records.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event arrives, treat the submitted absence context as the workflow trigger. When a return-session task status update event arrives, treat the submitted task identifier and new status as a user action to be processed. When a return access event arrives, treat it as a request to load current tasks for display. When out-of-office activation, return access, or task status update arrives, forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Retool out-of-office activation, return access, and task status update events when submitted in the app

event_sources: retool app: out-of-office activation submitted
retool app: return access requested
retool app: task status update submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Slack emoji reaction events from messages in #revops-requests during an active out-of-office period.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Slack reaction events received from #revops-requests.
objects.behavior: When a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, capture the message reference, reactor, emoji, channel, and requester context included in the event and forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction events from #revops-requests for task creation during out-of-office periods

event_sources: slack: reaction added in #revops-requests

objects.role: Read service object for the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table, providing task lookup and retrieval for current out-of-office tracking data.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime state; task data is retrieved on demand from the Airtable Tasks table.
objects.behavior: When asked for tasks by status, assignee, message reference, requester, or out-of-office session, return matching records from the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked for all accumulated tasks for return review, return the current task list with identifiers, source message references, requester details, reaction metadata, and statuses. When asked for a specific task, return the matching task record. When answering queries, call the `airtable-tasks_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object for creating and updating task records in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of task create and task update write requests sent to Airtable.
objects.behavior: When asked to create a task, record the provided message reference, requester, reaction metadata, out-of-office session context, and initial status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked to update a task, record the provided task identifier and new status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.

objects.role: Write service object for posting Slack notifications to #revops-team and #revops-requests.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound Slack messages posted by the automation.
objects.behavior: When asked to announce out-of-office activation, post to the #revops-team channel with the absence notice and task-tracking activation context. When asked to notify a requester of task status, post to the #revops-requests channel with the original request reference, updated status, and requester-facing context.

objects.role: Write service object for sending task lists and task-update results back to the Retool app UI for display.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound UI payloads sent to Retool for rendering task lists and status refreshes.
objects.behavior: When asked to display accumulated tasks, send the current task list with identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses to the Retool app UI. When asked to refresh a task after status change, send the updated task state to the Retool app UI.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies the rules for activating out-of-office tracking, converting Slack reactions into tracked tasks, and handling return-time task review and status updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks whether out-of-office tracking is active, the current absence session context, and correlations between Slack request messages, Airtable task records, and requester notification state.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event is received, mark tracking active for the submitted absence session and prepare notifications and task capture for that session. When tracking is active and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, interpret the reaction as a task/request to capture while the user is away, derive a task record from the message and reaction context, and assign initial status New. When tracking is inactive and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives, ignore it for task creation. When a return access event is received, retrieve the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent absence session and prepare them for display in Retool. When a task status update event is received, allow only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done; reject any other status. When a valid task status update is received, prepare the requester notification context tied to the original Slack request. Send to slack-notifications and airtable-task-writes for out-of-office activation. Send to airtable-task-writes when a new task should be created from a Slack reaction. Send to airtable-tasks and retool-task-view when return access requires task display. Send to airtable-task-writes, airtable-tasks, slack-notifications, and retool-task-view when a valid task status update is submitted.

peers.relationship: Send out-of-office announcement requests for #revops-team and requester status notifications for #revops-requests
Send task create requests for captured Slack reactions and task update requests for status changes
Request current task records for return review and task refresh context
Send task lists and refreshed task state for display in the Retool app

steps: When a user marks themselves out of office in the Retool interface, the workflow starts an absence-tracking session using the submitted absence context. It then sends a Slack announcement to the team and enables capture of incoming requests for that out-of-office period.

While that out-of-office session is active, the workflow listens for emoji reactions added to messages in Slack request conversations. If tracking is not active, those reactions are ignored and no task is created.

For each qualifying Slack reaction received during the active absence period, the workflow turns the message and reaction context into a tracked task in Airtable. It stores the original message reference, requester details, reaction metadata, the related out-of-office session, and sets the initial task status to New.

When the user returns and opens the Retool interface, the workflow retrieves the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent out-of-office session from Airtable. It displays the current list with task identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses.

As the user works through the backlog in Retool, they can update each task's status directly in the interface. The workflow accepts only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done, and rejects any other value.

Whenever a valid task status change is submitted, the workflow updates the task in Airtable and sends a Slack notification tied to the original request so the requester is informed of the new status. It also refreshes the task display in Retool with the updated state.

The workflow can also be viewed in Canvas as a visual map of how Retool, Slack, and Airtable interact across out-of-office activation, task capture, return review, and requester notifications. This step is for visualization only and does not change workflow data or behavior.

tools.description: Post a notification message to a Slack channel for out-of-office activation or requester task status updates.
Create a new task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with the provided request, reaction, session, and initial status data.
Update an existing task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with a new status and related task details.
Send accumulated task lists or updated task state back to the Retool app UI for display.

name: Out-of-Office Task Tracking System

objects.role: Dual-role service object for the Retool app that receives out-of-office status changes and return-session task updates, and presents task data to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Retool events such as out-of-office activation, return access, and task status updates initiated in the app; does not store authoritative task records.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event arrives, treat the submitted absence context as the workflow trigger. When a return-session task status update event arrives, treat the submitted task identifier and new status as a user action to be processed. When a return access event arrives, treat it as a request to load current tasks for display. When out-of-office activation, return access, or task status update arrives, forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Retool out-of-office activation, return access, and task status update events when submitted in the app

event_sources: retool app: out-of-office activation submitted
retool app: return access requested
retool app: task status update submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Slack emoji reaction events from messages in #revops-requests during an active out-of-office period.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Slack reaction events received from #revops-requests.
objects.behavior: When a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, capture the message reference, reactor, emoji, channel, and requester context included in the event and forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction events from #revops-requests for task creation during out-of-office periods

event_sources: slack: reaction added in #revops-requests

objects.role: Read service object for the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table, providing task lookup and retrieval for current out-of-office tracking data.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime state; task data is retrieved on demand from the Airtable Tasks table.
objects.behavior: When asked for tasks by status, assignee, message reference, requester, or out-of-office session, return matching records from the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked for all accumulated tasks for return review, return the current task list with identifiers, source message references, requester details, reaction metadata, and statuses. When asked for a specific task, return the matching task record. When answering queries, call the `airtable-tasks_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object for creating and updating task records in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of task create and task update write requests sent to Airtable.
objects.behavior: When asked to create a task, record the provided message reference, requester, reaction metadata, out-of-office session context, and initial status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked to update a task, record the provided task identifier and new status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.

objects.role: Write service object for posting Slack notifications to #revops-team and #revops-requests.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound Slack messages posted by the automation.
objects.behavior: When asked to announce out-of-office activation, post to the #revops-team channel with the absence notice and task-tracking activation context. When asked to notify a requester of task status, post to the #revops-requests channel with the original request reference, updated status, and requester-facing context.

objects.role: Write service object for sending task lists and task-update results back to the Retool app UI for display.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound UI payloads sent to Retool for rendering task lists and status refreshes.
objects.behavior: When asked to display accumulated tasks, send the current task list with identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses to the Retool app UI. When asked to refresh a task after status change, send the updated task state to the Retool app UI.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies the rules for activating out-of-office tracking, converting Slack reactions into tracked tasks, and handling return-time task review and status updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks whether out-of-office tracking is active, the current absence session context, and correlations between Slack request messages, Airtable task records, and requester notification state.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event is received, mark tracking active for the submitted absence session and prepare notifications and task capture for that session. When tracking is active and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, interpret the reaction as a task/request to capture while the user is away, derive a task record from the message and reaction context, and assign initial status New. When tracking is inactive and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives, ignore it for task creation. When a return access event is received, retrieve the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent absence session and prepare them for display in Retool. When a task status update event is received, allow only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done; reject any other status. When a valid task status update is received, prepare the requester notification context tied to the original Slack request. Send to slack-notifications and airtable-task-writes for out-of-office activation. Send to airtable-task-writes when a new task should be created from a Slack reaction. Send to airtable-tasks and retool-task-view when return access requires task display. Send to airtable-task-writes, airtable-tasks, slack-notifications, and retool-task-view when a valid task status update is submitted.

peers.relationship: Send out-of-office announcement requests for #revops-team and requester status notifications for #revops-requests
Send task create requests for captured Slack reactions and task update requests for status changes
Request current task records for return review and task refresh context
Send task lists and refreshed task state for display in the Retool app

steps: When a user marks themselves out of office in the Retool interface, the workflow starts an absence-tracking session using the submitted absence context. It then sends a Slack announcement to the team and enables capture of incoming requests for that out-of-office period.

While that out-of-office session is active, the workflow listens for emoji reactions added to messages in Slack request conversations. If tracking is not active, those reactions are ignored and no task is created.

For each qualifying Slack reaction received during the active absence period, the workflow turns the message and reaction context into a tracked task in Airtable. It stores the original message reference, requester details, reaction metadata, the related out-of-office session, and sets the initial task status to New.

When the user returns and opens the Retool interface, the workflow retrieves the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent out-of-office session from Airtable. It displays the current list with task identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses.

As the user works through the backlog in Retool, they can update each task's status directly in the interface. The workflow accepts only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done, and rejects any other value.

Whenever a valid task status change is submitted, the workflow updates the task in Airtable and sends a Slack notification tied to the original request so the requester is informed of the new status. It also refreshes the task display in Retool with the updated state.

The workflow can also be viewed in Canvas as a visual map of how Retool, Slack, and Airtable interact across out-of-office activation, task capture, return review, and requester notifications. This step is for visualization only and does not change workflow data or behavior.

tools.description: Post a notification message to a Slack channel for out-of-office activation or requester task status updates.
Create a new task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with the provided request, reaction, session, and initial status data.
Update an existing task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with a new status and related task details.
Send accumulated task lists or updated task state back to the Retool app UI for display.

name: Out-of-Office Task Tracking System

objects.role: Dual-role service object for the Retool app that receives out-of-office status changes and return-session task updates, and presents task data to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Retool events such as out-of-office activation, return access, and task status updates initiated in the app; does not store authoritative task records.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event arrives, treat the submitted absence context as the workflow trigger. When a return-session task status update event arrives, treat the submitted task identifier and new status as a user action to be processed. When a return access event arrives, treat it as a request to load current tasks for display. When out-of-office activation, return access, or task status update arrives, forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Retool out-of-office activation, return access, and task status update events when submitted in the app

event_sources: retool app: out-of-office activation submitted
retool app: return access requested
retool app: task status update submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Slack emoji reaction events from messages in #revops-requests during an active out-of-office period.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Slack reaction events received from #revops-requests.
objects.behavior: When a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, capture the message reference, reactor, emoji, channel, and requester context included in the event and forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction events from #revops-requests for task creation during out-of-office periods

event_sources: slack: reaction added in #revops-requests

objects.role: Read service object for the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table, providing task lookup and retrieval for current out-of-office tracking data.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime state; task data is retrieved on demand from the Airtable Tasks table.
objects.behavior: When asked for tasks by status, assignee, message reference, requester, or out-of-office session, return matching records from the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked for all accumulated tasks for return review, return the current task list with identifiers, source message references, requester details, reaction metadata, and statuses. When asked for a specific task, return the matching task record. When answering queries, call the `airtable-tasks_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object for creating and updating task records in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of task create and task update write requests sent to Airtable.
objects.behavior: When asked to create a task, record the provided message reference, requester, reaction metadata, out-of-office session context, and initial status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked to update a task, record the provided task identifier and new status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.

objects.role: Write service object for posting Slack notifications to #revops-team and #revops-requests.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound Slack messages posted by the automation.
objects.behavior: When asked to announce out-of-office activation, post to the #revops-team channel with the absence notice and task-tracking activation context. When asked to notify a requester of task status, post to the #revops-requests channel with the original request reference, updated status, and requester-facing context.

objects.role: Write service object for sending task lists and task-update results back to the Retool app UI for display.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound UI payloads sent to Retool for rendering task lists and status refreshes.
objects.behavior: When asked to display accumulated tasks, send the current task list with identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses to the Retool app UI. When asked to refresh a task after status change, send the updated task state to the Retool app UI.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies the rules for activating out-of-office tracking, converting Slack reactions into tracked tasks, and handling return-time task review and status updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks whether out-of-office tracking is active, the current absence session context, and correlations between Slack request messages, Airtable task records, and requester notification state.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event is received, mark tracking active for the submitted absence session and prepare notifications and task capture for that session. When tracking is active and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, interpret the reaction as a task/request to capture while the user is away, derive a task record from the message and reaction context, and assign initial status New. When tracking is inactive and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives, ignore it for task creation. When a return access event is received, retrieve the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent absence session and prepare them for display in Retool. When a task status update event is received, allow only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done; reject any other status. When a valid task status update is received, prepare the requester notification context tied to the original Slack request. Send to slack-notifications and airtable-task-writes for out-of-office activation. Send to airtable-task-writes when a new task should be created from a Slack reaction. Send to airtable-tasks and retool-task-view when return access requires task display. Send to airtable-task-writes, airtable-tasks, slack-notifications, and retool-task-view when a valid task status update is submitted.

peers.relationship: Send out-of-office announcement requests for #revops-team and requester status notifications for #revops-requests
Send task create requests for captured Slack reactions and task update requests for status changes
Request current task records for return review and task refresh context
Send task lists and refreshed task state for display in the Retool app

steps: When a user marks themselves out of office in the Retool interface, the workflow starts an absence-tracking session using the submitted absence context. It then sends a Slack announcement to the team and enables capture of incoming requests for that out-of-office period.

While that out-of-office session is active, the workflow listens for emoji reactions added to messages in Slack request conversations. If tracking is not active, those reactions are ignored and no task is created.

For each qualifying Slack reaction received during the active absence period, the workflow turns the message and reaction context into a tracked task in Airtable. It stores the original message reference, requester details, reaction metadata, the related out-of-office session, and sets the initial task status to New.

When the user returns and opens the Retool interface, the workflow retrieves the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent out-of-office session from Airtable. It displays the current list with task identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses.

As the user works through the backlog in Retool, they can update each task's status directly in the interface. The workflow accepts only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done, and rejects any other value.

Whenever a valid task status change is submitted, the workflow updates the task in Airtable and sends a Slack notification tied to the original request so the requester is informed of the new status. It also refreshes the task display in Retool with the updated state.

The workflow can also be viewed in Canvas as a visual map of how Retool, Slack, and Airtable interact across out-of-office activation, task capture, return review, and requester notifications. This step is for visualization only and does not change workflow data or behavior.

tools.description: Post a notification message to a Slack channel for out-of-office activation or requester task status updates.
Create a new task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with the provided request, reaction, session, and initial status data.
Update an existing task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with a new status and related task details.
Send accumulated task lists or updated task state back to the Retool app UI for display.

name: Out-of-Office Task Tracking System

objects.role: Dual-role service object for the Retool app that receives out-of-office status changes and return-session task updates, and presents task data to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Retool events such as out-of-office activation, return access, and task status updates initiated in the app; does not store authoritative task records.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event arrives, treat the submitted absence context as the workflow trigger. When a return-session task status update event arrives, treat the submitted task identifier and new status as a user action to be processed. When a return access event arrives, treat it as a request to load current tasks for display. When out-of-office activation, return access, or task status update arrives, forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Retool out-of-office activation, return access, and task status update events when submitted in the app

event_sources: retool app: out-of-office activation submitted
retool app: return access requested
retool app: task status update submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Slack emoji reaction events from messages in #revops-requests during an active out-of-office period.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Slack reaction events received from #revops-requests.
objects.behavior: When a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, capture the message reference, reactor, emoji, channel, and requester context included in the event and forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction events from #revops-requests for task creation during out-of-office periods

event_sources: slack: reaction added in #revops-requests

objects.role: Read service object for the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table, providing task lookup and retrieval for current out-of-office tracking data.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime state; task data is retrieved on demand from the Airtable Tasks table.
objects.behavior: When asked for tasks by status, assignee, message reference, requester, or out-of-office session, return matching records from the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked for all accumulated tasks for return review, return the current task list with identifiers, source message references, requester details, reaction metadata, and statuses. When asked for a specific task, return the matching task record. When answering queries, call the `airtable-tasks_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object for creating and updating task records in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of task create and task update write requests sent to Airtable.
objects.behavior: When asked to create a task, record the provided message reference, requester, reaction metadata, out-of-office session context, and initial status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked to update a task, record the provided task identifier and new status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.

objects.role: Write service object for posting Slack notifications to #revops-team and #revops-requests.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound Slack messages posted by the automation.
objects.behavior: When asked to announce out-of-office activation, post to the #revops-team channel with the absence notice and task-tracking activation context. When asked to notify a requester of task status, post to the #revops-requests channel with the original request reference, updated status, and requester-facing context.

objects.role: Write service object for sending task lists and task-update results back to the Retool app UI for display.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound UI payloads sent to Retool for rendering task lists and status refreshes.
objects.behavior: When asked to display accumulated tasks, send the current task list with identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses to the Retool app UI. When asked to refresh a task after status change, send the updated task state to the Retool app UI.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies the rules for activating out-of-office tracking, converting Slack reactions into tracked tasks, and handling return-time task review and status updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks whether out-of-office tracking is active, the current absence session context, and correlations between Slack request messages, Airtable task records, and requester notification state.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event is received, mark tracking active for the submitted absence session and prepare notifications and task capture for that session. When tracking is active and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, interpret the reaction as a task/request to capture while the user is away, derive a task record from the message and reaction context, and assign initial status New. When tracking is inactive and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives, ignore it for task creation. When a return access event is received, retrieve the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent absence session and prepare them for display in Retool. When a task status update event is received, allow only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done; reject any other status. When a valid task status update is received, prepare the requester notification context tied to the original Slack request. Send to slack-notifications and airtable-task-writes for out-of-office activation. Send to airtable-task-writes when a new task should be created from a Slack reaction. Send to airtable-tasks and retool-task-view when return access requires task display. Send to airtable-task-writes, airtable-tasks, slack-notifications, and retool-task-view when a valid task status update is submitted.

peers.relationship: Send out-of-office announcement requests for #revops-team and requester status notifications for #revops-requests
Send task create requests for captured Slack reactions and task update requests for status changes
Request current task records for return review and task refresh context
Send task lists and refreshed task state for display in the Retool app

steps: When a user marks themselves out of office in the Retool interface, the workflow starts an absence-tracking session using the submitted absence context. It then sends a Slack announcement to the team and enables capture of incoming requests for that out-of-office period.

While that out-of-office session is active, the workflow listens for emoji reactions added to messages in Slack request conversations. If tracking is not active, those reactions are ignored and no task is created.

For each qualifying Slack reaction received during the active absence period, the workflow turns the message and reaction context into a tracked task in Airtable. It stores the original message reference, requester details, reaction metadata, the related out-of-office session, and sets the initial task status to New.

When the user returns and opens the Retool interface, the workflow retrieves the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent out-of-office session from Airtable. It displays the current list with task identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses.

As the user works through the backlog in Retool, they can update each task's status directly in the interface. The workflow accepts only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done, and rejects any other value.

Whenever a valid task status change is submitted, the workflow updates the task in Airtable and sends a Slack notification tied to the original request so the requester is informed of the new status. It also refreshes the task display in Retool with the updated state.

The workflow can also be viewed in Canvas as a visual map of how Retool, Slack, and Airtable interact across out-of-office activation, task capture, return review, and requester notifications. This step is for visualization only and does not change workflow data or behavior.

tools.description: Post a notification message to a Slack channel for out-of-office activation or requester task status updates.
Create a new task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with the provided request, reaction, session, and initial status data.
Update an existing task record in the OOO Requests Airtable Tasks table with a new status and related task details.
Send accumulated task lists or updated task state back to the Retool app UI for display.

name: Comprehensive Employee Offboarding System

objects.role: Entry-point service for new Employee Offboarding Request Google Form submissions.
objects.state_description: Stores an audit trail of received offboarding request submissions and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a new Employee Offboarding Request Google Form submission arrives, record the submission payload including employee name, termination type, and last working day.

peers.relationship: Forward each new offboarding request submission for policy evaluation and downstream action selection

event_sources: google forms: Employee Offboarding Request submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service for Exit Survey Form submissions.
objects.state_description: Stores an audit trail of received exit survey submissions and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a new Exit Survey Form submission arrives, record the survey payload and any exit interview request flag.

peers.relationship: Forward each exit survey submission for enrichment, summarization, and follow-up decisions

event_sources: google forms: Exit Survey Form submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service for farewell message submissions awaiting review.
objects.state_description: Stores an audit trail of received farewell message submissions and forwarding attempts.
objects.behavior: When a farewell message submission arrives, record the message content, submitter, target employee, and requested posting context.

peers.relationship: Forward each farewell message submission for review routing and approval handling

event_sources: form/webhook: farewell message submitted

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for outbound posts and inbound emoji or approval action events.
objects.state_description: Stores an audit trail of outbound Slack messages, DMs, channel posts, and inbound interaction events.
objects.behavior: When asked to send a message, post to the specified Slack destination including #hr-operations, #it-helpdesk, #payroll-ops, or an employee DM. When asked to send a review request, post to the specified Slack approval destination. When asked to post an approved farewell, post to the specified Slack channel. When emoji reaction event arrives, forward it to backfill-tracking via peer message. When approval action event arrives, forward it to farewell-approval via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Slack emoji reaction events related to Backfill Requests tracking updates
Forward Slack approval action events for farewell review decisions

event_sources: slack: emoji reaction added
slack: approval action submitted

objects.role: Read service for BambooHR employee and employment data lookups.
objects.state_description: Holds no persistent state; retrieves BambooHR records on demand.
objects.behavior: When asked for employee profile, tenure, work location, employment status, or current termination and severance-related fields, return the requested BambooHR data. When answering queries, call the `bamboohr_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service for generating drafted documents and summaries from provided prompts.
objects.state_description: Holds no persistent state; retrieves generated text on demand.
objects.behavior: When asked for a separation agreement draft, generate a draft using the provided termination details, severance inputs, and jurisdiction context. When asked for an exit survey TLDR and action steps, generate concise summary text and recommended actions from the enriched feedback payload. When answering queries, call the `chatgpt-generation_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for recording termination and severance-related updates into BambooHR.
objects.state_description: Stores an audit trail of outbound BambooHR update requests for termination details, severance tracking fields, and related status changes.
objects.behavior: When termination details are received, write the employee termination type and last working day to BambooHR. When severance payment details are received, write the payment tracking details and severance status to BambooHR.

objects.role: Write service for creating and tracking Backfill Requests records in the Offboarding Backfills Airtable base.
objects.state_description: Stores an audit trail of outbound Airtable record writes for Backfill Requests and subsequent status updates.
objects.behavior: When a backfill request is received, create a tracking record in the Offboarding Backfills Airtable base, Backfill Requests table. When a backfill status update is received, update the corresponding Backfill Requests record with the new emoji-derived status.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies offboarding rules to each termination request.
objects.state_description: Tracks active offboarding cases with employee identity, termination type, last working day, notification status, BambooHR update status, voluntary or involuntary branch, and linked downstream tasks.
objects.behavior: When a new offboarding request arrives, classify it as voluntary or involuntary using the submitted termination type. When any offboarding request is accepted, prepare customized action messages and send to #hr-operations, #it-helpdesk, and #payroll-ops. When any offboarding request is accepted, prepare a termination update for BambooHR. When the departure is voluntary, prepare a Slack DM to the employee with the Employee Offboarding Guide and Exit Survey Form link. When the departure is involuntary, request employee tenure and work location data needed for severance evaluation. When any offboarding request is accepted, prepare a backfill tracking request for Talent Acquisition.

peers.relationship: Send customized offboarding action messages to #hr-operations, #it-helpdesk, and #payroll-ops, and send voluntary-departure DM content to the employee
Send termination details for BambooHR recording
Send involuntary termination cases for severance evaluation
Send accepted offboarding cases to create and manage backfill tracking

objects.role: Business logic object that applies severance rules and prepares separation-agreement inputs.
objects.state_description: Tracks involuntary termination cases with tenure, work location, severance eligibility, severance amount, agreement draft status, and payment tracking requirements.
objects.behavior: When an involuntary termination case arrives, use tenure and work location to determine severance eligibility and calculate severance according to policy. When severance is due, prepare structured inputs for a separation agreement draft. When severance is due, prepare severance tracking details for BambooHR. When severance is not due, mark the case as no-severance-required.

peers.relationship: Request employee tenure, work location, and related employment facts needed for severance calculation
Request a separation agreement draft for eligible severance cases
Send severance payment tracking details and severance status for BambooHR recording

objects.role: Business logic object that enriches exit survey responses and determines follow-up actions.
objects.state_description: Tracks exit survey cases with raw responses, enriched employee context, generated TLDR, recommended action steps, exit interview request status, and notification status.
objects.behavior: When an exit survey submission arrives, enrich the feedback with BambooHR employee data. When enriched feedback is available, prepare a TLDR and action steps. When the survey indicates an exit interview request, identify that follow-up is required and prepare a notification for the assigned People Business Partner.

peers.relationship: Request BambooHR context needed to enrich exit survey feedback
Request TLDR and action-step generation from enriched exit feedback
Send exit interview request notifications for the assigned People Business Partner

objects.role: Business logic object that manages backfill request creation and Slack-emoji-driven status interpretation.
objects.state_description: Tracks backfill cases with employee departure context, Airtable record linkage, Talent Acquisition status, and most recent Slack emoji-derived signal.
objects.behavior: When a new offboarding case arrives, prepare a Backfill Requests record with employee and vacancy context. When a Slack emoji reaction event arrives for a tracked backfill item, interpret the emoji according to backfill status rules and prepare the corresponding Airtable status update.

peers.relationship: Send new Backfill Requests records and emoji-derived status updates to Airtable

objects.role: Business logic object that applies review and approval rules to farewell messages before posting.
objects.state_description: Tracks farewell message submissions with review status, approver routing, decision history, approved content, and posting status.
objects.behavior: When a farewell message submission arrives, determine the required review path and prepare a review request. When an approval action arrives, update the decision status. When a farewell message is approved, prepare the approved message for posting to Slack. When a farewell message is rejected or needs edits, mark the submission accordingly and retain the review history.

peers.relationship: Send farewell review requests to the Slack approval destination and send approved farewell messages for posting to the designated Slack channel

objects.role: Write service for notifying the assigned People Business Partner about requested exit interviews.
objects.state_description: Stores an audit trail of outbound People Business Partner notifications and delivery attempts.
objects.behavior: When an exit interview request notification is received, send the notification to the assigned People Business Partner with employee context and requested follow-up.

steps: When an employee offboarding request is submitted through Google Forms, the workflow records the employee name, termination type, and last working day, then classifies the departure as voluntary or involuntary based on the submitted termination type.

For every accepted offboarding request, the workflow immediately sends customized Slack notifications to the relevant operational teams, including HR, IT, and payroll, with team-specific action guidance tied to the departure details.
After intake, the workflow writes the termination information into BambooHR, updating the employee’s termination type and last working day.

If the termination is involuntary, the workflow looks up the employee’s tenure and work location in BambooHR, applies severance policy to determine eligibility and calculate severance, and, when severance is due, uses AI to draft a separation agreement using the termination details, severance inputs, and jurisdiction context.
If the departure is voluntary, the workflow sends the employee a direct message in Slack with an offboarding guide and a link to the exit survey.

When an exit survey is submitted through the survey form, the workflow enriches the response with employee data from BambooHR and then uses AI to generate a concise summary of the feedback along with recommended action steps.
If the exit survey indicates that the employee wants an exit interview, the workflow notifies the assigned People Business Partner with the employee context and requested follow-up.

For each offboarding case, the workflow creates a backfill tracking record in Airtable for Talent Acquisition, and later interprets Slack emoji reactions on the tracked item to update the backfill status according to predefined status rules.

If the involuntary termination results in severance, the workflow prepares severance payment tracking details and updates BambooHR with the payment information and severance status; if no severance is due, it marks the case accordingly.

When a farewell message is submitted, the workflow routes it for review in Slack according to the required approval path, records approval or rejection decisions, and only posts the message to Slack after it has been approved; rejected or edit-requested messages are retained with their review history instead of being posted.

modifications.intent: For farewell notes headed to #company-announcements, be a little more careful about the review path before anything goes out.
