name: HR Help Desk & Knowledge Loop

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound employee questions posted in #hr-help and outbound bot replies to that same channel or thread.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received #hr-help message events and outbound reply payloads sent to Slack; does not store HR knowledge itself.
objects.behavior: When a new message event arrives in the #hr-help channel, capture the message text, urgency marker, user, channel, thread context, and timestamp. When a new message event arrives, forward it to hr-help-triage via peer message. When asked to write a response, post to the #hr-help channel.

peers.relationship: Forward employee HR question events from #hr-help when a new message arrives

event_sources: slack: message posted in #hr-help

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound resolution reactions indicating a People Operations specialist has marked a conversation resolved.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received :white_check_mark: reaction events associated with Slack conversations.
objects.behavior: When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives on a relevant Slack conversation, capture the reacting user, channel, thread or message reference, and timestamp. When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives, forward it to knowledge-capture via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward resolution reaction events when a People Operations specialist marks a conversation resolved

event_sources: slack: :white_check_mark: reaction added to conversation

objects.role: Scheduler service that emits the weekly reporting trigger every Monday at 09:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Tracks emitted schedule events for audit purposes.
objects.behavior: When the scheduled time Monday 09:00 UTC occurs, emit a weekly reporting event and forward it to topic-analytics via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly reporting trigger every Monday at 09:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: monday 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service representing the HR Knowledge Base Notion database for retrieving prior answers, policies, and logged interactions.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime data; retrieves HR knowledge base records on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for relevant HR answers, return matching knowledge base entries, prior resolved interactions, and policy snippets. When asked whether a resolved answer already exists, return the closest matching existing entries and similarity indicators. When asked for historical interactions for reporting, return logged question and topic records for the requested time window. When answering queries, call the `hr-knowledge-base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for recording resolved HR interactions and adding new knowledge entries into the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of interaction logs and knowledge entries written to Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked to log a resolved interaction, write the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database. When asked to add a new answer, write a new knowledge entry with topic, question pattern, answer, source interaction reference, and review metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.

objects.role: Write service for posting escalation notifications to the People Operations Slack channel.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of escalation notifications posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to notify People Operations about an escalated or high-priority issue, post to the #people-ops-escalations channel with the question, urgency, employee context, confidence or sensitivity rationale, and thread reference.

objects.role: Write service for posting weekly HR topic reports to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of weekly report messages posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to publish the weekly HR question report, post to the #hr-ops-weekly channel with top topics, counts, notable trends, and summary observations.

objects.role: Business logic object that interprets employee HR questions, applies confidence and sensitivity rules, and decides whether to answer directly or escalate.
objects.state_description: Tracks active HR help conversations with extracted topic, urgency, confidence estimate, sensitivity flags, complexity flags, proposed answer, and escalation decision.
objects.behavior: When an employee HR question arrives, extract the topic, urgency marker if present, and any indicators of sensitivity or complexity. When a question concerns compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, or ambiguous policy interpretation, mark it sensitive or complex. When relevant knowledge is available, draft an answer grounded only in internal HR policies and retrieved knowledge. When confidence in the drafted answer is 80% or higher and the question is not sensitive or complex, prepare an instant response. When confidence is below 80%, or the question is sensitive or complex, escalate the case to People Operations. When urgency is High, treat the issue as high priority in the escalation payload even if an instant response is also available. When a direct answer is appropriate, send the response to slack-hr-questions. When escalation is required, send the escalation notice to slack-people-ops-escalations. When both an employee-facing acknowledgement and an escalation are appropriate, send to slack-hr-questions and slack-people-ops-escalations.

peers.relationship: Query for relevant HR policies, prior answers, and similar resolved interactions when a new HR question arrives
Send instant HR answers or escalation acknowledgements for employee questions in #hr-help
Send escalated or high-priority issue notifications when confidence is low or the issue is sensitive or complex

objects.role: Business logic object that determines what resolved Slack interactions should be logged and whether new knowledge should be added to the HR Knowledge Base.
objects.state_description: Tracks resolved conversation candidates with Slack references, extracted question-answer pair, resolver identity, deduplication result, and logging or knowledge-add decisions.
objects.behavior: When a resolution reaction arrives, identify the associated conversation and extract the employee question, the final People Operations answer, urgency, and resolution context. When the resolved interaction lacks a usable final answer, do not create a new knowledge entry and log only the resolution metadata if available. When a resolved interaction contains a substantive answer, prepare an interaction log entry. When the final answer is materially new compared with existing HR Knowledge Base content, mark it as a new knowledge candidate. When the answer already exists or is substantially duplicative, do not add a new knowledge entry. When a resolved interaction is ready to be recorded, send the interaction log to notion-hr-kb-writer. When a materially new answer is identified, send the new knowledge entry to notion-hr-kb-writer.

peers.relationship: Query existing HR knowledge to check whether the resolved answer is already present
Write resolved interaction logs and new knowledge entries after evaluation

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes historical HR questions to identify top recurring topics and generate the weekly report.
objects.state_description: Tracks reporting windows, aggregated topic counts, trend summaries, and the latest generated weekly report payload.
objects.behavior: When the weekly reporting trigger arrives, retrieve resolved and logged HR interactions for the reporting window. When interaction history is retrieved, group questions by topic, count frequency, identify the most common themes, and summarize notable trends. When the report is prepared, send it to slack-weekly-reports.

peers.relationship: Query historical logged HR interactions for the weekly reporting window
Send the weekly topic report for publication in #hr-ops-weekly

steps: When an employee posts an HR question in Slack, the workflow captures the message text, who asked it, when and where it was posted, any thread context, and any urgency signal included in the message such as a priority label or emoji.
The workflow searches the HR knowledge base in Notion for relevant policy content, prior resolved interactions, and matching answer entries that can help address the employee’s question.

Using only retrieved internal HR policies and knowledge base content, the workflow drafts and posts an immediate reply in Slack when it has enough relevant information to answer the question directly.

If the drafted answer has less than 80% confidence, or if the question is identified as sensitive or complex, the workflow escalates it to the HR team instead of relying only on the bot response; sensitive or complex topics include compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, and ambiguous policy interpretation. If the employee marked the issue as high urgency, the escalation is treated as high priority even when an immediate acknowledgement or partial answer is also sent in Slack.

For any escalated or high-priority case, the workflow sends a notification in Slack to the HR or People Operations team with the employee’s question, urgency level, relevant employee context, the reason for escalation such as low confidence or sensitivity, and a reference to the original conversation.

When an HR or People Operations specialist marks the Slack conversation as resolved using a check-mark reaction, the workflow identifies the related conversation and records the resolved interaction in Notion, including the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution details.

If the resolved interaction contains a substantive final answer that is materially new compared with existing Notion knowledge base content, the workflow adds it as a new knowledge entry; if the answer is missing, unusable, or substantially duplicative of existing content, no new knowledge entry is created.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow reviews logged HR interactions from the reporting period, groups them by topic, counts the most frequent question themes, summarizes notable trends, and posts the resulting report to Slack for HR operations review.

modifications.intent: For Week 2 only, do not emit the weekly reporting trigger on Monday at 09:00 UTC. Instead, emit it on Tuesday at 09:00 UTC, and then resume the normal Monday 09:00 UTC schedule afterward.

events.input: A new Slack message is posted in `#hr-help` by James Brown: "High: I'm relocating to Germany next month. What is the company policy on visa support, payroll changes, and tax handling for an international move?" Channel: `#hr-help`. Thread: none. Timestamp: `2026-05-18T14:22:00Z`.

expect.action: A Slack reply is posted in James Brown’s new #hr-help conversation acknowledging the request and giving policy-based guidance that the company can support visa documentation for a business-approved international relocation subject to eligibility and required legal review, that payroll changes are coordinated for the new work location and pay cycle, and that cross-border tax handling requires coordination rather than personal tax advice. Because the request is high urgency and involves sensitive or complex international relocation, a high-priority Slack notification is also sent to the People Operations team with James Brown’s question, urgency level, employee context, the escalation reason, and a reference to the original #hr-help conversation.
expect.reason: The Slack question is high priority and covers complex international relocation topics with legal and tax implications, so the workflow may send an immediate policy-based reply from the HR knowledge base but must also escalate the case to HR/People Operations as high priority in Slack.

events.input: A `:white_check_mark:` reaction is added in Slack by Priya Shah, People Operations Specialist, to the resolved HR help conversation in channel `#hr-help`, thread started by James Brown's message at `2026-05-18T14:22:00Z`. Reaction timestamp: `2026-05-18T16:05:00Z`.

expect.action: The resolved #hr-help conversation for James Brown’s relocation question is recorded in Notion as a resolved HR interaction, including the question, the final answer in the thread, participants James Brown and Priya Shah, timestamps, high urgency, escalated status, and resolution details tied to the original Slack thread at 2026-05-18T14:22:00Z. No new Notion knowledge-base entry is created from this resolution because the final answer is materially duplicative of existing Notion content about relocation to Germany, visa support, payroll changes, and tax handling.
expect.reason: A white-check-mark reaction from the People Operations specialist marks the Slack conversation resolved, which triggers logging of the resolved interaction in Notion and a duplicate check before creating any new knowledge entry.

events.input: It is Monday at 09:00 UTC, and the weekly reporting schedule fires for the HR help desk workflow.

expect.reason: When the weekly reporting schedule fires, the workflow compiles the reporting window’s logged HR interactions into a topic-based summary and posts the report to Slack.

name: HR Help Desk & Knowledge Loop

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound employee questions posted in #hr-help and outbound bot replies to that same channel or thread.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received #hr-help message events and outbound reply payloads sent to Slack; does not store HR knowledge itself.
objects.behavior: When a new message event arrives in the #hr-help channel, capture the message text, urgency marker, user, channel, thread context, and timestamp. When a new message event arrives, forward it to hr-help-triage via peer message. When asked to write a response, post to the #hr-help channel.

peers.relationship: Forward employee HR question events from #hr-help when a new message arrives

event_sources: slack: message posted in #hr-help

objects.behavior: When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives on a relevant Slack conversation, capture the reacting user, channel, thread or message reference, and timestamp. When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives, forward it to knowledge-capture via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward resolution reaction events when a People Operations specialist marks a conversation resolved

event_sources: slack: :white_check_mark: reaction added to conversation

objects.behavior: When the scheduled time Monday 09:00 UTC occurs, emit a weekly reporting event and forward it to topic-analytics via peer message.

objects.role: Read service representing the HR Knowledge Base Notion database for retrieving prior answers, policies, and logged interactions.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime data; retrieves HR knowledge base records on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for relevant HR answers, return matching knowledge base entries, prior resolved interactions, and policy snippets. When asked whether a resolved answer already exists, return the closest matching existing entries and similarity indicators. When asked for historical interactions for reporting, return logged question and topic records for the requested time window. When answering queries, call the `hr-knowledge-base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for recording resolved HR interactions and adding new knowledge entries into the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of interaction logs and knowledge entries written to Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked to log a resolved interaction, write the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database. When asked to add a new answer, write a new knowledge entry with topic, question pattern, answer, source interaction reference, and review metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.

objects.role: Write service for posting escalation notifications to the People Operations Slack channel.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of escalation notifications posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to notify People Operations about an escalated or high-priority issue, post to the #people-ops-escalations channel with the question, urgency, employee context, confidence or sensitivity rationale, and thread reference.

objects.role: Write service for posting weekly HR topic reports to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of weekly report messages posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to publish the weekly HR question report, post to the #hr-ops-weekly channel with top topics, counts, notable trends, and summary observations.

objects.role: Business logic object that interprets employee HR questions, applies confidence and sensitivity rules, and decides whether to answer directly or escalate.
objects.state_description: Tracks active HR help conversations with extracted topic, urgency, confidence estimate, sensitivity flags, complexity flags, proposed answer, and escalation decision.
objects.behavior: When an employee HR question arrives, extract the topic, urgency marker if present, and any indicators of sensitivity or complexity. When a question concerns compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, or ambiguous policy interpretation, mark it sensitive or complex. When relevant knowledge is available, draft an answer grounded only in internal HR policies and retrieved knowledge. When confidence in the drafted answer is 80% or higher and the question is not sensitive or complex, prepare an instant response. When confidence is below 80%, or the question is sensitive or complex, escalate the case to People Operations. When urgency is High, treat the issue as high priority in the escalation payload even if an instant response is also available. When a direct answer is appropriate, send the response to slack-hr-questions. When escalation is required, send the escalation notice to slack-people-ops-escalations. When both an employee-facing acknowledgement and an escalation are appropriate, send to slack-hr-questions and slack-people-ops-escalations.

peers.relationship: Query for relevant HR policies, prior answers, and similar resolved interactions when a new HR question arrives
Send instant HR answers or escalation acknowledgements for employee questions in #hr-help
Send escalated or high-priority issue notifications when confidence is low or the issue is sensitive or complex

objects.role: Business logic object that determines what resolved Slack interactions should be logged and whether new knowledge should be added to the HR Knowledge Base.
objects.state_description: Tracks resolved conversation candidates with Slack references, extracted question-answer pair, resolver identity, deduplication result, and logging or knowledge-add decisions.
objects.behavior: When a resolution reaction arrives, identify the associated conversation and extract the employee question, the final People Operations answer, urgency, and resolution context. When the resolved interaction lacks a usable final answer, do not create a new knowledge entry and log only the resolution metadata if available. When a resolved interaction contains a substantive answer, prepare an interaction log entry. When the final answer is materially new compared with existing HR Knowledge Base content, mark it as a new knowledge candidate. When the answer already exists or is substantially duplicative, do not add a new knowledge entry. When a resolved interaction is ready to be recorded, send the interaction log to notion-hr-kb-writer. When a materially new answer is identified, send the new knowledge entry to notion-hr-kb-writer.

peers.relationship: Query existing HR knowledge to check whether the resolved answer is already present
Write resolved interaction logs and new knowledge entries after evaluation

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes historical HR questions to identify top recurring topics and generate the weekly report.
objects.state_description: Tracks reporting windows, aggregated topic counts, trend summaries, and the latest generated weekly report payload.
objects.behavior: When the weekly reporting trigger arrives, retrieve resolved and logged HR interactions for the reporting window. When interaction history is retrieved, group questions by topic, count frequency, identify the most common themes, and summarize notable trends. When the report is prepared, send it to slack-weekly-reports.

peers.relationship: Query historical logged HR interactions for the weekly reporting window
Send the weekly topic report for publication in #hr-ops-weekly

steps: When an employee posts an HR question in Slack, the workflow captures the message text, who asked it, when and where it was posted, any thread context, and any urgency signal included in the message such as a priority label or emoji.
The workflow searches the HR knowledge base in Notion for relevant policy content, prior resolved interactions, and matching answer entries that can help address the employee’s question.

Using only retrieved internal HR policies and knowledge base content, the workflow drafts and posts an immediate reply in Slack when it has enough relevant information to answer the question directly.

If the drafted answer has less than 80% confidence, or if the question is identified as sensitive or complex, the workflow escalates it to the HR team instead of relying only on the bot response; sensitive or complex topics include compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, and ambiguous policy interpretation. If the employee marked the issue as high urgency, the escalation is treated as high priority even when an immediate acknowledgement or partial answer is also sent in Slack.

For any escalated or high-priority case, the workflow sends a notification in Slack to the HR or People Operations team with the employee’s question, urgency level, relevant employee context, the reason for escalation such as low confidence or sensitivity, and a reference to the original conversation.

When an HR or People Operations specialist marks the Slack conversation as resolved using a check-mark reaction, the workflow identifies the related conversation and records the resolved interaction in Notion, including the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution details.

If the resolved interaction contains a substantive final answer that is materially new compared with existing Notion knowledge base content, the workflow adds it as a new knowledge entry; if the answer is missing, unusable, or substantially duplicative of existing content, no new knowledge entry is created.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow reviews logged HR interactions from the reporting period, groups them by topic, counts the most frequent question themes, summarizes notable trends, and posts the resulting report to Slack for HR operations review.

modifications.intent: That weekly HR topics post is landing a day early for how we close things out — have it go Tuesday at 09:00 UTC instead.

events.input: A new Slack message is posted in `#hr-help` by James Brown: "High: I'm relocating to Germany next month. What is the company policy on visa support, payroll changes, and tax handling for an international move?" Channel: `#hr-help`. Thread: none. Timestamp: `2026-05-18T14:22:00Z`.

expect.action: A Slack reply is posted in #hr-help to James Brown acknowledging the international relocation question and providing policy-based guidance that visa support can be coordinated by People Operations subject to business approval and work authorization requirements, that payroll changes must be coordinated for the Germany work location change, and that tax handling requires cross-border review rather than personal tax advice. A high-priority escalation notification is also posted in Slack to the People Operations team with James Brown’s question, High urgency, his employee context as a full-time Engineering employee in the United States, the reason for escalation that the topic is sensitive/complex international relocation involving visa and tax matters, and a reference to the original #hr-help conversation.
expect.reason: Slack HR questions that involve sensitive or complex topics such as immigration and tax must be escalated to HR, and a High urgency marker makes the escalation high priority even if an immediate Slack acknowledgement or partial answer is also sent.

events.input: A `:white_check_mark:` reaction is added in Slack by Priya Shah, People Operations Specialist, to the resolved HR help conversation in channel `#hr-help`, thread started by James Brown's message at `2026-05-18T14:22:00Z`. Reaction timestamp: `2026-05-18T16:05:00Z`.

expect.action: The resolved HR help interaction for James Brown’s #hr-help thread is recorded in Notion as a resolved case with the question, the final answer provided in Slack, participants James Brown and Priya Shah, timestamps including the original post at 2026-05-18T14:22:00Z and resolution at 2026-05-18T16:05:00Z, High urgency, escalated status, and resolution details. No new Notion knowledge-base entry is created from this resolution because materially matching guidance already exists in Notion for international relocation to Germany covering visa support, payroll changes, and tax handling.
expect.reason: A white-check-mark resolution reaction causes the workflow to log the resolved interaction in Notion, and a new knowledge entry is only added when the final answer is materially new rather than duplicative of existing HR knowledge-base content.

name: HR Help Desk & Knowledge Loop

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound employee questions posted in #hr-help and outbound bot replies to that same channel or thread.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received #hr-help message events and outbound reply payloads sent to Slack; does not store HR knowledge itself.
objects.behavior: When a new message event arrives in the #hr-help channel, capture the message text, urgency marker, user, channel, thread context, and timestamp. When a new message event arrives, forward it to hr-help-triage via peer message. When asked to write a response, post to the #hr-help channel.

peers.relationship: Forward employee HR question events from #hr-help when a new message arrives

event_sources: slack: message posted in #hr-help

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound resolution reactions indicating a People Operations specialist has marked a conversation resolved.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received :white_check_mark: reaction events associated with Slack conversations.
objects.behavior: When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives on a relevant Slack conversation, capture the reacting user, channel, thread or message reference, and timestamp. When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives, forward it to knowledge-capture via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward resolution reaction events when a People Operations specialist marks a conversation resolved

event_sources: slack: :white_check_mark: reaction added to conversation

objects.role: Scheduler service that emits the weekly reporting trigger every Monday at 09:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Tracks emitted schedule events for audit purposes.
objects.behavior: When the scheduled time Monday 09:00 UTC occurs, emit a weekly reporting event and forward it to topic-analytics via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly reporting trigger every Monday at 09:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: monday 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service representing the HR Knowledge Base Notion database for retrieving prior answers, policies, and logged interactions.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime data; retrieves HR knowledge base records on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for relevant HR answers, return matching knowledge base entries, prior resolved interactions, and policy snippets. When asked whether a resolved answer already exists, return the closest matching existing entries and similarity indicators. When asked for historical interactions for reporting, return logged question and topic records for the requested time window. When answering queries, call the `hr-knowledge-base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for recording resolved HR interactions and adding new knowledge entries into the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of interaction logs and knowledge entries written to Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked to log a resolved interaction, write the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database. When asked to add a new answer, write a new knowledge entry with topic, question pattern, answer, source interaction reference, and review metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.

objects.role: Write service for posting escalation notifications to the People Operations Slack channel.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of escalation notifications posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to notify People Operations about an escalated or high-priority issue, post to the #people-ops-escalations channel with the question, urgency, employee context, confidence or sensitivity rationale, and thread reference.

objects.role: Write service for posting weekly HR topic reports to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of weekly report messages posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to publish the weekly HR question report, post to the #hr-ops-weekly channel with top topics, counts, notable trends, and summary observations.

objects.role: Business logic object that interprets employee HR questions, applies confidence and sensitivity rules, and decides whether to answer directly or escalate.
objects.state_description: Tracks active HR help conversations with extracted topic, urgency, confidence estimate, sensitivity flags, complexity flags, proposed answer, and escalation decision.
objects.behavior: When an employee HR question arrives, extract the topic, urgency marker if present, and any indicators of sensitivity or complexity. When a question concerns compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, or ambiguous policy interpretation, mark it sensitive or complex. When relevant knowledge is available, draft an answer grounded only in internal HR policies and retrieved knowledge. When confidence in the drafted answer is 80% or higher and the question is not sensitive or complex, prepare an instant response. When confidence is below 80%, or the question is sensitive or complex, escalate the case to People Operations. When urgency is High, treat the issue as high priority in the escalation payload even if an instant response is also available. When a direct answer is appropriate, send the response to slack-hr-questions. When escalation is required, send the escalation notice to slack-people-ops-escalations. When both an employee-facing acknowledgement and an escalation are appropriate, send to slack-hr-questions and slack-people-ops-escalations.

peers.relationship: Query for relevant HR policies, prior answers, and similar resolved interactions when a new HR question arrives
Send instant HR answers or escalation acknowledgements for employee questions in #hr-help
Send escalated or high-priority issue notifications when confidence is low or the issue is sensitive or complex

objects.role: Business logic object that determines what resolved Slack interactions should be logged and whether new knowledge should be added to the HR Knowledge Base.
objects.state_description: Tracks resolved conversation candidates with Slack references, extracted question-answer pair, resolver identity, deduplication result, and logging or knowledge-add decisions.
objects.behavior: When a resolution reaction arrives, identify the associated conversation and extract the employee question, the final People Operations answer, urgency, and resolution context. When the resolved interaction lacks a usable final answer, do not create a new knowledge entry and log only the resolution metadata if available. When a resolved interaction contains a substantive answer, prepare an interaction log entry. When the final answer is materially new compared with existing HR Knowledge Base content, mark it as a new knowledge candidate. When the answer already exists or is substantially duplicative, do not add a new knowledge entry. When a resolved interaction is ready to be recorded, send the interaction log to notion-hr-kb-writer. When a materially new answer is identified, send the new knowledge entry to notion-hr-kb-writer.

peers.relationship: Query existing HR knowledge to check whether the resolved answer is already present
Write resolved interaction logs and new knowledge entries after evaluation

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes historical HR questions to identify top recurring topics and generate the weekly report.
objects.state_description: Tracks reporting windows, aggregated topic counts, trend summaries, and the latest generated weekly report payload.
objects.behavior: When the weekly reporting trigger arrives, retrieve resolved and logged HR interactions for the reporting window. When interaction history is retrieved, group questions by topic, count frequency, identify the most common themes, and summarize notable trends. When the report is prepared, send it to slack-weekly-reports.

peers.relationship: Query historical logged HR interactions for the weekly reporting window
Send the weekly topic report for publication in #hr-ops-weekly

steps: When an employee posts an HR question in Slack, the workflow captures the message text, who asked it, when and where it was posted, any thread context, and any urgency signal included in the message such as a priority label or emoji.
The workflow searches the HR knowledge base in Notion for relevant policy content, prior resolved interactions, and matching answer entries that can help address the employee’s question.

Using only retrieved internal HR policies and knowledge base content, the workflow drafts and posts an immediate reply in Slack when it has enough relevant information to answer the question directly.

If the drafted answer has less than 80% confidence, or if the question is identified as sensitive or complex, the workflow escalates it to the HR team instead of relying only on the bot response; sensitive or complex topics include compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, and ambiguous policy interpretation. If the employee marked the issue as high urgency, the escalation is treated as high priority even when an immediate acknowledgement or partial answer is also sent in Slack.

For any escalated or high-priority case, the workflow sends a notification in Slack to the HR or People Operations team with the employee’s question, urgency level, relevant employee context, the reason for escalation such as low confidence or sensitivity, and a reference to the original conversation.

When an HR or People Operations specialist marks the Slack conversation as resolved using a check-mark reaction, the workflow identifies the related conversation and records the resolved interaction in Notion, including the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution details.

If the resolved interaction contains a substantive final answer that is materially new compared with existing Notion knowledge base content, the workflow adds it as a new knowledge entry; if the answer is missing, unusable, or substantially duplicative of existing content, no new knowledge entry is created.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow reviews logged HR interactions from the reporting period, groups them by topic, counts the most frequent question themes, summarizes notable trends, and posts the resulting report to Slack for HR operations review.

modifications.intent: For any new #hr-help question marked with urgency High that is not sensitive or complex and has enough knowledge for a direct answer, keep sending the direct answer to slack-hr-questions and also send an employee-facing acknowledgement in the same Slack conversation that clearly says People Operations has been alerted because the issue is high priority.

events.input: A new Slack message is posted in `#hr-help` by James Brown: "High: I'm relocating to Germany next month. What is the company policy on visa support, payroll changes, and tax handling for an international move?" Channel: `#hr-help`. Thread: none. Timestamp: `2026-05-18T14:22:00Z`.

expect.action: A reply is posted in Slack in James Brown’s #hr-help conversation giving policy-based guidance on international relocation to Germany, including that People Operations can coordinate visa support documentation subject to approval and legal review, payroll will be updated for the Germany work location and pay cycle, and the employee should use the company’s tax guidance process rather than rely on personal tax advice. Because the question is marked High and involves international relocation, a high-priority Slack notification is also sent to the People Operations team with James Brown’s question, urgency, employee context, the escalation reason, and a reference to the original #hr-help conversation.
expect.reason: The workflow uses retrieved HR policy and prior knowledge to draft an answer, but international relocation with visa and tax handling is treated as sensitive or complex, so it escalates to People Operations; the High marker makes the escalation high priority.

events.input: A `:white_check_mark:` reaction is added in Slack by Priya Shah, People Operations Specialist, to the resolved HR help conversation in channel `#hr-help`, thread started by James Brown's message at `2026-05-18T14:22:00Z`. Reaction timestamp: `2026-05-18T16:05:00Z`.

expect.action: The resolved #hr-help conversation for James Brown’s 2026-05-18T14:22:00Z thread is recorded in Notion as a resolved HR interaction, including the question, the final answer, participants James Brown and Priya Shah, timestamps, High urgency, escalated status, and resolution details. No new Notion knowledge-base entry is created from this resolution.
expect.reason: A white-check-mark reaction from Priya Shah marks the conversation resolved, so the workflow logs the resolved interaction in Notion; the final answer is materially duplicative of existing Notion knowledge on Germany relocation, payroll, and tax handling, so no new knowledge entry is added.

events.input: It is Monday at 09:00 UTC, and the weekly reporting schedule fires for the HR help desk workflow.

expect.action: A weekly HR help desk report is posted to Slack for HR operations review summarizing the reporting period’s logged interactions, including that the most frequent theme was International relocation to Germany with 1 interaction, along with a brief trend summary based on the available reporting window data.
expect.reason: When the weekly schedule fires, the workflow groups logged HR interactions by topic, counts frequent themes, summarizes trends, and posts the report to Slack.

Channel: #hr-help. Thread: none. Thread timestamp: none. Message timestamp: 2026-05-11T10:12:00Z. Urgency marker: none. User: Olivia Chen.

expect.reason: The question is a standard policy lookup with no High urgency and no sensitive or complex topic, so the workflow answers directly in Slack from the HR knowledge base without escalation.

events.input: A new Slack message is posted in #hr-help by Marcus Hill: "High: I need to know today whether unused wellness reimbursement funds roll over into the next plan year or expire at year end." Channel: #hr-help. Thread: none. Thread timestamp: none. Message timestamp: 2026-05-12T11:05:00Z. Urgency marker: High. User: Marcus Hill.

expect.action: A direct answer is posted in Slack in Marcus Hill’s #hr-help conversation stating the wellness reimbursement rollover policy for unused funds at year end. [added by M001] The same Slack conversation also receives an employee-facing acknowledgement that clearly says People Operations has been alerted because the issue is high priority. [added by M001] A high-priority Slack notification is sent to the People Operations team with Marcus Hill’s question, urgency, employee context, and a reference to the original conversation.
expect.reason: M001 applies because this is a new #hr-help question marked High that is not sensitive or complex and has enough knowledge for a direct answer, so the workflow keeps the direct answer and additionally alerts both the employee and People Operations due to priority.

events.input: A :white_check_mark: reaction is added in Slack by Priya Shah, People Operations Specialist, to the resolved HR help conversation in channel #hr-help, thread started by employee message timestamp 2026-05-12T11:05:00Z. Reaction timestamp: 2026-05-12T15:40:00Z. Conversation context included in the reaction payload: employee Marcus Hill asked, "High: I need to know today whether unused wellness reimbursement funds roll over into the next plan year or expire at year end." Final People Operations answer in thread: "Unused wellness reimbursement funds do not roll over into the next plan year; submit eligible expenses by the stated year-end deadline." Resolver identity: Priya Shah. Urgency: High. Thread timestamp: 2026-05-12T11:05:00Z. Channel: #hr-help.

expect.action: The resolved #hr-help conversation for Marcus Hill’s 2026-05-12T11:05:00Z thread is recorded in Notion as a resolved HR interaction, including the question, final answer that unused wellness reimbursement funds do not roll over and must be submitted by the year-end deadline, participants Marcus Hill and Priya Shah, timestamps, High urgency, escalated/high-priority handling, and resolution details. A new Notion knowledge-base entry is also created for the wellness reimbursement rollover rule because the resolved final answer is substantive and usable as a standalone policy answer.
expect.reason: A white-check-mark reaction marks the conversation resolved, so the workflow logs the resolution in Notion; because the provided final answer is substantive and there is no indication it is missing, unusable, or duplicative, the workflow adds it as a new knowledge entry.

events.input: A new Slack message is posted in #hr-help by Nina Patel: "High: My manager shared details from my medical leave paperwork in a team discussion. What should I do and who can help?" Channel: #hr-help. Thread: none. Thread timestamp: none. Message timestamp: 2026-05-13T09:18:00Z. Urgency marker: High. User: Nina Patel.

expect.action: A high-priority Slack notification is sent to the People Operations team with Nina Patel’s question, urgency, relevant employee context, the escalation reason that the issue involves medical leave privacy and sensitive employee information, and a reference to the original #hr-help conversation.
expect.reason: The question concerns medical leave details and personally sensitive information, which the workflow treats as sensitive or complex, so it escalates to People Operations instead of relying only on a bot response; M001 does not apply because the issue is not non-sensitive/non-complex.

events.input: It is Monday at 09:00 UTC, and the weekly reporting schedule fires for the HR help desk workflow for the reporting window ending this week.

expect.action: A weekly HR help desk report is posted to Slack for HR operations review summarizing the reporting period’s logged interactions by topic, the most frequent question themes, and notable trends from the week’s HR help conversations.
expect.reason: The weekly reporting trigger always compiles logged HR interactions into a topic and trend summary and posts the report to Slack; [M001 condition not met: the modification targets new High #hr-help questions, not the weekly reporting schedule].

name: HR Help Desk & Knowledge Loop

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound employee questions posted in #hr-help and outbound bot replies to that same channel or thread.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received #hr-help message events and outbound reply payloads sent to Slack; does not store HR knowledge itself.
objects.behavior: When a new message event arrives in the #hr-help channel, capture the message text, urgency marker, user, channel, thread context, and timestamp. When a new message event arrives, forward it to hr-help-triage via peer message. When asked to write a response, post to the #hr-help channel.

peers.relationship: Forward employee HR question events from #hr-help when a new message arrives

event_sources: slack: message posted in #hr-help

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound resolution reactions indicating a People Operations specialist has marked a conversation resolved.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received :white_check_mark: reaction events associated with Slack conversations.
objects.behavior: When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives on a relevant Slack conversation, capture the reacting user, channel, thread or message reference, and timestamp. When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives, forward it to knowledge-capture via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward resolution reaction events when a People Operations specialist marks a conversation resolved

event_sources: slack: :white_check_mark: reaction added to conversation

objects.role: Scheduler service that emits the weekly reporting trigger every Monday at 09:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Tracks emitted schedule events for audit purposes.
objects.behavior: When the scheduled time Monday 09:00 UTC occurs, emit a weekly reporting event and forward it to topic-analytics via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly reporting trigger every Monday at 09:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: monday 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service representing the HR Knowledge Base Notion database for retrieving prior answers, policies, and logged interactions.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime data; retrieves HR knowledge base records on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for relevant HR answers, return matching knowledge base entries, prior resolved interactions, and policy snippets. When asked whether a resolved answer already exists, return the closest matching existing entries and similarity indicators. When asked for historical interactions for reporting, return logged question and topic records for the requested time window. When answering queries, call the `hr-knowledge-base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for recording resolved HR interactions and adding new knowledge entries into the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of interaction logs and knowledge entries written to Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked to log a resolved interaction, write the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database. When asked to add a new answer, write a new knowledge entry with topic, question pattern, answer, source interaction reference, and review metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.

objects.role: Write service for posting escalation notifications to the People Operations Slack channel.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of escalation notifications posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to notify People Operations about an escalated or high-priority issue, post to the #people-ops-escalations channel with the question, urgency, employee context, confidence or sensitivity rationale, and thread reference.

objects.role: Write service for posting weekly HR topic reports to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of weekly report messages posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to publish the weekly HR question report, post to the #hr-ops-weekly channel with top topics, counts, notable trends, and summary observations.

objects.role: Business logic object that interprets employee HR questions, applies confidence and sensitivity rules, and decides whether to answer directly or escalate.
objects.state_description: Tracks active HR help conversations with extracted topic, urgency, confidence estimate, sensitivity flags, complexity flags, proposed answer, and escalation decision.
objects.behavior: When an employee HR question arrives, extract the topic, urgency marker if present, and any indicators of sensitivity or complexity. When a question concerns compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, or ambiguous policy interpretation, mark it sensitive or complex. When relevant knowledge is available, draft an answer grounded only in internal HR policies and retrieved knowledge. When confidence in the drafted answer is 80% or higher and the question is not sensitive or complex, prepare an instant response. When confidence is below 80%, or the question is sensitive or complex, escalate the case to People Operations. When urgency is High, treat the issue as high priority in the escalation payload even if an instant response is also available. When a direct answer is appropriate, send the response to slack-hr-questions. When escalation is required, send the escalation notice to slack-people-ops-escalations. When both an employee-facing acknowledgement and an escalation are appropriate, send to slack-hr-questions and slack-people-ops-escalations.

peers.relationship: Query for relevant HR policies, prior answers, and similar resolved interactions when a new HR question arrives
Send instant HR answers or escalation acknowledgements for employee questions in #hr-help
Send escalated or high-priority issue notifications when confidence is low or the issue is sensitive or complex

objects.role: Business logic object that determines what resolved Slack interactions should be logged and whether new knowledge should be added to the HR Knowledge Base.
objects.state_description: Tracks resolved conversation candidates with Slack references, extracted question-answer pair, resolver identity, deduplication result, and logging or knowledge-add decisions.
objects.behavior: When a resolution reaction arrives, identify the associated conversation and extract the employee question, the final People Operations answer, urgency, and resolution context. When the resolved interaction lacks a usable final answer, do not create a new knowledge entry and log only the resolution metadata if available. When a resolved interaction contains a substantive answer, prepare an interaction log entry. When the final answer is materially new compared with existing HR Knowledge Base content, mark it as a new knowledge candidate. When the answer already exists or is substantially duplicative, do not add a new knowledge entry. When a resolved interaction is ready to be recorded, send the interaction log to notion-hr-kb-writer. When a materially new answer is identified, send the new knowledge entry to notion-hr-kb-writer.

peers.relationship: Query existing HR knowledge to check whether the resolved answer is already present
Write resolved interaction logs and new knowledge entries after evaluation

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes historical HR questions to identify top recurring topics and generate the weekly report.
objects.state_description: Tracks reporting windows, aggregated topic counts, trend summaries, and the latest generated weekly report payload.
objects.behavior: When the weekly reporting trigger arrives, retrieve resolved and logged HR interactions for the reporting window. When interaction history is retrieved, group questions by topic, count frequency, identify the most common themes, and summarize notable trends. When the report is prepared, send it to slack-weekly-reports.

peers.relationship: Query historical logged HR interactions for the weekly reporting window
Send the weekly topic report for publication in #hr-ops-weekly

steps: When an employee posts an HR question in Slack, the workflow captures the message text, who asked it, when and where it was posted, any thread context, and any urgency signal included in the message such as a priority label or emoji.
The workflow searches the HR knowledge base in Notion for relevant policy content, prior resolved interactions, and matching answer entries that can help address the employee’s question.

Using only retrieved internal HR policies and knowledge base content, the workflow drafts and posts an immediate reply in Slack when it has enough relevant information to answer the question directly.

If the drafted answer has less than 80% confidence, or if the question is identified as sensitive or complex, the workflow escalates it to the HR team instead of relying only on the bot response; sensitive or complex topics include compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, and ambiguous policy interpretation. If the employee marked the issue as high urgency, the escalation is treated as high priority even when an immediate acknowledgement or partial answer is also sent in Slack.

For any escalated or high-priority case, the workflow sends a notification in Slack to the HR or People Operations team with the employee’s question, urgency level, relevant employee context, the reason for escalation such as low confidence or sensitivity, and a reference to the original conversation.

When an HR or People Operations specialist marks the Slack conversation as resolved using a check-mark reaction, the workflow identifies the related conversation and records the resolved interaction in Notion, including the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution details.

If the resolved interaction contains a substantive final answer that is materially new compared with existing Notion knowledge base content, the workflow adds it as a new knowledge entry; if the answer is missing, unusable, or substantially duplicative of existing content, no new knowledge entry is created.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow reviews logged HR interactions from the reporting period, groups them by topic, counts the most frequent question themes, summarizes notable trends, and posts the resulting report to Slack for HR operations review.

modifications.intent: For the really urgent international move questions, go ahead and give the employee a quick reply too, even if People Ops still needs to look at it.

name: HR Help Desk & Knowledge Loop

objects.role: Read service representing the HR Knowledge Base Notion database for retrieving prior answers, policies, and logged interactions.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime data; retrieves HR knowledge base records on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for relevant HR answers, return matching knowledge base entries, prior resolved interactions, and policy snippets. When asked whether a resolved answer already exists, return the closest matching existing entries and similarity indicators. When asked for historical interactions for reporting, return logged question and topic records for the requested time window. When answering queries, call the `hr-knowledge-base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for recording resolved HR interactions and adding new knowledge entries into the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of interaction logs and knowledge entries written to Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked to log a resolved interaction, write the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database. When asked to add a new answer, write a new knowledge entry with topic, question pattern, answer, source interaction reference, and review metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.

objects.role: Write service for posting escalation notifications to the People Operations Slack channel.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of escalation notifications posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to notify People Operations about an escalated or high-priority issue, post to the #people-ops-escalations channel with the question, urgency, employee context, confidence or sensitivity rationale, and thread reference.

objects.role: Write service for posting weekly HR topic reports to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of weekly report messages posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to publish the weekly HR question report, post to the #hr-ops-weekly channel with top topics, counts, notable trends, and summary observations.

objects.role: Business logic object that interprets employee HR questions, applies confidence and sensitivity rules, and decides whether to answer directly or escalate.
objects.state_description: Tracks active HR help conversations with extracted topic, urgency, confidence estimate, sensitivity flags, complexity flags, proposed answer, and escalation decision.
objects.behavior: When an employee HR question arrives, extract the topic, urgency marker if present, and any indicators of sensitivity or complexity. When a question concerns compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, or ambiguous policy interpretation, mark it sensitive or complex. When relevant knowledge is available, draft an answer grounded only in internal HR policies and retrieved knowledge. When confidence in the drafted answer is 80% or higher and the question is not sensitive or complex, prepare an instant response. When confidence is below 80%, or the question is sensitive or complex, escalate the case to People Operations. When urgency is High, treat the issue as high priority in the escalation payload even if an instant response is also available. When a direct answer is appropriate, send the response to slack-hr-questions. When escalation is required, send the escalation notice to slack-people-ops-escalations. When both an employee-facing acknowledgement and an escalation are appropriate, send to slack-hr-questions and slack-people-ops-escalations.

peers.relationship: Query for relevant HR policies, prior answers, and similar resolved interactions when a new HR question arrives
Send instant HR answers or escalation acknowledgements for employee questions in #hr-help
Send escalated or high-priority issue notifications when confidence is low or the issue is sensitive or complex

objects.role: Business logic object that determines what resolved Slack interactions should be logged and whether new knowledge should be added to the HR Knowledge Base.
objects.state_description: Tracks resolved conversation candidates with Slack references, extracted question-answer pair, resolver identity, deduplication result, and logging or knowledge-add decisions.
objects.behavior: When a resolution reaction arrives, identify the associated conversation and extract the employee question, the final People Operations answer, urgency, and resolution context. When the resolved interaction lacks a usable final answer, do not create a new knowledge entry and log only the resolution metadata if available. When a resolved interaction contains a substantive answer, prepare an interaction log entry. When the final answer is materially new compared with existing HR Knowledge Base content, mark it as a new knowledge candidate. When the answer already exists or is substantially duplicative, do not add a new knowledge entry. When a resolved interaction is ready to be recorded, send the interaction log to notion-hr-kb-writer. When a materially new answer is identified, send the new knowledge entry to notion-hr-kb-writer.

peers.relationship: Query existing HR knowledge to check whether the resolved answer is already present
Write resolved interaction logs and new knowledge entries after evaluation

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes historical HR questions to identify top recurring topics and generate the weekly report.
objects.state_description: Tracks reporting windows, aggregated topic counts, trend summaries, and the latest generated weekly report payload.
objects.behavior: When the weekly reporting trigger arrives, retrieve resolved and logged HR interactions for the reporting window. When interaction history is retrieved, group questions by topic, count frequency, identify the most common themes, and summarize notable trends. When the report is prepared, send it to slack-weekly-reports.

peers.relationship: Query historical logged HR interactions for the weekly reporting window
Send the weekly topic report for publication in #hr-ops-weekly

steps: When an employee posts an HR question in Slack, the workflow captures the message text, who asked it, when and where it was posted, any thread context, and any urgency signal included in the message such as a priority label or emoji.
The workflow searches the HR knowledge base in Notion for relevant policy content, prior resolved interactions, and matching answer entries that can help address the employee’s question.

Using only retrieved internal HR policies and knowledge base content, the workflow drafts and posts an immediate reply in Slack when it has enough relevant information to answer the question directly.

If the drafted answer has less than 80% confidence, or if the question is identified as sensitive or complex, the workflow escalates it to the HR team instead of relying only on the bot response; sensitive or complex topics include compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, and ambiguous policy interpretation. If the employee marked the issue as high urgency, the escalation is treated as high priority even when an immediate acknowledgement or partial answer is also sent in Slack.

For any escalated or high-priority case, the workflow sends a notification in Slack to the HR or People Operations team with the employee’s question, urgency level, relevant employee context, the reason for escalation such as low confidence or sensitivity, and a reference to the original conversation.

When an HR or People Operations specialist marks the Slack conversation as resolved using a check-mark reaction, the workflow identifies the related conversation and records the resolved interaction in Notion, including the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution details.

If the resolved interaction contains a substantive final answer that is materially new compared with existing Notion knowledge base content, the workflow adds it as a new knowledge entry; if the answer is missing, unusable, or substantially duplicative of existing content, no new knowledge entry is created.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow reviews logged HR interactions from the reporting period, groups them by topic, counts the most frequent question themes, summarizes notable trends, and posts the resulting report to Slack for HR operations review.

modifications.intent: Stop posting employee-facing acknowledgement replies in #hr-help for high-priority questions when those cases are being escalated to People Operations; just send the escalation notice.

name: HR Help Desk & Knowledge Loop

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound employee questions posted in #hr-help and outbound bot replies to that same channel or thread.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received #hr-help message events and outbound reply payloads sent to Slack; does not store HR knowledge itself.
objects.behavior: When a new message event arrives in the #hr-help channel, capture the message text, urgency marker, user, channel, thread context, and timestamp. When a new message event arrives, forward it to hr-help-triage via peer message. When asked to write a response, post to the #hr-help channel.

peers.relationship: Forward employee HR question events from #hr-help when a new message arrives

event_sources: slack: message posted in #hr-help

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service for inbound resolution reactions indicating a People Operations specialist has marked a conversation resolved.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of received :white_check_mark: reaction events associated with Slack conversations.
objects.behavior: When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives on a relevant Slack conversation, capture the reacting user, channel, thread or message reference, and timestamp. When a :white_check_mark: reaction event arrives, forward it to knowledge-capture via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward resolution reaction events when a People Operations specialist marks a conversation resolved

event_sources: slack: :white_check_mark: reaction added to conversation

objects.role: Scheduler service that emits the weekly reporting trigger every Monday at 09:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Tracks emitted schedule events for audit purposes.
objects.behavior: When the scheduled time Monday 09:00 UTC occurs, emit a weekly reporting event and forward it to topic-analytics via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly reporting trigger every Monday at 09:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: monday 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service representing the HR Knowledge Base Notion database for retrieving prior answers, policies, and logged interactions.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime data; retrieves HR knowledge base records on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for relevant HR answers, return matching knowledge base entries, prior resolved interactions, and policy snippets. When asked whether a resolved answer already exists, return the closest matching existing entries and similarity indicators. When asked for historical interactions for reporting, return logged question and topic records for the requested time window. When answering queries, call the `hr-knowledge-base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for recording resolved HR interactions and adding new knowledge entries into the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of interaction logs and knowledge entries written to Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked to log a resolved interaction, write the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database. When asked to add a new answer, write a new knowledge entry with topic, question pattern, answer, source interaction reference, and review metadata to the HR Knowledge Base Notion database.

objects.role: Write service for posting escalation notifications to the People Operations Slack channel.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of escalation notifications posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to notify People Operations about an escalated or high-priority issue, post to the #people-ops-escalations channel with the question, urgency, employee context, confidence or sensitivity rationale, and thread reference.

objects.role: Write service for posting weekly HR topic reports to Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty audit trail of weekly report messages posted to Slack.
objects.behavior: When asked to publish the weekly HR question report, post to the #hr-ops-weekly channel with top topics, counts, notable trends, and summary observations.

objects.role: Business logic object that interprets employee HR questions, applies confidence and sensitivity rules, and decides whether to answer directly or escalate.
objects.state_description: Tracks active HR help conversations with extracted topic, urgency, confidence estimate, sensitivity flags, complexity flags, proposed answer, and escalation decision.
objects.behavior: When an employee HR question arrives, extract the topic, urgency marker if present, and any indicators of sensitivity or complexity. When a question concerns compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, or ambiguous policy interpretation, mark it sensitive or complex. When relevant knowledge is available, draft an answer grounded only in internal HR policies and retrieved knowledge. When confidence in the drafted answer is 80% or higher and the question is not sensitive or complex, prepare an instant response. When confidence is below 80%, or the question is sensitive or complex, escalate the case to People Operations. When urgency is High, treat the issue as high priority in the escalation payload even if an instant response is also available. When a direct answer is appropriate, send the response to slack-hr-questions. When escalation is required, send the escalation notice to slack-people-ops-escalations. When both an employee-facing acknowledgement and an escalation are appropriate, send to slack-hr-questions and slack-people-ops-escalations.

peers.relationship: Query for relevant HR policies, prior answers, and similar resolved interactions when a new HR question arrives
Send instant HR answers or escalation acknowledgements for employee questions in #hr-help
Send escalated or high-priority issue notifications when confidence is low or the issue is sensitive or complex

objects.role: Business logic object that determines what resolved Slack interactions should be logged and whether new knowledge should be added to the HR Knowledge Base.
objects.state_description: Tracks resolved conversation candidates with Slack references, extracted question-answer pair, resolver identity, deduplication result, and logging or knowledge-add decisions.
objects.behavior: When a resolution reaction arrives, identify the associated conversation and extract the employee question, the final People Operations answer, urgency, and resolution context. When the resolved interaction lacks a usable final answer, do not create a new knowledge entry and log only the resolution metadata if available. When a resolved interaction contains a substantive answer, prepare an interaction log entry. When the final answer is materially new compared with existing HR Knowledge Base content, mark it as a new knowledge candidate. When the answer already exists or is substantially duplicative, do not add a new knowledge entry. When a resolved interaction is ready to be recorded, send the interaction log to notion-hr-kb-writer. When a materially new answer is identified, send the new knowledge entry to notion-hr-kb-writer.

peers.relationship: Query existing HR knowledge to check whether the resolved answer is already present
Write resolved interaction logs and new knowledge entries after evaluation

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes historical HR questions to identify top recurring topics and generate the weekly report.
objects.state_description: Tracks reporting windows, aggregated topic counts, trend summaries, and the latest generated weekly report payload.
objects.behavior: When the weekly reporting trigger arrives, retrieve resolved and logged HR interactions for the reporting window. When interaction history is retrieved, group questions by topic, count frequency, identify the most common themes, and summarize notable trends. When the report is prepared, send it to slack-weekly-reports.

peers.relationship: Query historical logged HR interactions for the weekly reporting window
Send the weekly topic report for publication in #hr-ops-weekly

steps: When an employee posts an HR question in Slack, the workflow captures the message text, who asked it, when and where it was posted, any thread context, and any urgency signal included in the message such as a priority label or emoji.
The workflow searches the HR knowledge base in Notion for relevant policy content, prior resolved interactions, and matching answer entries that can help address the employee’s question.

Using only retrieved internal HR policies and knowledge base content, the workflow drafts and posts an immediate reply in Slack when it has enough relevant information to answer the question directly.

If the drafted answer has less than 80% confidence, or if the question is identified as sensitive or complex, the workflow escalates it to the HR team instead of relying only on the bot response; sensitive or complex topics include compensation, legal matters, harassment, medical leave details, employee relations disputes, personally sensitive data, and ambiguous policy interpretation. If the employee marked the issue as high urgency, the escalation is treated as high priority even when an immediate acknowledgement or partial answer is also sent in Slack.

For any escalated or high-priority case, the workflow sends a notification in Slack to the HR or People Operations team with the employee’s question, urgency level, relevant employee context, the reason for escalation such as low confidence or sensitivity, and a reference to the original conversation.

When an HR or People Operations specialist marks the Slack conversation as resolved using a check-mark reaction, the workflow identifies the related conversation and records the resolved interaction in Notion, including the question, answer, participants, timestamps, urgency, escalation status, and resolution details.

If the resolved interaction contains a substantive final answer that is materially new compared with existing Notion knowledge base content, the workflow adds it as a new knowledge entry; if the answer is missing, unusable, or substantially duplicative of existing content, no new knowledge entry is created.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow reviews logged HR interactions from the reporting period, groups them by topic, counts the most frequent question themes, summarizes notable trends, and posts the resulting report to Slack for HR operations review.

modifications.intent: For resolved HR help conversations, if the final answer is substantially duplicative of what is already in the HR knowledge base, treat that as a skip case altogether and do not write an interaction log for it; only send records to Notion when the resolution adds something materially new.

events.input: A new Slack message is posted in `#hr-help` by James Brown: "High: I'm relocating to Germany next month. What is the company policy on visa support, payroll changes, and tax handling for an international move?" Channel: `#hr-help`. Thread: none. Timestamp: `2026-05-18T14:22:00Z`.

expect.action: A reply is posted in Slack in #hr-help to James Brown acknowledging the high-priority international relocation question and providing policy-based guidance from the HR knowledge base on visa support, payroll changes, and tax handling for a move to Germany. Because the request is high urgency and involves sensitive or complex international mobility and tax/legal interpretation, a high-priority Slack notification is also sent to the People Operations team with James Brown's question, urgency level, employee context, the escalation reason, and a reference to the original #hr-help conversation.
expect.reason: The Slack question can be partially answered from existing HR policy content, but international relocation with visa and tax handling is a sensitive/complex topic and the message is marked High, so the workflow both responds in Slack and escalates to People Operations as high priority.

No new Notion knowledge-base entry is created because the final answer is substantially duplicative of existing HR knowledge content about relocation to Germany, visa support, payroll changes, and tax handling.
expect.reason: Under the baseline workflow, a check-mark resolution writes the resolved interaction to Notion, but a new knowledge entry is added only when the final answer is materially new compared with existing Notion content.
