name: AI Incident Postmortem Review & Coaching

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives closed-incident webhooks from Incident.io and forwards incident closure data into the review domain.
objects.state_description: Records received Incident.io closed-incident webhook payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a closed-incident webhook arrives from Incident.io, record the payload and treat it as a new incident closure event. Then forward the review to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward closed-incident details when an Incident.io closed-incident webhook arrives

event_sources: webhook: Incident.io incident closed

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives new retrospective document events from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database and forwards them for analysis.
objects.state_description: Records received Notion retrospective-created event payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a new retrospective document event arrives from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database, record the payload and treat it as a new retrospective available event. Then forward the event to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward retrospective document event details when a new Notion retrospective is detected

event_sources: external system: Notion Incident Retrospectives record created

objects.role: Entry-point service that emits the weekly analytics trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Records emitted weekly schedule ticks as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When the Friday 16:00 UTC schedule fires, record the tick and treat it as a weekly digest trigger.

peers.relationship: Forward the weekly digest trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: Friday 16:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service for retrospective documents and related metadata stored in the Incident Retrospectives Notion database.
objects.state_description: Holds no persistent state; retrieves retrospective records and metadata on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for a retrospective document, reviewer email, incident linkage, or a time-bounded set of retrospectives, return the requested Notion data. When answering queries, call the `retrospective-store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for delivering direct messages, channel posts, and threaded breakdowns to Slack.
objects.state_description: Records outbound Slack messages, destinations, and thread references as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When asked to send reviewer feedback with a reviewer email, send a Slack DM to that reviewer. When asked to send fallback feedback without a reviewer email, post to the #incident-reviews channel. When asked to publish the weekly digest, post to the #incident-reviews channel and post detailed breakdowns in the digest thread.

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates individual incident retrospectives against postmortem best practices and determines how feedback should be delivered.
objects.state_description: Tracks incident closure signals, retrospective analysis status, generated feedback, best-practice scores, and delivery decisions for each retrospective.
objects.behavior: When a closed incident arrives, remember the incident closure context for later retrospective association. When a new retrospective event arrives, obtain the retrospective content and metadata, evaluate it against best practices for customer impact, root cause, and action items, and generate concrete coaching feedback. When evaluating, score completeness and quality for customer impact, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, and identify missing or weak sections. When a reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for direct delivery to that reviewer. When no reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for delivery to the #incident-reviews channel.

peers.relationship: Query for retrospective content and metadata when a new retrospective event arrives
Send generated feedback as a Slack DM when reviewer email is present, or post feedback to the #incident-reviews channel when reviewer email is absent

objects.role: Business logic object that compiles weekly retrospective analytics and TLDR insights across all retrospectives.
objects.state_description: Tracks weekly aggregation windows, compiled retrospective metrics, generated TLDR summaries, and digest content for each reporting period.
objects.behavior: When the weekly digest trigger arrives, retrieve all retrospectives for the reporting window, analyze patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and compile aggregate analytics. When summarizing the week, generate TLDR insights plus detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns. When preparing the digest, organize the summary for posting to the #incident-reviews channel with detailed breakdowns suitable for thread replies.

peers.relationship: Query for all retrospectives in the weekly reporting window when the Friday digest trigger arrives
Send the weekly insights digest to the #incident-reviews channel and send detailed breakdowns for the thread

steps: The workflow captures incident closure events from Incident.io when an incident is marked closed, whether the closure is received by webhook, polling, or manual submission, and records that closure context so later retrospective reviews can be tied back to the incident.
When a new incident retrospective is created in Notion, the workflow detects that a retrospective is available, retrieves the document and its related metadata, and sends the content for analysis.

The workflow evaluates each retrospective against postmortem best practices by assessing customer impact coverage, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, then generates concrete coaching feedback that highlights completeness, quality, and any missing or weak sections.
If the retrospective includes a reviewer email, the workflow routes the generated feedback to that person as a direct message in Slack.
If no reviewer email is available, the workflow instead posts the feedback to the designated incident review team channel in Slack.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow gathers all retrospectives from the reporting window in Notion, analyzes patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and produces aggregate analytics along with concise TLDR summaries.
The workflow publishes the weekly digest to Slack in the incident review team channel and adds detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns as replies in the digest thread.

modifications.intent: For the next reporting cycle, skip the Friday weekly digest this week and resume sending it starting with next Friday's 16:00 UTC run.

In the digest thread, a detailed reply is posted for retrospective "Retrospective: API latency spike impacting checkout" (incident INC-2026-1042) with its retrospective-by-retrospective breakdown.

name: AI Incident Postmortem Review & Coaching

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives closed-incident webhooks from Incident.io and forwards incident closure data into the review domain.
objects.state_description: Records received Incident.io closed-incident webhook payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a closed-incident webhook arrives from Incident.io, record the payload and treat it as a new incident closure event. Then forward the review to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward closed-incident details when an Incident.io closed-incident webhook arrives

event_sources: webhook: Incident.io incident closed

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives new retrospective document events from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database and forwards them for analysis.
objects.state_description: Records received Notion retrospective-created event payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a new retrospective document event arrives from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database, record the payload and treat it as a new retrospective available event. Then forward the event to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward retrospective document event details when a new Notion retrospective is detected

event_sources: external system: Notion Incident Retrospectives record created

objects.role: Entry-point service that emits the weekly analytics trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Records emitted weekly schedule ticks as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When the Friday 16:00 UTC schedule fires, record the tick and treat it as a weekly digest trigger.

peers.relationship: Forward the weekly digest trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: Friday 16:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service for retrospective documents and related metadata stored in the Incident Retrospectives Notion database.
objects.state_description: Holds no persistent state; retrieves retrospective records and metadata on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for a retrospective document, reviewer email, incident linkage, or a time-bounded set of retrospectives, return the requested Notion data. When answering queries, call the `retrospective-store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for delivering direct messages, channel posts, and threaded breakdowns to Slack.
objects.state_description: Records outbound Slack messages, destinations, and thread references as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When asked to send reviewer feedback with a reviewer email, send a Slack DM to that reviewer. When asked to send fallback feedback without a reviewer email, post to the #incident-reviews channel. When asked to publish the weekly digest, post to the #incident-reviews channel and post detailed breakdowns in the digest thread.

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates individual incident retrospectives against postmortem best practices and determines how feedback should be delivered.
objects.state_description: Tracks incident closure signals, retrospective analysis status, generated feedback, best-practice scores, and delivery decisions for each retrospective.
objects.behavior: When a closed incident arrives, remember the incident closure context for later retrospective association. When a new retrospective event arrives, obtain the retrospective content and metadata, evaluate it against best practices for customer impact, root cause, and action items, and generate concrete coaching feedback. When evaluating, score completeness and quality for customer impact, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, and identify missing or weak sections. When a reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for direct delivery to that reviewer. When no reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for delivery to the #incident-reviews channel.

peers.relationship: Query for retrospective content and metadata when a new retrospective event arrives
Send generated feedback as a Slack DM when reviewer email is present, or post feedback to the #incident-reviews channel when reviewer email is absent

objects.role: Business logic object that compiles weekly retrospective analytics and TLDR insights across all retrospectives.
objects.state_description: Tracks weekly aggregation windows, compiled retrospective metrics, generated TLDR summaries, and digest content for each reporting period.
objects.behavior: When the weekly digest trigger arrives, retrieve all retrospectives for the reporting window, analyze patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and compile aggregate analytics. When summarizing the week, generate TLDR insights plus detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns. When preparing the digest, organize the summary for posting to the #incident-reviews channel with detailed breakdowns suitable for thread replies.

peers.relationship: Query for all retrospectives in the weekly reporting window when the Friday digest trigger arrives
Send the weekly insights digest to the #incident-reviews channel and send detailed breakdowns for the thread

steps: The workflow captures incident closure events from Incident.io when an incident is marked closed, whether the closure is received by webhook, polling, or manual submission, and records that closure context so later retrospective reviews can be tied back to the incident.
When a new incident retrospective is created in Notion, the workflow detects that a retrospective is available, retrieves the document and its related metadata, and sends the content for analysis.

The workflow evaluates each retrospective against postmortem best practices by assessing customer impact coverage, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, then generates concrete coaching feedback that highlights completeness, quality, and any missing or weak sections.
If the retrospective includes a reviewer email, the workflow routes the generated feedback to that person as a direct message in Slack.
If no reviewer email is available, the workflow instead posts the feedback to the designated incident review team channel in Slack.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow gathers all retrospectives from the reporting window in Notion, analyzes patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and produces aggregate analytics along with concise TLDR summaries.
The workflow publishes the weekly digest to Slack in the incident review team channel and adds detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns as replies in the digest thread.

modifications.intent: The fallback path should apply whenever the retrospective does not have a usable reviewer email, not just when the field is literally blank. Treat placeholder or malformed reviewer emails as missing reviewer information.

events.input: Incident.io sends a closed-incident webhook for incident INC-4821 titled "EU API latency spike". The payload includes status "closed", closed_at "2026-05-19T14:32:00Z", severity "SEV-2", incident lead "Maya Patel", and incident URL "https://app.incident.io/incidents/INC-4821".

expect.action: A closure record for incident INC-4821 is stored with the incident title, closed timestamp, severity, lead, and incident URL so the retrospective can be tied back to the closed incident.
expect.reason: A closed-incident event from Incident.io triggers the workflow to capture and persist closure context for later retrospective linkage.

events.input: The Incident Retrospectives Notion database emits a new record created event for retrospective page "Retro: EU API latency spike" with Notion page ID "9f3c2b1a-e7d4-4c91-8f20-3b7b0c9a1122", linked incident ID "INC-4821", reviewer email "maya.patel@example.com", created_at "2026-05-20T09:15:00Z", and database name "Incident Retrospectives".

expect.action: The retrospective document for "Retro: EU API latency spike" is retrieved from Notion, analyzed for postmortem quality, and a coaching feedback message is sent as a direct message in Slack to maya.patel@example.com.
expect.reason: A new retrospective in Notion triggers document retrieval and analysis, and because a usable reviewer email is present the feedback is routed to that reviewer in Slack.

events.input: The Friday 16:00 UTC weekly digest schedule fires for reporting time "2026-05-22T16:00:00Z".

expect.action: A weekly incident review digest is posted in the Slack incident review team channel, including aggregate retrospective analytics and concise TLDR summaries for the reporting window, with detailed per-retrospective breakdowns added as replies in the thread.
expect.reason: The weekly schedule fires, so the workflow compiles retrospectives from the reporting window and publishes the digest and threaded breakdowns to Slack.

events.input: The Incident Retrospectives Notion database emits a new record created event for retrospective page "Retro: Checkout timeout surge" with Notion page ID "1c4d8a52-6e43-4c3a-b1bf-9a7d5f4e2201", linked incident ID "INC-4902", reviewer email "oncall-reviewer", created_at "2026-05-11T09:12:00Z", database name "Incident Retrospectives", and page URL "https://www.notion.so/incident-retrospectives/1c4d8a526e434c3ab1bf9a7d5f4e2201".

expect.action: The retrospective document for "Retro: Checkout timeout surge" is retrieved from Notion, analyzed for postmortem quality, and a coaching feedback message is sent as a direct message in Slack to oncall-reviewer.
expect.reason: A new retrospective in Notion triggers analysis, and the provided reviewer email is treated as usable so the feedback is delivered directly in Slack.

events.input: The Incident Retrospectives Notion database emits a new record created event for retrospective page "Retro: Mobile auth token expiry" with Notion page ID "7a9f2d31-3d6e-4f55-98f7-2de9b0a11844", linked incident ID "INC-4911", reviewer email "n/a", created_at "2026-05-12T11:05:00Z", database name "Incident Retrospectives", and page URL "https://www.notion.so/incident-retrospectives/7a9f2d313d6e4f5598f72de9b0a11844".

expect.action: The retrospective document for "Retro: Mobile auth token expiry" is retrieved from Notion, analyzed for postmortem quality, and the resulting coaching feedback is posted in the Slack incident review team channel instead of a direct message because the reviewer email is not usable.
expect.reason: The active fallback rule treats placeholder or malformed reviewer emails as missing, so feedback is routed to the team channel when no usable reviewer email is available.

expect.action: The retrospective document for "Retro: Search indexing lag" is retrieved from Notion, analyzed for postmortem quality, and a coaching feedback message is sent as a direct message in Slack to priya.raman@example.com.
expect.reason: A new retrospective in Notion triggers analysis, and the valid reviewer email causes the feedback to be sent directly to that reviewer in Slack.

events.input: The Friday 16:00 UTC weekly digest schedule fires for reporting time "2026-05-15T16:00:00Z".

expect.action: A weekly incident review digest is posted in the Slack incident review team channel, including aggregate retrospective analytics and concise TLDR summaries for the reporting window, with detailed per-retrospective breakdowns added as replies in the thread.
expect.reason: The weekly schedule fires, so the workflow compiles retrospectives from the reporting window and publishes the digest and threaded breakdowns to Slack.

tools.description: Persist an incoming closed-incident webhook payload as a recorded incident closure event in the external review system.
Persist an incoming retrospective document event payload as a recorded retrospective availability event in the external review system.
Persist the weekly Friday 16:00 UTC schedule tick as an external analytics trigger event.
Send a direct message to a Slack user.
Post a message to a Slack channel.
Post a threaded reply in Slack under an existing channel message.
Retrieve reference data from retrospective-store data. Retrospective documents and related Notion metadata, including reviewer email, incident linkage, and time-bounded retrospective records.

name: AI Incident Postmortem Review & Coaching

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives closed-incident webhooks from Incident.io and forwards incident closure data into the review domain.
objects.state_description: Records received Incident.io closed-incident webhook payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a closed-incident webhook arrives from Incident.io, record the payload and treat it as a new incident closure event. Then forward the review to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward closed-incident details when an Incident.io closed-incident webhook arrives

event_sources: webhook: Incident.io incident closed

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives new retrospective document events from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database and forwards them for analysis.
objects.state_description: Records received Notion retrospective-created event payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a new retrospective document event arrives from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database, record the payload and treat it as a new retrospective available event. Then forward the event to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward retrospective document event details when a new Notion retrospective is detected

event_sources: external system: Notion Incident Retrospectives record created

objects.role: Entry-point service that emits the weekly analytics trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Records emitted weekly schedule ticks as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When the Friday 16:00 UTC schedule fires, record the tick and treat it as a weekly digest trigger.

peers.relationship: Forward the weekly digest trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: Friday 16:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service for retrospective documents and related metadata stored in the Incident Retrospectives Notion database.
objects.state_description: Holds no persistent state; retrieves retrospective records and metadata on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for a retrospective document, reviewer email, incident linkage, or a time-bounded set of retrospectives, return the requested Notion data. When answering queries, call the `retrospective-store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for delivering direct messages, channel posts, and threaded breakdowns to Slack.
objects.state_description: Records outbound Slack messages, destinations, and thread references as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When asked to send reviewer feedback with a reviewer email, send a Slack DM to that reviewer. When asked to send fallback feedback without a reviewer email, post to the #incident-reviews channel. When asked to publish the weekly digest, post to the #incident-reviews channel and post detailed breakdowns in the digest thread.

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates individual incident retrospectives against postmortem best practices and determines how feedback should be delivered.
objects.state_description: Tracks incident closure signals, retrospective analysis status, generated feedback, best-practice scores, and delivery decisions for each retrospective.
objects.behavior: When a closed incident arrives, remember the incident closure context for later retrospective association. When a new retrospective event arrives, obtain the retrospective content and metadata, evaluate it against best practices for customer impact, root cause, and action items, and generate concrete coaching feedback. When evaluating, score completeness and quality for customer impact, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, and identify missing or weak sections. When a reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for direct delivery to that reviewer. When no reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for delivery to the #incident-reviews channel.

peers.relationship: Query for retrospective content and metadata when a new retrospective event arrives
Send generated feedback as a Slack DM when reviewer email is present, or post feedback to the #incident-reviews channel when reviewer email is absent

objects.role: Business logic object that compiles weekly retrospective analytics and TLDR insights across all retrospectives.
objects.state_description: Tracks weekly aggregation windows, compiled retrospective metrics, generated TLDR summaries, and digest content for each reporting period.
objects.behavior: When the weekly digest trigger arrives, retrieve all retrospectives for the reporting window, analyze patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and compile aggregate analytics. When summarizing the week, generate TLDR insights plus detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns. When preparing the digest, organize the summary for posting to the #incident-reviews channel with detailed breakdowns suitable for thread replies.

peers.relationship: Query for all retrospectives in the weekly reporting window when the Friday digest trigger arrives
Send the weekly insights digest to the #incident-reviews channel and send detailed breakdowns for the thread

steps: The workflow captures incident closure events from Incident.io when an incident is marked closed, whether the closure is received by webhook, polling, or manual submission, and records that closure context so later retrospective reviews can be tied back to the incident.
When a new incident retrospective is created in Notion, the workflow detects that a retrospective is available, retrieves the document and its related metadata, and sends the content for analysis.

The workflow evaluates each retrospective against postmortem best practices by assessing customer impact coverage, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, then generates concrete coaching feedback that highlights completeness, quality, and any missing or weak sections.
If the retrospective includes a reviewer email, the workflow routes the generated feedback to that person as a direct message in Slack.
If no reviewer email is available, the workflow instead posts the feedback to the designated incident review team channel in Slack.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow gathers all retrospectives from the reporting window in Notion, analyzes patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and produces aggregate analytics along with concise TLDR summaries.
The workflow publishes the weekly digest to Slack in the incident review team channel and adds detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns as replies in the digest thread.

modifications.intent: For any new retrospective whose linked incident was previously closed with severity "SEV-1", also send the generated coaching feedback to the #incident-reviews channel in addition to the normal delivery path. Keep the existing behavior unchanged for all other severities: if reviewer email is present, still send a Slack DM to that reviewer; if reviewer email is missing, still post to the #incident-reviews channel only.

events.input: Incident.io sends a closed-incident webhook for incident INC-4821 titled "EU API latency spike". The payload includes status "closed", closed_at "2026-05-19T14:32:00Z", severity "SEV-2", incident lead "Maya Patel", and incident URL "https://app.incident.io/incidents/INC-4821".

expect.action: A closure record for incident INC-4821 is stored with the incident title, closed timestamp, severity, lead, and incident URL so the incident can be tied to later retrospective review.
expect.reason: A closed-incident webhook from Incident.io triggers the incident closure capture flow, which records closure context for downstream retrospective linkage.

events.input: The Incident Retrospectives Notion database emits a new record created event for retrospective page "Retro: EU API latency spike" with Notion page ID "9f3c2b1a-e7d4-4c91-8f20-3b7b0c9a1122", linked incident ID "INC-4821", reviewer email "maya.patel@example.com", created_at "2026-05-20T09:15:00Z", and database name "Incident Retrospectives".

expect.reason: A new Notion retrospective event with a reviewer email triggers retrospective analysis and the normal delivery path is a Slack DM to the reviewer.

events.input: The Friday 16:00 UTC weekly digest schedule fires for reporting time "2026-05-22T16:00:00Z".

expect.action: A weekly digest is generated from retrospectives in the reporting window, including aggregate analytics and TLDR summaries, and it is posted to the incident review team Slack channel with detailed retrospective breakdowns added as thread replies.
expect.reason: The weekly schedule triggers digest generation and publication to Slack for the incident review team.

tools.description: Persist an incoming closed-incident webhook payload as a recorded incident closure event in the external review system.
Persist an incoming retrospective document event payload as a recorded retrospective availability event in the external review system.
Persist the weekly Friday 16:00 UTC schedule tick as an external analytics trigger event.
Send a direct message to a Slack user.
Post a message to a Slack channel.
Post a threaded reply in Slack under an existing channel message.
Retrieve reference data from retrospective-store data. Retrospective documents and related Notion metadata, including reviewer email, incident linkage, and time-bounded retrospective records.

name: AI Incident Postmortem Review & Coaching

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives closed-incident webhooks from Incident.io and forwards incident closure data into the review domain.
objects.state_description: Records received Incident.io closed-incident webhook payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a closed-incident webhook arrives from Incident.io, record the payload and treat it as a new incident closure event. Then forward the review to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward closed-incident details when an Incident.io closed-incident webhook arrives

event_sources: webhook: Incident.io incident closed

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives new retrospective document events from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database and forwards them for analysis.
objects.state_description: Records received Notion retrospective-created event payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a new retrospective document event arrives from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database, record the payload and treat it as a new retrospective available event. Then forward the event to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward retrospective document event details when a new Notion retrospective is detected

event_sources: external system: Notion Incident Retrospectives record created

objects.role: Entry-point service that emits the weekly analytics trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Records emitted weekly schedule ticks as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When the Friday 16:00 UTC schedule fires, record the tick and treat it as a weekly digest trigger.

peers.relationship: Forward the weekly digest trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: Friday 16:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service for retrospective documents and related metadata stored in the Incident Retrospectives Notion database.
objects.state_description: Holds no persistent state; retrieves retrospective records and metadata on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for a retrospective document, reviewer email, incident linkage, or a time-bounded set of retrospectives, return the requested Notion data. When answering queries, call the `retrospective-store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for delivering direct messages, channel posts, and threaded breakdowns to Slack.
objects.state_description: Records outbound Slack messages, destinations, and thread references as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When asked to send reviewer feedback with a reviewer email, send a Slack DM to that reviewer. When asked to send fallback feedback without a reviewer email, post to the #incident-reviews channel. When asked to publish the weekly digest, post to the #incident-reviews channel and post detailed breakdowns in the digest thread.

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates individual incident retrospectives against postmortem best practices and determines how feedback should be delivered.
objects.state_description: Tracks incident closure signals, retrospective analysis status, generated feedback, best-practice scores, and delivery decisions for each retrospective.
objects.behavior: When a closed incident arrives, remember the incident closure context for later retrospective association. When a new retrospective event arrives, obtain the retrospective content and metadata, evaluate it against best practices for customer impact, root cause, and action items, and generate concrete coaching feedback. When evaluating, score completeness and quality for customer impact, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, and identify missing or weak sections. When a reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for direct delivery to that reviewer. When no reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for delivery to the #incident-reviews channel.

peers.relationship: Query for retrospective content and metadata when a new retrospective event arrives
Send generated feedback as a Slack DM when reviewer email is present, or post feedback to the #incident-reviews channel when reviewer email is absent

objects.role: Business logic object that compiles weekly retrospective analytics and TLDR insights across all retrospectives.
objects.state_description: Tracks weekly aggregation windows, compiled retrospective metrics, generated TLDR summaries, and digest content for each reporting period.
objects.behavior: When the weekly digest trigger arrives, retrieve all retrospectives for the reporting window, analyze patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and compile aggregate analytics. When summarizing the week, generate TLDR insights plus detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns. When preparing the digest, organize the summary for posting to the #incident-reviews channel with detailed breakdowns suitable for thread replies.

peers.relationship: Query for all retrospectives in the weekly reporting window when the Friday digest trigger arrives
Send the weekly insights digest to the #incident-reviews channel and send detailed breakdowns for the thread

steps: The workflow captures incident closure events from Incident.io when an incident is marked closed, whether the closure is received by webhook, polling, or manual submission, and records that closure context so later retrospective reviews can be tied back to the incident.
When a new incident retrospective is created in Notion, the workflow detects that a retrospective is available, retrieves the document and its related metadata, and sends the content for analysis.

The workflow evaluates each retrospective against postmortem best practices by assessing customer impact coverage, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, then generates concrete coaching feedback that highlights completeness, quality, and any missing or weak sections.
If the retrospective includes a reviewer email, the workflow routes the generated feedback to that person as a direct message in Slack.
If no reviewer email is available, the workflow instead posts the feedback to the designated incident review team channel in Slack.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow gathers all retrospectives from the reporting window in Notion, analyzes patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and produces aggregate analytics along with concise TLDR summaries.
The workflow publishes the weekly digest to Slack in the incident review team channel and adds detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns as replies in the digest thread.

modifications.intent: For retrospectives linked to incident ID INC-9007 only, always prepare feedback for delivery to the #incident-reviews channel instead of direct reviewer delivery, even when the Notion event includes a reviewer email.

expect.reason: When Incident.io reports an incident as closed, the workflow persists the closure details for later retrospective linkage and review.

name: AI Incident Postmortem Review & Coaching

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives closed-incident webhooks from Incident.io and forwards incident closure data into the review domain.
objects.state_description: Records received Incident.io closed-incident webhook payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a closed-incident webhook arrives from Incident.io, record the payload and treat it as a new incident closure event. Then forward the review to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward closed-incident details when an Incident.io closed-incident webhook arrives

event_sources: webhook: Incident.io incident closed

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives new retrospective document events from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database and forwards them for analysis.
objects.state_description: Records received Notion retrospective-created event payloads as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When a new retrospective document event arrives from the Incident Retrospectives Notion database, record the payload and treat it as a new retrospective available event. Then forward the event to incident-review.

peers.relationship: Forward retrospective document event details when a new Notion retrospective is detected

event_sources: external system: Notion Incident Retrospectives record created

objects.role: Entry-point service that emits the weekly analytics trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC.
objects.state_description: Records emitted weekly schedule ticks as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When the Friday 16:00 UTC schedule fires, record the tick and treat it as a weekly digest trigger.

peers.relationship: Forward the weekly digest trigger every Friday at 16:00 UTC

event_sources: schedule: Friday 16:00 UTC

objects.role: Read service for retrospective documents and related metadata stored in the Incident Retrospectives Notion database.
objects.state_description: Holds no persistent state; retrieves retrospective records and metadata on demand from Notion.
objects.behavior: When asked for a retrospective document, reviewer email, incident linkage, or a time-bounded set of retrospectives, return the requested Notion data. When answering queries, call the `retrospective-store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service for delivering direct messages, channel posts, and threaded breakdowns to Slack.
objects.state_description: Records outbound Slack messages, destinations, and thread references as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When asked to send reviewer feedback with a reviewer email, send a Slack DM to that reviewer. When asked to send fallback feedback without a reviewer email, post to the #incident-reviews channel. When asked to publish the weekly digest, post to the #incident-reviews channel and post detailed breakdowns in the digest thread.

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates individual incident retrospectives against postmortem best practices and determines how feedback should be delivered.
objects.state_description: Tracks incident closure signals, retrospective analysis status, generated feedback, best-practice scores, and delivery decisions for each retrospective.
objects.behavior: When a closed incident arrives, remember the incident closure context for later retrospective association. When a new retrospective event arrives, obtain the retrospective content and metadata, evaluate it against best practices for customer impact, root cause, and action items, and generate concrete coaching feedback. When evaluating, score completeness and quality for customer impact, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, and identify missing or weak sections. When a reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for direct delivery to that reviewer. When no reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for delivery to the #incident-reviews channel.

peers.relationship: Query for retrospective content and metadata when a new retrospective event arrives
Send generated feedback as a Slack DM when reviewer email is present, or post feedback to the #incident-reviews channel when reviewer email is absent

objects.role: Business logic object that compiles weekly retrospective analytics and TLDR insights across all retrospectives.
objects.state_description: Tracks weekly aggregation windows, compiled retrospective metrics, generated TLDR summaries, and digest content for each reporting period.
objects.behavior: When the weekly digest trigger arrives, retrieve all retrospectives for the reporting window, analyze patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and compile aggregate analytics. When summarizing the week, generate TLDR insights plus detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns. When preparing the digest, organize the summary for posting to the #incident-reviews channel with detailed breakdowns suitable for thread replies.

peers.relationship: Query for all retrospectives in the weekly reporting window when the Friday digest trigger arrives
Send the weekly insights digest to the #incident-reviews channel and send detailed breakdowns for the thread

steps: The workflow captures incident closure events from Incident.io when an incident is marked closed, whether the closure is received by webhook, polling, or manual submission, and records that closure context so later retrospective reviews can be tied back to the incident.
When a new incident retrospective is created in Notion, the workflow detects that a retrospective is available, retrieves the document and its related metadata, and sends the content for analysis.

The workflow evaluates each retrospective against postmortem best practices by assessing customer impact coverage, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, then generates concrete coaching feedback that highlights completeness, quality, and any missing or weak sections.
If the retrospective includes a reviewer email, the workflow routes the generated feedback to that person as a direct message in Slack.
If no reviewer email is available, the workflow instead posts the feedback to the designated incident review team channel in Slack.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow gathers all retrospectives from the reporting window in Notion, analyzes patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and produces aggregate analytics along with concise TLDR summaries.
The workflow publishes the weekly digest to Slack in the incident review team channel and adds detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns as replies in the digest thread.

modifications.intent: For retros tied to incidents that look pretty customer-facing, lean a little harder on the customer impact part of the feedback and make that the main thing you focus on.

events.input: Incident.io sends a closed-incident webhook for incident INC-4821 titled "EU API latency spike". The payload includes status "closed", closed_at "2026-05-19T14:32:00Z", severity "SEV-2", incident lead "Maya Patel", and incident URL "https://app.incident.io/incidents/INC-4821".

expect.action: The incident closure for incident INC-4821 ("EU API latency spike") is recorded in the incident review tracking record as a closed Incident.io incident, including the closure timestamp 2026-05-19T14:32:00Z and the incident context needed to link a later retrospective back to this incident.
expect.reason: When Incident.io reports an incident as closed, the workflow stores the closure context so any future retrospective can be tied to the closed incident.

events.input: The Incident Retrospectives Notion database emits a new record created event for retrospective page "Retro: EU API latency spike" with Notion page ID "9f3c2b1a-e7d4-4c91-8f20-3b7b0c9a1122", linked incident ID "INC-4821", reviewer email "maya.patel@example.com", created_at "2026-05-20T09:15:00Z", and database name "Incident Retrospectives".

expect.reason: A Notion new-record event triggers retrospective retrieval and analysis, but without retrievable retrospective content there is no Slack feedback message to send.

events.input: The Friday 16:00 UTC weekly digest schedule fires for reporting time "2026-05-22T16:00:00Z".

expect.action: A weekly digest message is posted in the Slack incident review team channel summarizing retrospective quality patterns for the reporting window ending 2026-05-22T16:00:00Z. The digest covers the available retrospective "Retrospective: API latency spike impacting checkout" for incident INC-2026-1042 and includes aggregate observations and a concise TLDR on customer impact coverage, root cause clarity, and action item specificity. In the digest thread, a reply is posted with the detailed breakdown for that retrospective, including that customer impact, root cause, and action items are all present and the retrospective includes concrete action items AI-2026-3001 and AI-2026-3002.

name: AI Incident Postmortem Review & Coaching

peers.relationship: Forward retrospective document event details when a new Notion retrospective is detected

When asked to send fallback feedback without a reviewer email, post to the #incident-reviews channel. When asked to publish the weekly digest, post to the #incident-reviews channel and post detailed breakdowns in the digest thread.

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates individual incident retrospectives against postmortem best practices and determines how feedback should be delivered.
objects.state_description: Tracks incident closure signals, retrospective analysis status, generated feedback, best-practice scores, and delivery decisions for each retrospective.
objects.behavior: When a closed incident arrives, remember the incident closure context for later retrospective association. When a new retrospective event arrives, obtain the retrospective content and metadata, evaluate it against best practices for customer impact, root cause, and action items, and generate concrete coaching feedback. When evaluating, score completeness and quality for customer impact, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, and identify missing or weak sections. When a reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for direct delivery to that reviewer. When no reviewer email is present, prepare feedback for delivery to the #incident-reviews channel.

peers.relationship: Query for retrospective content and metadata when a new retrospective event arrives
Send generated feedback as a Slack DM when reviewer email is present, or post feedback to the #incident-reviews channel when reviewer email is absent

objects.role: Business logic object that compiles weekly retrospective analytics and TLDR insights across all retrospectives.
objects.state_description: Tracks weekly aggregation windows, compiled retrospective metrics, generated TLDR summaries, and digest content for each reporting period.
objects.behavior: When the weekly digest trigger arrives, retrieve all retrospectives for the reporting window, analyze patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and compile aggregate analytics. When summarizing the week, generate TLDR insights plus detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns. When preparing the digest, organize the summary for posting to the #incident-reviews channel with detailed breakdowns suitable for thread replies.

peers.relationship: Query for all retrospectives in the weekly reporting window when the Friday digest trigger arrives
Send the weekly insights digest to the #incident-reviews channel and send detailed breakdowns for the thread

steps: The workflow captures incident closure events from Incident.io when an incident is marked closed, whether the closure is received by webhook, polling, or manual submission, and records that closure context so later retrospective reviews can be tied back to the incident.
When a new incident retrospective is created in Notion, the workflow detects that a retrospective is available, retrieves the document and its related metadata, and sends the content for analysis.

The workflow evaluates each retrospective against postmortem best practices by assessing customer impact coverage, root cause clarity, and action item specificity, then generates concrete coaching feedback that highlights completeness, quality, and any missing or weak sections.
If the retrospective includes a reviewer email, the workflow routes the generated feedback to that person as a direct message in Slack.
If no reviewer email is available, the workflow instead posts the feedback to the designated incident review team channel in Slack.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow gathers all retrospectives from the reporting window in Notion, analyzes patterns across customer impact, root cause, and action items, and produces aggregate analytics along with concise TLDR summaries.
The workflow publishes the weekly digest to Slack in the incident review team channel and adds detailed retrospective-by-retrospective breakdowns as replies in the digest thread.

modifications.intent: If a retrospective comes through without anyone attached to review it, let's just leave it there instead of dropping it into the channel.

name: Slack-ClickUp Support Automation

objects.role: Slack entry-point that receives /support slash command submissions from users in #help-desk and forwards them to the support-triage business logic object
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming support requests with user ID, request text, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a /support slash command event arrives, forward the full request payload (user ID, request text, channel, timestamp) to support-triage via peer message. When a ticket status update command arrives from a team member, forward it to support-triage.

peers.relationship: Forward support request payload when a /support slash command is received
Forward ticket status update commands from team members

event_sources: webhook: Slack /support slash command
webhook: Slack ticket status update action from team member

objects.role: Dual-role Slack write service that posts ticket notifications to #support-queue and receives status update actions from team members, forwarding them to support-triage
objects.state_description: Audit log of all messages posted to #support-queue and status update actions received
objects.behavior: Record all incoming messages and post them to the #support-queue channel. When a team member submits a status update action (e.g., marking a ticket In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved) from within #support-queue, forward the action payload to support-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward team member status update actions received in #support-queue

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action from team member in #support-queue

objects.role: Read service representing the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence, answering queries about existing solutions for common issues
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles on demand
objects.behavior: Respond to queries about known issues and solutions by searching the IT Support knowledge base. When answering queries, call the `confluence_kb_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies support requests, resolves common issues instantly, creates ClickUp tickets for complex issues, assigns priority and routing, and orchestrates all status transitions
objects.state_description: Tracks active support requests with request ID, user ID, request text, classification, priority, assigned team, ClickUp ticket ID, and current status (Open, In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved)
objects.behavior: When a support request arrives: query confluence-kb for matching solutions. If a known solution is found, send the step-by-step instructions to user-dm-notifier. If no solution is found or the issue is complex, classify the request into category (Bug, Access Request, Hardware, General Inquiry) and assign priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low) based on request content and keywords. Determine the appropriate team (Tier 1 Support, Tier 2 Engineering, or Infrastructure) based on category and current workload tracked in state. Send the ticket creation payload to clickup-tickets and simultaneously send the team notification to support-queue-channel and send an acknowledgement to user-dm-notifier. When a status update arrives (In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved): update the tracked status in state, send the status notification to user-dm-notifier, and if status is Resolved, send the resolution details to confluence-kb-updater. On Monday at 09:00 UTC, compute weekly report metrics from state (common issues, average resolution times, team performance) and send the report to support-metrics-channel.

peers.relationship: Query for matching solutions when a new support request arrives
Send ticket creation payload with category, priority, and assigned team for complex or unrecognized requests
Send team notification with ticket ID, category, priority, and context when a new ticket is created
Send instant solution instructions for resolved common issues, acknowledgement messages, and status transition notifications to the requesting user
Send resolution details and new solution content when a ticket is marked Resolved
Send weekly report with common issues, average resolution times, and team performance metrics every Monday at 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Write service that creates and updates tickets in ClickUp under the Customer Support space
objects.state_description: Audit log of all ticket creation and update payloads sent to ClickUp, including ticket ID, category, priority, assigned team, and status
objects.behavior: Record all incoming ticket creation and update requests and write them to ClickUp under the Customer Support space with the specified category, priority, assigned team, and status.

objects.role: Write service that sends direct Slack messages to requesting users with solutions, acknowledgements, and status update notifications
objects.state_description: Audit log of all direct messages sent to users, including user ID, message content, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Record all incoming message payloads and send them as direct Slack messages to the specified user in Slack.

objects.role: Write service that updates the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence with new solutions from resolved tickets
objects.state_description: Audit log of all knowledge base update payloads written to Confluence, including category, issue description, and solution content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming resolution payloads and write new solution articles to the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence under the relevant category.

objects.role: Write service that posts weekly support metrics reports to the #support-metrics Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit log of all weekly report messages posted to #support-metrics, including timestamp and report content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming report payloads and post them to the #support-metrics channel in Slack.

steps: A user submits an IT support request in Slack through a support command, and the workflow captures the request details such as the requester, message content, source conversation, and submission time for triage.
The workflow analyzes the Slack request and queries the IT support knowledge base in Confluence to determine whether the issue matches an existing documented solution.
If Confluence contains a known fix, the workflow sends the user a direct Slack message with step-by-step instructions so common issues can be resolved immediately without creating a ticket.

If no matching knowledge base solution is found or the request is judged to be complex, the workflow creates a ticket in ClickUp and categorizes it as a Bug, Access Request, Hardware issue, or General Inquiry.

For requests that become tickets, the workflow assigns a priority of Urgent, High, Medium, or Low based on the request content and keywords, then routes the work to the most appropriate team—such as frontline support, engineering, or infrastructure—based on issue type and current workload.

After routing, the workflow posts a Slack notification to the support team with the relevant ticket context, and team members can send status changes such as In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved back through Slack.

Whenever a ticket status changes, the workflow sends the requesting user a direct Slack update so they stay informed throughout the resolution process, including acknowledgement when a ticket is first created.

When a ticket is marked Resolved, the workflow sends the resolution details to Confluence so new solution articles can be added to the IT support knowledge base under the appropriate category for future reuse.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow compiles support metrics from its tracked ticket history—including common issue patterns, average resolution times, and team performance—and posts the report to Slack.

modifications.intent: For the onboarding-type access issues, can we treat those as a bit more urgent when they come in from new hires who are blocked from getting started?

events.input: Slack /support submission payload: user_id=U1842; user_name=Maria Chen; channel=#help-desk; channel_id=C1001; command=/support; text="My monitor docking station flickers on and off and external displays keep disconnecting at my desk."; thread_ts=1716200400.112200; request_ts=1716200400.112200

expect.action: A new ClickUp ticket is created for Maria Chen’s request about a flickering monitor docking station and recurring external display disconnects, categorized as Hardware, prioritized according to the hardware issue content, and routed to Infrastructure. A Slack notification is posted to the support team channel with the new ticket details and routing context. Maria Chen also receives a direct Slack acknowledgement that her support ticket has been created and is being handled.
expect.reason: The request describes a physical device problem with a docking station and monitors, no matching Confluence self-service fix is provided in the reference data, so the workflow creates and routes a Hardware ticket and sends the required Slack notifications.

events.input: Slack /support submission payload: user_id=U2099; user_name=Ethan Alvarez; channel=#help-desk; channel_id=C1001; command=/support; text="I'm a new hire starting today and I still can't log into Okta or Slack, so I'm blocked from onboarding."; thread_ts=1716285600.221900; request_ts=1716285600.221900

expect.action: A new ClickUp ticket is created for Ethan Alvarez’s onboarding access issue, categorized as Access Request and [adjusted by M001] prioritized as Urgent because the requester is a new hire who is blocked from starting onboarding. The ticket is routed to Tier 1 Support. A Slack notification is posted to the support team channel with the ticket context, including that the requester is blocked from onboarding. Ethan Alvarez also receives a direct Slack acknowledgement that his support ticket has been created and marked urgent for follow-up.
expect.reason: The request is an access problem involving Okta and Slack login, and M001 applies because it is an onboarding-type access issue from a new hire who is blocked from getting started, raising the priority above the baseline treatment.

events.input: Slack /support submission payload: user_id=U3110; user_name=Priya Raman; channel=#help-desk; channel_id=C1001; command=/support; text="Can someone help me find the latest laptop reimbursement policy? This is just a general question."; thread_ts=1716290100.445500; request_ts=1716290100.445500

expect.action: A new ClickUp ticket is created for Priya Raman’s question about the latest laptop reimbursement policy, categorized as General Inquiry, prioritized according to the non-urgent question content, and routed to Tier 1 Support. A Slack notification is posted to the support team channel with the ticket details. Priya Raman also receives a direct Slack acknowledgement that her support request has been received and turned into a support ticket.
expect.reason: The request is explicitly a general question, does not match the onboarding access urgency modification, and no matching Confluence solution article for this policy question is provided in the reference data, so the workflow creates a General Inquiry ticket and notifies Slack recipients. [M001 condition not met: not a new-hire onboarding access issue]

events.input: Slack /support submission payload: user_id=U2877; user_name=Noah Patel; channel=#help-desk; channel_id=C1001; command=/support; text="New hire here — my laptop account was created, but I still don't have access to the payroll system and HR portal, so I can't finish setup."; thread_ts=1716374700.778100; request_ts=1716374700.778100

The ticket is routed to Tier 1 Support. A Slack notification is posted to the support team channel with the ticket context, including that payroll system and HR portal access are blocking onboarding setup. Noah Patel also receives a direct Slack acknowledgement that his support ticket has been created and marked urgent for follow-up.
expect.reason: The request is an onboarding-related access problem from a new hire who cannot complete setup, so M001 applies and raises the priority relative to the baseline Access Request handling.

tools.description: Retrieve reference data from confluence kb data. No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles on demand
tools.response_template: {"knowledge_base_articles": [{"article_id": "KB-20481", "title": "VPN Connection Failure with Error Code 0x800704ec on Windows 11", "category": "Network & Connectivity", "tags": ["VPN", "Windows 11", "error 0x800704ec", "Windows update", "connectivity", "23H2"], "status": "Published", "last_updated": "2024-05-28", "author": "IT Knowledge Base Team", "problem_description": "Users running Windows 11 Pro (version 23H2) report being unable to connect to the corporate VPN after applying a recent Windows update. Error 0x800704ec specifically maps to ERROR_PROTOCOL_UNREACHABLE, meaning the selected VPN protocol is being blocked at the network stack level.", "solution": {"steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Verify the error code", "detail": "Open Event Viewer (eventvwr.msc), navigate to Windows Logs > Application, and search for RasClient or RasSstp events to confirm error 0x800704ec is logged."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Check Windows Defender Firewall settings", "detail": "Open Windows Defender Firewall with Advanced Security (wf.msc). Ensure inbound and outbound rules for your VPN client application are set to 'Allow'. Look for any rules that may have been added or modified by the recent update that block VPN traffic."}, {"step": 3, "action": "Re-enable the VPN protocol via Registry Editor", "detail": "Press Win+R, type regedit, and navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\PolicyAgent. Locate or create a DWORD value named 'AssumeUDPEncapsulationContextOnSendRule' and set it to 2. This re-enables NAT traversal for L2TP/IPsec VPNs. Restart the machine after making this change."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Restart VPN-related Windows services", "detail": "Open Services (services.msc) and restart the following services: 'IKE and AuthIP IPsec Keying Modules' (IKEEXT), 'IPsec Policy Agent' (PolicyAgent), and 'Remote Access Connection Manager' (RasMan). Set their startup type to 'Automatic' if not already configured."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Reinstall the VPN client", "detail": "If the above steps do not resolve the issue, uninstall the VPN client via Settings > Apps > Installed Apps, reboot the machine, then download and reinstall the latest version of the corporate VPN client from the IT software portal at https://intranet.company.com/software."}, {"step": 6, "action": "Apply the Group Policy fix (for domain-joined machines)", "detail": "If the machine is domain-joined, run 'gpupdate /force' in an elevated Command Prompt. Windows Event Viewer contains detailed logs from the RasClient, RasSstp, and IKEEXT services that can help diagnose the root cause of VPN failures, including error 0x800704ec and related codes.", "symptoms": ["VPN client displays a generic error message", "Error code is shown but cause is unclear", "VPN worked previously but stopped after a system change"], "solution": {"steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Open Event Viewer", "detail": "Press Win+R, type eventvwr.msc, and press Enter to open Event Viewer."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Navigate to Application and System logs", "detail": "Expand 'Windows Logs' in the left pane. name: Slack-ClickUp Support Automation

objects.role: Slack entry-point that receives /support slash command submissions from users in #help-desk and forwards them to the support-triage business logic object
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming support requests with user ID, request text, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a /support slash command event arrives, forward the full request payload (user ID, request text, channel, timestamp) to support-triage via peer message. When a ticket status update command arrives from a team member, forward it to support-triage.

peers.relationship: Forward support request payload when a /support slash command is received
Forward ticket status update commands from team members

event_sources: webhook: Slack /support slash command
webhook: Slack ticket status update action from team member

objects.role: Dual-role Slack write service that posts ticket notifications to #support-queue and receives status update actions from team members, forwarding them to support-triage
objects.state_description: Audit log of all messages posted to #support-queue and status update actions received
objects.behavior: Record all incoming messages and post them to the #support-queue channel. When a team member submits a status update action (e.g., marking a ticket In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved) from within #support-queue, forward the action payload to support-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward team member status update actions received in #support-queue

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action from team member in #support-queue

objects.role: Read service representing the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence, answering queries about existing solutions for common issues
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles on demand
objects.behavior: Respond to queries about known issues and solutions by searching the IT Support knowledge base. When answering queries, call the `confluence_kb_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies support requests, resolves common issues instantly, creates ClickUp tickets for complex issues, assigns priority and routing, and orchestrates all status transitions
objects.state_description: Tracks active support requests with request ID, user ID, request text, classification, priority, assigned team, ClickUp ticket ID, and current status (Open, In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved)
objects.behavior: When a support request arrives: query confluence-kb for matching solutions. If a known solution is found, send the step-by-step instructions to user-dm-notifier. If no solution is found or the issue is complex, classify the request into category (Bug, Access Request, Hardware, General Inquiry) and assign priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low) based on request content and keywords. Determine the appropriate team (Tier 1 Support, Tier 2 Engineering, or Infrastructure) based on category and current workload tracked in state. Send the ticket creation payload to clickup-tickets and simultaneously send the team notification to support-queue-channel and send an acknowledgement to user-dm-notifier. When a status update arrives (In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved): update the tracked status in state, send the status notification to user-dm-notifier, and if status is Resolved, send the resolution details to confluence-kb-updater. On Monday at 09:00 UTC, compute weekly report metrics from state (common issues, average resolution times, team performance) and send the report to support-metrics-channel.

peers.relationship: Query for matching solutions when a new support request arrives

objects.role: Write service that posts weekly support metrics reports to the #support-metrics Slack channel

objects.behavior: Record all incoming report payloads and post them to the #support-metrics channel in Slack.

steps: A user submits an IT support request in Slack through a support command, and the workflow captures the request details such as the requester, message content, source conversation, and submission time for triage.
The workflow analyzes the Slack request and queries the IT support knowledge base in Confluence to determine whether the issue matches an existing documented solution.
If Confluence contains a known fix, the workflow sends the user a direct Slack message with step-by-step instructions so common issues can be resolved immediately without creating a ticket.

If no matching knowledge base solution is found or the request is judged to be complex, the workflow creates a ticket in ClickUp and categorizes it as a Bug, Access Request, Hardware issue, or General Inquiry.

For requests that become tickets, the workflow assigns a priority of Urgent, High, Medium, or Low based on the request content and keywords, then routes the work to the most appropriate team—such as frontline support, engineering, or infrastructure—based on issue type and current workload.

After routing, the workflow posts a Slack notification to the support team with the relevant ticket context, and team members can send status changes such as In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved back through Slack.

Whenever a ticket status changes, the workflow sends the requesting user a direct Slack update so they stay informed throughout the resolution process, including acknowledgement when a ticket is first created.

When a ticket is marked Resolved, the workflow sends the resolution details to Confluence so new solution articles can be added to the IT support knowledge base under the appropriate category for future reuse.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow compiles support metrics from its tracked ticket history—including common issue patterns, average resolution times, and team performance—and posts the report to Slack.

modifications.intent: That weekly support report time is a little off — have it go out around 10:00 UTC on Monday instead of the earlier slot.

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support; user_id=U1842; channel_id=C-helpdesk; channel_name=help-desk; request_text="I can't connect to the office VPN after this morning's password reset. It says authentication failed on Windows 11. Need access before my 11:00 client call."; message_ts=1717405500.120100; thread_ts=1717405500.120100; request_id=REQ-1001; submitted_at=W02-1T09:15

expect.action: A new ClickUp ticket is created for request REQ-1001 and categorized as Bug, because the Slack request describes a VPN failure and no published Confluence knowledge base article exactly matches the reported issue of authentication failure after a password reset on Windows 11. The ticket is assigned priority Urgent because the request says the user needs access before an 11:00 client call, and it is routed to Tier 2 Engineering. A direct Slack message is sent to user U1842 acknowledging that a support ticket was created and that updates will follow. A Slack notification is posted to the support queue channel with the new ticket details, including request REQ-1001, category Bug, priority Urgent, and assigned team Tier 2 Engineering.
expect.reason: The request contains failure language and time-critical impact, so under the support policy it becomes a Bug ticket with Urgent priority when no clear Confluence self-service fix matches the reported symptoms.

events.input: Scheduled weekly report trigger for support metrics: schedule_name=weekly_support_report; scheduled_for=W02-1T10:00; report_window_start=W01-1T00:00; report_window_end=W02-1T09:59; channel_id=C-support-metrics; channel_name=support-metrics

expect.reason: The Monday weekly metrics post is in scope for M001, which changes the report delivery time to around 10:00 UTC instead of the earlier baseline slot.
