objects.role: Slack entry-point that receives /support slash command submissions from users in #help-desk and forwards them to the support-triage business logic object
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming support requests with user ID, request text, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a /support slash command event arrives, forward the full request payload (user ID, request text, channel, timestamp) to support-triage via peer message. When a ticket status update command arrives from a team member, forward it to support-triage.

peers.relationship: Forward support request payload when a /support slash command is received
Forward ticket status update commands from team members

event_sources: webhook: Slack /support slash command
webhook: Slack ticket status update action from team member

objects.role: Dual-role Slack write service that posts ticket notifications to #support-queue and receives status update actions from team members, forwarding them to support-triage
objects.state_description: Audit log of all messages posted to #support-queue and status update actions received
objects.behavior: Record all incoming messages and post them to the #support-queue channel. When a team member submits a status update action (e.g., marking a ticket In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved) from within #support-queue, forward the action payload to support-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward team member status update actions received in #support-queue

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action from team member in #support-queue

objects.role: Read service representing the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence, answering queries about existing solutions for common issues
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles on demand
objects.behavior: Respond to queries about known issues and solutions by searching the IT Support knowledge base. When answering queries, call the `confluence_kb_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies support requests, resolves common issues instantly, creates ClickUp tickets for complex issues, assigns priority and routing, and orchestrates all status transitions
objects.state_description: Tracks active support requests with request ID, user ID, request text, classification, priority, assigned team, ClickUp ticket ID, and current status (Open, In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved)
objects.behavior: When a support request arrives: query confluence-kb for matching solutions. If a known solution is found, send the step-by-step instructions to user-dm-notifier. If no solution is found or the issue is complex, classify the request into category (Bug, Access Request, Hardware, General Inquiry) and assign priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low) based on request content and keywords. Determine the appropriate team (Tier 1 Support, Tier 2 Engineering, or Infrastructure) based on category and current workload tracked in state. Send the ticket creation payload to clickup-tickets and simultaneously send the team notification to support-queue-channel and send an acknowledgement to user-dm-notifier. When a status update arrives (In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved): update the tracked status in state, send the status notification to user-dm-notifier, and if status is Resolved, send the resolution details to confluence-kb-updater. On Monday at 09:00 UTC, compute weekly report metrics from state (common issues, average resolution times, team performance) and send the report to support-metrics-channel.

peers.relationship: Query for matching solutions when a new support request arrives
Send ticket creation payload with category, priority, and assigned team for complex or unrecognized requests
Send team notification with ticket ID, category, priority, and context when a new ticket is created
Send instant solution instructions for resolved common issues, acknowledgement messages, and status transition notifications to the requesting user
Send resolution details and new solution content when a ticket is marked Resolved
Send weekly report with common issues, average resolution times, and team performance metrics every Monday at 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Write service that creates and updates tickets in ClickUp under the Customer Support space
objects.state_description: Audit log of all ticket creation and update payloads sent to ClickUp, including ticket ID, category, priority, assigned team, and status
objects.behavior: Record all incoming ticket creation and update requests and write them to ClickUp under the Customer Support space with the specified category, priority, assigned team, and status.

objects.role: Write service that sends direct Slack messages to requesting users with solutions, acknowledgements, and status update notifications
objects.state_description: Audit log of all direct messages sent to users, including user ID, message content, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Record all incoming message payloads and send them as direct Slack messages to the specified user in Slack.

objects.role: Write service that updates the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence with new solutions from resolved tickets
objects.state_description: Audit log of all knowledge base update payloads written to Confluence, including category, issue description, and solution content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming resolution payloads and write new solution articles to the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence under the relevant category.

objects.role: Write service that posts weekly support metrics reports to the #support-metrics Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit log of all weekly report messages posted to #support-metrics, including timestamp and report content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming report payloads and post them to the #support-metrics channel in Slack.

steps: A user submits an IT support request in Slack through a support command, and the workflow captures the request details such as the requester, message content, source conversation, and submission time for triage.
The workflow analyzes the Slack request and queries the IT support knowledge base in Confluence to determine whether the issue matches an existing documented solution.
If Confluence contains a known fix, the workflow sends the user a direct Slack message with step-by-step instructions so common issues can be resolved immediately without creating a ticket.

If no matching knowledge base solution is found or the request is judged to be complex, the workflow creates a ticket in ClickUp and categorizes it as a Bug, Access Request, Hardware issue, or General Inquiry.

For requests that become tickets, the workflow assigns a priority of Urgent, High, Medium, or Low based on the request content and keywords, then routes the work to the most appropriate team—such as frontline support, engineering, or infrastructure—based on issue type and current workload.

After routing, the workflow posts a Slack notification to the support team with the relevant ticket context, and team members can send status changes such as In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved back through Slack.

Whenever a ticket status changes, the workflow sends the requesting user a direct Slack update so they stay informed throughout the resolution process, including acknowledgement when a ticket is first created.

When a ticket is marked Resolved, the workflow sends the resolution details to Confluence so new solution articles can be added to the IT support knowledge base under the appropriate category for future reuse.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow compiles support metrics from its tracked ticket history—including common issue patterns, average resolution times, and team performance—and posts the report to Slack.

modifications.intent: For any new support request whose text indicates an Access Request and includes urgent access language such as 'urgent', 'ASAP', 'immediately', 'locked out', or 'cannot access', keep querying Confluence as usual, but if a matching knowledge base solution is found, also send a team notification to support-queue-channel with the request context and note that the user received self-service instructions. Do not create a ClickUp ticket for this rule unless the request would already require one under the existing logic.

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support; user_id=U1842; user_name=Janelle Ortiz; channel_id=C-helpdesk; channel_name=help-desk; trigger_id=trig-2001; request_ts=2026-05-11T09:00:00Z; thread_ts=1746954000.001200; text="How do I connect to the office VPN from my new laptop? I need the setup steps for macOS."

expect.action: A direct Slack message is sent to Janelle Ortiz with self-service guidance for connecting to the office VPN from a new macOS laptop, using the knowledge base answer instead of creating a support ticket.
expect.reason: The request is a how-to/general inquiry and the workflow first checks Confluence for a documented solution, sending instructions by Slack DM when a known answer is found.

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support; user_id=U2055; user_name=Marcus Lee; channel_id=C-helpdesk; channel_name=help-desk; trigger_id=trig-2002; request_ts=2026-05-11T13:00:00Z; thread_ts=1746968400.004100; text="Urgent: I am locked out of the payroll system and need access restored immediately before payroll closes today. Is there a standard access reset process?"

expect.action: A direct Slack message is sent to Marcus Lee with the documented access-reset or access-restoration instructions for the payroll system if a matching Confluence article is found; [added by M001] a Slack notification is also posted to the support team queue channel summarizing Marcus Lee's urgent locked-out payroll access request, noting that self-service instructions were sent and including the request context. If no matching article is found, a ClickUp support ticket is created for Marcus Lee's payroll access issue, categorized as Access Request, assigned Urgent priority, routed to Tier 1 Support unless privileged-access handling requires Infrastructure, a Slack notification with the ticket context is posted to the support team, and Marcus Lee receives a Slack DM acknowledging ticket creation.
expect.reason: This is an urgent access request with locked-out language, so baseline behavior is to search Confluence first and create a ticket only if no documented solution is found or fulfillment is still required; under M001, when a matching knowledge base solution exists, the workflow must also notify the support queue channel even though no ticket is created.

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support; user_id=U3110; user_name=Priya Nair; channel_id=C-helpdesk; channel_name=help-desk; trigger_id=trig-2003; request_ts=2026-05-11T14:00:00Z; thread_ts=1746972000.002700; text="The label printer on Floor 3 is jamming every other page after we replaced the toner. Please help."

expect.action: A ClickUp support ticket is created for Priya Nair's Floor 3 label printer issue, categorized as Hardware, prioritized according to the request content, and routed to Infrastructure. A Slack notification with the ticket context is posted to the support team, and Priya Nair receives a direct Slack message acknowledging that the ticket was created and is being worked.
expect.reason: The request describes a physical printer malfunction with no indicated self-service knowledge base match, so the workflow creates and routes a hardware support ticket and notifies both the team and the requester.

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support; user_id=U2291; user_name=Elena Petrova; channel_id=C-helpdesk; channel_name=help-desk; trigger_id=trig-2004; request_ts=2026-05-12T09:30:00Z; thread_ts=1747042200.006500; text="ASAP access request: I cannot access Salesforce after my role change this morning. If there is a documented permission refresh process, please send it."

expect.action: A direct Slack message is sent to Elena Petrova with the documented Salesforce permission-refresh or access-restoration instructions if a matching Confluence article is found; [added by M001] a Slack notification is also posted to the support team queue channel summarizing Elena Petrova's ASAP access request and noting that self-service instructions were sent. If no matching article is found, a ClickUp support ticket is created for Elena Petrova's Salesforce access problem, categorized as Access Request, assigned Urgent priority, routed to Tier 1 Support unless privileged-access handling requires Infrastructure, a Slack notification with the ticket context is posted to the support team, and Elena Petrova receives a Slack DM acknowledging ticket creation.
expect.reason: This request falls within M001 because it is an urgent access request with 'ASAP' and 'cannot access' language, so the workflow still searches Confluence first and, if a documented fix exists, also sends a team queue notification about the self-service response.

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support; user_id=U1988; user_name=Noah Kim; channel_id=C-helpdesk; channel_name=help-desk; trigger_id=trig-2005; request_ts=2026-05-12T11:00:00Z; thread_ts=1747047600.008800; text="I need access to the marketing shared drive next week when I start covering for Sam. What is the request process?"

expect.action: A direct Slack message is sent to Noah Kim with the documented process for requesting access to the marketing shared drive if a matching Confluence article is found. If no matching article is found or the request requires fulfillment rather than instructions, a ClickUp support ticket is created for the shared-drive access request, categorized as Access Request, assigned priority based on the request text, routed to Tier 1 Support unless privileged-access handling requires Infrastructure, a Slack notification with the ticket context is posted to the support team, and Noah Kim receives a Slack DM acknowledging ticket creation.
expect.reason: This is an access request, but M001 does not apply because the text does not include urgent-access language; therefore baseline behavior applies: search Confluence first, then create and route a ticket only if needed.

name: Out-of-Office Task Tracking System

objects.role: Dual-role service object for the Retool app that receives out-of-office status changes and return-session task updates, and presents task data to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Retool events such as out-of-office activation, return access, and task status updates initiated in the app; does not store authoritative task records.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event arrives, treat the submitted absence context as the workflow trigger. When a return-session task status update event arrives, treat the submitted task identifier and new status as a user action to be processed. When a return access event arrives, treat it as a request to load current tasks for display. When out-of-office activation, return access, or task status update arrives, forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Retool out-of-office activation, return access, and task status update events when submitted in the app

event_sources: retool app: out-of-office activation submitted
retool app: return access requested
retool app: task status update submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service object for Slack emoji reaction events from messages in #revops-requests during an active out-of-office period.
objects.state_description: Tracks an audit trail of inbound Slack reaction events received from #revops-requests.
objects.behavior: When a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, capture the message reference, reactor, emoji, channel, and requester context included in the event and forward it to ooo-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction events from #revops-requests for task creation during out-of-office periods

event_sources: slack: reaction added in #revops-requests

objects.role: Read service object for the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table, providing task lookup and retrieval for current out-of-office tracking data.
objects.state_description: Holds no runtime state; task data is retrieved on demand from the Airtable Tasks table.
objects.behavior: When asked for tasks by status, assignee, message reference, requester, or out-of-office session, return matching records from the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked for all accumulated tasks for return review, return the current task list with identifiers, source message references, requester details, reaction metadata, and statuses. When asked for a specific task, return the matching task record. When answering queries, call the `airtable-tasks_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object for creating and updating task records in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of task create and task update write requests sent to Airtable.
objects.behavior: When asked to create a task, record the provided message reference, requester, reaction metadata, out-of-office session context, and initial status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table. When asked to update a task, record the provided task identifier and new status in the OOO Requests Airtable base Tasks table.

objects.role: Write service object for posting Slack notifications to #revops-team and #revops-requests.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound Slack messages posted by the automation.
objects.behavior: When asked to announce out-of-office activation, post to the #revops-team channel with the absence notice and task-tracking activation context. When asked to notify a requester of task status, post to the #revops-requests channel with the original request reference, updated status, and requester-facing context.

objects.role: Write service object for sending task lists and task-update results back to the Retool app UI for display.
objects.state_description: Tracks the audit trail of outbound UI payloads sent to Retool for rendering task lists and status refreshes.
objects.behavior: When asked to display accumulated tasks, send the current task list with identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses to the Retool app UI. When asked to refresh a task after status change, send the updated task state to the Retool app UI.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies the rules for activating out-of-office tracking, converting Slack reactions into tracked tasks, and handling return-time task review and status updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks whether out-of-office tracking is active, the current absence session context, and correlations between Slack request messages, Airtable task records, and requester notification state.
objects.behavior: When an out-of-office activation event is received, mark tracking active for the submitted absence session and prepare notifications and task capture for that session. When tracking is active and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives from #revops-requests, interpret the reaction as a task/request to capture while the user is away, derive a task record from the message and reaction context, and assign initial status New. When tracking is inactive and a Slack emoji reaction event arrives, ignore it for task creation. When a return access event is received, retrieve the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent absence session and prepare them for display in Retool. When a task status update event is received, allow only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done; reject any other status. When a valid task status update is received, prepare the requester notification context tied to the original Slack request. Send to slack-notifications and airtable-task-writes for out-of-office activation. Send to airtable-task-writes when a new task should be created from a Slack reaction. Send to airtable-tasks and retool-task-view when return access requires task display. Send to airtable-task-writes, airtable-tasks, slack-notifications, and retool-task-view when a valid task status update is submitted.

peers.relationship: Send out-of-office announcement requests for #revops-team and requester status notifications for #revops-requests
Send task create requests for captured Slack reactions and task update requests for status changes
Request current task records for return review and task refresh context
Send task lists and refreshed task state for display in the Retool app

steps: When a user marks themselves out of office in the Retool interface, the workflow starts an absence-tracking session using the submitted absence context. It then sends a Slack announcement to the team and enables capture of incoming requests for that out-of-office period.

While that out-of-office session is active, the workflow listens for emoji reactions added to messages in Slack request conversations. If tracking is not active, those reactions are ignored and no task is created.

For each qualifying Slack reaction received during the active absence period, the workflow turns the message and reaction context into a tracked task in Airtable. It stores the original message reference, requester details, reaction metadata, the related out-of-office session, and sets the initial task status to New.

When the user returns and opens the Retool interface, the workflow retrieves the accumulated tasks for the active or most recent out-of-office session from Airtable. It displays the current list with task identifiers, requester details, source message references, reaction metadata, and statuses.

As the user works through the backlog in Retool, they can update each task's status directly in the interface. The workflow accepts only the statuses New, In Progress, and Done, and rejects any other value.

Whenever a valid task status change is submitted, the workflow updates the task in Airtable and sends a Slack notification tied to the original request so the requester is informed of the new status. It also refreshes the task display in Retool with the updated state.

The workflow can also be viewed in Canvas as a visual map of how Retool, Slack, and Airtable interact across out-of-office activation, task capture, return review, and requester notifications. This step is for visualization only and does not change workflow data or behavior.

modifications.intent: When I come back and open the Retool return view, also limit the task list to items from the active or most recent out-of-office session that are still New or In Progress so completed work does not show up in the review queue.

events.input: Retool out-of-office activation submitted for user Dana Kim. Session ID: ooo-2025-02-dkim. Absence starts W02-1T09:00 and ends W02-3T09:00. Reason: annual leave. Coverage note: track inbound requests from #revops-requests while away.

expect.action: A new out-of-office absence session record for Dana Kim with session ID ooo-2025-02-dkim is started using the submitted dates and coverage note, and a Slack team announcement is posted that Dana Kim is out of office from W02-1T09:00 to W02-3T09:00 with instructions to track inbound requests from #revops-requests during that period.
expect.reason: When a user marks themselves out of office in Retool, the workflow starts the absence-tracking session and sends a Slack announcement so request capture is enabled for the out-of-office period.

events.input: Retool return access opened by Dana Kim. Session ID: ooo-2025-02-dkim. Request current accumulated tasks for return review. Existing task context in Airtable for this session includes task T-101 status New from Slack message ts 1736152200.000100 by requester Priya Shah, task T-102 status Done from Slack message ts 1736155800.000200 by requester Marco Ruiz, and task T-103 status In Progress from Slack message ts 1736159400.000300 by requester Elena Park.

expect.action: [adjusted by M001] The Retool return review displays only the open tasks from session ooo-2025-02-dkim: task T-101 for requester Priya Shah from Slack message ts 1736152200.000100 with status New, and task T-103 for requester Elena Park from Slack message ts 1736159400.000300 with status In Progress. Task T-102 is not shown in the review queue because its status is Done.
expect.reason: M001 applies to return-view task retrievals and changes the baseline task list from all tasks in the active or most recent session to only tasks still in New or In Progress.

events.input: Slack reaction event from channel #revops-requests. Message ts: 1736163000.000400. Thread ts: 1736163000.000400. Reactor: Sam Lee. Emoji: eyes. Requester: Jordan Bell. Message text: 'Can someone update the Salesforce opportunity stage before the forecast call?' Active out-of-office session for Dana Kim: ooo-2025-02-dkim.

expect.action: A new Airtable task is created for Dana Kim's out-of-office session ooo-2025-02-dkim from the #revops-requests Slack message ts 1736163000.000400, capturing the original message reference, requester Jordan Bell, reactor Sam Lee, emoji eyes, and the related session, with the task's initial status set to New.
expect.reason: During an active out-of-office session, a qualifying eyes reaction in the tracked Slack request channel is converted into a new Airtable task linked to that absence session; M001 does not change reaction-based task creation.

events.input: Retool return access opened by Dana Kim. Session ID: ooo-2025-02-dkim. Request current accumulated tasks for return review. Existing task context in Airtable for this session includes task T-201 status Done from Slack message ts 1736245800.000500 by requester Nina Patel and task T-202 status Done from Slack message ts 1736249400.000600 by requester Omar Hassan.

expect.action: [adjusted by M001] The Retool return review for session ooo-2025-02-dkim shows an empty review queue because the only Airtable tasks provided for that session, T-201 and T-202, are both Done and therefore excluded from the return list.
expect.reason: M001 applies because this is a return-view retrieval, and it filters the displayed tasks to only New or In Progress items from the active or most recent out-of-office session.

events.input: Retool task status update submitted by Dana Kim. Session ID: ooo-2025-02-dkim. Task ID: T-101. Original Slack message ts: 1736152200.000100. Thread ts: 1736152200.000100. Requester: Priya Shah. New status: Done.

expect.action: Task T-101 is updated to status Done in Airtable for session ooo-2025-02-dkim, a Slack notification is posted on the original request thread for Priya Shah tied to message ts 1736152200.000100 informing the requester that the task is now Done, and the Retool task display refreshes to show T-101 with status Done.
expect.reason: A submitted status of Done is one of the workflow's accepted values, so the workflow updates Airtable, notifies the requester in Slack, and refreshes the Retool view; M001 only affects return-list filtering, not status updates.

name: Slack-ClickUp Support Automation

objects.role: Slack entry-point that receives /support slash command submissions from users in #help-desk and forwards them to the support-triage business logic object
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming support requests with user ID, request text, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a /support slash command event arrives, forward the full request payload (user ID, request text, channel, timestamp) to support-triage via peer message. When a ticket status update command arrives from a team member, forward it to support-triage.

peers.relationship: Forward support request payload when a /support slash command is received
Forward ticket status update commands from team members

event_sources: webhook: Slack /support slash command
webhook: Slack ticket status update action from team member

objects.role: Dual-role Slack write service that posts ticket notifications to #support-queue and receives status update actions from team members, forwarding them to support-triage
objects.state_description: Audit log of all messages posted to #support-queue and status update actions received
objects.behavior: Record all incoming messages and post them to the #support-queue channel. When a team member submits a status update action (e.g., marking a ticket In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved) from within #support-queue, forward the action payload to support-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward team member status update actions received in #support-queue

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action from team member in #support-queue

objects.role: Read service representing the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence, answering queries about existing solutions for common issues
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles on demand
objects.behavior: Respond to queries about known issues and solutions by searching the IT Support knowledge base. When answering queries, call the `confluence_kb_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies support requests, resolves common issues instantly, creates ClickUp tickets for complex issues, assigns priority and routing, and orchestrates all status transitions
objects.state_description: Tracks active support requests with request ID, user ID, request text, classification, priority, assigned team, ClickUp ticket ID, and current status (Open, In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved)
objects.behavior: When a support request arrives: query confluence-kb for matching solutions. If a known solution is found, send the step-by-step instructions to user-dm-notifier. If no solution is found or the issue is complex, classify the request into category (Bug, Access Request, Hardware, General Inquiry) and assign priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low) based on request content and keywords. Determine the appropriate team (Tier 1 Support, Tier 2 Engineering, or Infrastructure) based on category and current workload tracked in state. Send the ticket creation payload to clickup-tickets and simultaneously send the team notification to support-queue-channel and send an acknowledgement to user-dm-notifier. When a status update arrives (In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved): update the tracked status in state, send the status notification to user-dm-notifier, and if status is Resolved, send the resolution details to confluence-kb-updater. On Monday at 09:00 UTC, compute weekly report metrics from state (common issues, average resolution times, team performance) and send the report to support-metrics-channel.

peers.relationship: Query for matching solutions when a new support request arrives
Send ticket creation payload with category, priority, and assigned team for complex or unrecognized requests
Send team notification with ticket ID, category, priority, and context when a new ticket is created
Send instant solution instructions for resolved common issues, acknowledgement messages, and status transition notifications to the requesting user
Send resolution details and new solution content when a ticket is marked Resolved
Send weekly report with common issues, average resolution times, and team performance metrics every Monday at 09:00 UTC

objects.role: Write service that creates and updates tickets in ClickUp under the Customer Support space
objects.state_description: Audit log of all ticket creation and update payloads sent to ClickUp, including ticket ID, category, priority, assigned team, and status
objects.behavior: Record all incoming ticket creation and update requests and write them to ClickUp under the Customer Support space with the specified category, priority, assigned team, and status.

objects.role: Write service that sends direct Slack messages to requesting users with solutions, acknowledgements, and status update notifications
objects.state_description: Audit log of all direct messages sent to users, including user ID, message content, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Record all incoming message payloads and send them as direct Slack messages to the specified user in Slack.

objects.role: Write service that updates the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence with new solutions from resolved tickets
objects.state_description: Audit log of all knowledge base update payloads written to Confluence, including category, issue description, and solution content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming resolution payloads and write new solution articles to the IT Support knowledge base in Confluence under the relevant category.

objects.role: Write service that posts weekly support metrics reports to the #support-metrics Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit log of all weekly report messages posted to #support-metrics, including timestamp and report content
objects.behavior: Record all incoming report payloads and post them to the #support-metrics channel in Slack.

steps: A user submits an IT support request in Slack through a support command, and the workflow captures the request details such as the requester, message content, source conversation, and submission time for triage.
The workflow analyzes the Slack request and queries the IT support knowledge base in Confluence to determine whether the issue matches an existing documented solution.
If Confluence contains a known fix, the workflow sends the user a direct Slack message with step-by-step instructions so common issues can be resolved immediately without creating a ticket.

If no matching knowledge base solution is found or the request is judged to be complex, the workflow creates a ticket in ClickUp and categorizes it as a Bug, Access Request, Hardware issue, or General Inquiry.

For requests that become tickets, the workflow assigns a priority of Urgent, High, Medium, or Low based on the request content and keywords, then routes the work to the most appropriate team—such as frontline support, engineering, or infrastructure—based on issue type and current workload.

After routing, the workflow posts a Slack notification to the support team with the relevant ticket context, and team members can send status changes such as In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved back through Slack.

Whenever a ticket status changes, the workflow sends the requesting user a direct Slack update so they stay informed throughout the resolution process, including acknowledgement when a ticket is first created.

When a ticket is marked Resolved, the workflow sends the resolution details to Confluence so new solution articles can be added to the IT support knowledge base under the appropriate category for future reuse.

On a weekly schedule, the workflow compiles support metrics from its tracked ticket history—including common issue patterns, average resolution times, and team performance—and posts the report to Slack.

modifications.intent: This week, can the weekly support report go out a bit later instead of the usual Monday morning run?

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support, user_id=U1842, channel_id=C-helpdesk, channel_name=help-desk, request_text="I can't access the VPN after my password reset. It keeps saying authentication failed on my MacBook.", thread_ts="1716205200.001100", event_ts="1716205200.001100"

expect.action: A new ClickUp support ticket is created for user U1842 from the Slack /support request about VPN access failing after a password reset on a MacBook. The ticket is categorized as a Bug, assigned priority High, and routed to Tier 2 Engineering. A Slack notification with the ticket context is posted to the support team channel, and the requesting user receives a direct Slack acknowledgement that a support ticket has been created and is being worked.
expect.reason: The request does not match the Confluence article provided because that article is for Windows 11 VPN error 0x800704ec, while this request is a MacBook authentication problem, so the workflow creates and routes a ClickUp ticket and notifies both support staff and the requester.

events.input: Scheduled weekly report trigger for support metrics: schedule_id=weekly-support-report, scheduled_for=W02-3T09:00, report_window_start=W01-1T00:00, report_window_end=W02-1T08:59, channel=#support-metrics

expect.action: [deferred by M001] The weekly support metrics report is held and is not posted to Slack #support-metrics at W02-3T09:00.
expect.reason: M001 applies to this week's weekly support report and changes the timing so the scheduled Slack report goes out later than the usual run time.

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support, user_id=U5521, channel_id=C-helpdesk, channel_name=help-desk, request_text="New hire in Finance needs access to the quarterly forecasting folder in Google Drive before tomorrow morning.", thread_ts="1716379200.004200", event_ts="1716379200.004200"

expect.action: A new ClickUp support ticket is created for user U5521 from the Slack /support request for Google Drive access to the quarterly forecasting folder. The ticket is categorized as an Access Request, assigned priority High, and routed to Tier 1 Support. A Slack notification with the ticket context is posted to the support team channel, and the requesting user receives a direct Slack acknowledgement that a support ticket has been created and routed for fulfillment.
expect.reason: No matching Confluence self-service fix is provided for this access fulfillment request, so the workflow creates an Access Request ticket, prioritizes it based on the deadline language 'before tomorrow morning,' routes it to Tier 1 Support, and sends the standard Slack notifications.

events.input: Scheduled weekly report trigger for support metrics: schedule_id=weekly-support-report, scheduled_for=W02-4T09:00, report_window_start=W01-1T00:00, report_window_end=W02-3T23:59, channel=#support-metrics

expect.action: [fired-on-schedule by M001] The weekly support metrics report for the period W01-1T00:00 through W02-3T23:59 is now posted to Slack channel #support-metrics at W02-4T09:00 instead of the usual earlier run. The report includes support metrics compiled from tracked ticket history, including common issue patterns, average resolution times, and team performance.
expect.reason: M001 asked for this week's weekly support report to go out later, so the report is deferred from the earlier schedule and then posted when the later scheduled trigger occurs.

events.input: Slack slash command payload: command=/support, user_id=U9088, channel_id=C-helpdesk, channel_name=help-desk, request_text="My laptop webcam stopped working after the latest update, and I have customer calls this afternoon.", thread_ts="1716461100.002700", event_ts="1716461100.002700"

expect.action: A new ClickUp support ticket is created for user U9088 from the Slack /support request about a laptop webcam failing after the latest update. The ticket is categorized as Hardware, assigned priority Urgent, and routed to Infrastructure. A Slack notification with the ticket context is posted to the support team channel, and the requesting user receives a direct Slack acknowledgement that a support ticket has been created and escalated.
expect.reason: No matching Confluence self-service article is provided for this webcam hardware issue, and the request indicates business urgency with customer calls this afternoon, so the workflow creates a Hardware ticket, marks it Urgent, routes it to Infrastructure, and sends Slack notifications.

tools.description: Retrieve reference data from confluence kb data. No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles on demand
tools.response_template: {"knowledge_base_articles": [{"article_id": "KB-20481", "title": "VPN Connection Failure with Error Code 0x800704ec on Windows 11", "category": "Network & Connectivity", "tags": ["VPN", "Windows 11", "error 0x800704ec", "Windows update", "connectivity", "23H2"], "status": "Published", "last_updated": "2024-05-28", "author": "IT Knowledge Base Team", "problem_description": "Users running Windows 11 Pro (version 23H2) report being unable to connect to the corporate VPN after applying a recent Windows update. The error code 0x800704ec is displayed during the connection attempt. This error typically indicates that the VPN protocol or a required network component has been blocked or disabled by a Windows security policy change introduced in the update.", "affected_systems": ["Windows 11 Pro", "Windows 11 Home", "Windows 11 Enterprise"], "affected_versions": ["23H2", "22H2"], "symptoms": ["VPN client fails to establish a connection", "Error code 0x800704ec displayed in VPN client or Windows event log", "Issue appears immediately after a Windows cumulative or feature update", "Other network connectivity (browsing, LAN) remains unaffected", "VPN connection may have worked prior to the update"], "root_cause": "Windows update KB5034765 and related cumulative updates for 23H2 introduced changes to the Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) and updated Group Policy defaults that can block legacy VPN protocols such as PPTP and L2TP. Error 0x800704ec specifically maps to ERROR_PROTOCOL_UNREACHABLE, meaning the selected VPN protocol is being blocked at the network stack level.", "solution": {"steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Verify the error code", "detail": "Open Event Viewer (eventvwr.msc), navigate to Windows Logs > Application, and search for RasClient or RasSstp events to confirm error 0x800704ec is logged."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Check Windows Defender Firewall settings", "detail": "Open Windows Defender Firewall with Advanced Security (wf.msc). Ensure inbound and outbound rules for your VPN client application are set to 'Allow'. Look for any rules that may have been added or modified by the recent update that block VPN traffic."}, {"step": 3, "action": "Re-enable the VPN protocol via Registry Editor", "detail": "Press Win+R, type regedit, and navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\PolicyAgent. Locate or create a DWORD value named 'AssumeUDPEncapsulationContextOnSendRule' and set it to 2. This re-enables NAT traversal for L2TP/IPsec VPNs. Restart the machine after making this change."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Restart VPN-related Windows services", "detail": "Open Services (services.msc) and restart the following services: 'IKE and AuthIP IPsec Keying Modules' (IKEEXT), 'IPsec Policy Agent' (PolicyAgent), and 'Remote Access Connection Manager' (RasMan). Set their startup type to 'Automatic' if not already configured."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Reinstall the VPN client", "detail": "If the above steps do not resolve the issue, uninstall the VPN client via Settings > Apps > Installed Apps, reboot the machine, then download and reinstall the latest version of the corporate VPN client from the IT software portal at https://intranet.company.com/software."}, {"step": 6, "action": "Apply the Group Policy fix (for domain-joined machines)", "detail": "If the machine is domain-joined, run 'gpupdate /force' in an elevated Command Prompt. Save this ticket ID for future reference."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Track your ticket", "detail": "You can check the status of your ticket at any time by visiting the IT portal at https://intranet.company.com/support and entering your ticket ID, or by asking in #help-desk referencing your ticket number."}]}, "related_articles": ["KB-20481", "KB-20512"], "ticket_references": []}, {"article_id": "KB-20512", "title": "IT Support Ticket Lifecycle and Status Definitions", "category": "Support Processes", "tags": ["ticket", "support queue", "status", "In Progress", "triage", "escalation", "TKT"], "status": "Published", "last_updated": "2024-05-01", "author": "IT Knowledge Base Team", "problem_description": "This article explains the lifecycle of an IT support ticket from submission through resolution, including all possible status values and what they mean for the submitter and the support team.", "symptoms": ["User or team member is unsure what a ticket status means", "Team member needs to understand how to update ticket status in Slack", "Manager wants to understand support queue workflow"], "ticket_statuses": [{"status": "Open", "description": "Ticket has been submitted and is awaiting triage by a support team member. No action has been taken yet."}, {"status": "In Progress", "description": "A support team member has picked up the ticket and is actively working on the issue. The submitter may be contacted for additional information."}, {"status": "Pending User", "description": "The support team is waiting for a response or additional information from the ticket submitter before proceeding."}, {"status": "Escalated", "description": "The ticket has been escalated to a higher-tier support team or specialist due to complexity or SLA breach risk."}, {"status": "Resolved", "description": "The issue has been addressed and a solution has been provided. The ticket will auto-close after 48 hours if no further response is received."}, {"status": "Closed", "description": "The ticket is fully closed. No further action will be taken unless a new ticket is opened."}], "solution": {"steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Understand ticket submission", "detail": "Tickets are created automatically when a user submits a /support command in #help-desk or fills out the form at https://intranet.company.com/support. Each ticket receives a unique ID in the format TKT-XXXX (e.g., TKT-1042)."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Triage in #support-queue", "detail": "All new tickets appear in the #support-queue Slack channel. Support team members can view ticket details and claim tickets by using the status update action within the ticket message."}, {"step": 3, "action": "Updating ticket status", "detail": "Team members with support roles can update ticket status directly from #support-queue by clicking the 'Update Status' action button on the ticket message. Select the new status from the dropdown (e.g., 'In Progress', 'Pending User', 'Resolved'). The ticket submitter will be notified automatically via Slack DM."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Escalation procedure", "detail": "If a ticket cannot be resolved within the SLA window (4 business hours for P2, 1 business hour for P1), the team member must escalate by updating the status to 'Escalated' and tagging the relevant specialist team in #support-queue."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Closing a ticket", "detail": "Tickets are resolved by the assigned team member once the issue is fixed. The submitter is notified and given 48 hours to confirm resolution before the ticket is automatically closed."}]}, "example_ticket": {"ticket_id": "TKT-1042", "submitted_by": "Maya Chen", "slack_user_id": "U04MCHEN", "channel_submitted": "#help-desk", "submission_timestamp": "1717200000.000100", "issue_summary": "VPN connectivity issue — error code 0x800704ec after Windows update on Windows 11 Pro 23H2", "current_status": "In Progress", "assigned_to": "Jordan Lee", "assignee_slack_user_id": "U09JLEE", "status_updated_channel": "#support-queue", "status_update_timestamp": "1717200500.000200", "related_kb_articles": ["KB-20481"]}, "related_articles": ["KB-20481", "KB-20103"], "ticket_references": ["TKT-1042"]}, {"article_id": "KB-19874", "title": "Diagnosing VPN Errors Using Windows Event Viewer", "category": "Network & Connectivity", "tags": ["VPN", "Event Viewer", "diagnostics", "error codes", "Windows 11", "RasClient", "troubleshooting"], "status": "Published", "last_updated": "2024-03-22", "author": "IT Knowledge Base Team", "problem_description": "When a VPN connection fails on Windows, the error code shown in the client may not provide enough context. Windows Event Viewer contains detailed logs from the RasClient, RasSstp, and IKEEXT services that can help diagnose the root cause of VPN failures, including error 0x800704ec and related codes.", "symptoms": ["VPN client displays a generic error message", "Error code is shown but cause is unclear", "VPN worked previously but stopped after a system change"], "solution": {"steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Open Event Viewer", "detail": "Press Win+R, type eventvwr.msc, and press Enter to open Event Viewer."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Navigate to Application and System logs", "detail": "Expand 'Windows Logs' in the left pane. Check both 'Application' and 'System' logs for errors timestamped around the time of the VPN failure."}, {"step": 3, "action": "Filter for VPN-related events", "detail": "In the Application log, filter by source 'RasClient'. In the System log, filter by sources 'IKEEXT' and 'PolicyAgent'. These sources log all VPN authentication and connection events."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Interpret error 0x800704ec", "detail": "Error 0x800704ec maps to ERROR_PROTOCOL_UNREACHABLE. This indicates the VPN protocol (e.g., L2TP, PPTP, SSTP) is being blocked at the Windows network stack level. This is commonly caused by Windows Firewall rules, Windows Filtering Platform policy changes, or a Windows update modifying IPsec or RAS service configurations. Refer to KB-20481 for the full resolution steps."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Export logs for escalation", "detail": "If escalating to the Network Infrastructure team, right-click the relevant log in Event Viewer, select 'Save All Events As', and save as .evtx. Attach this file to your support ticket (e.g., TKT-1042) for faster diagnosis."}]}, "common_vpn_error_codes": [{"code": "0x800704ec", "meaning": "ERROR_PROTOCOL_UNREACHABLE — VPN protocol blocked by Windows Filtering Platform or firewall policy"}, {"code": "0x80072746", "meaning": "WSAECONNRESET — Connection was forcibly closed by the remote host; check server-side VPN configuration"}, {"code": "0x800704D4", "meaning": "ERROR_CONNECTION_ABORTED — Connection was aborted; often a timeout or certificate issue"}, {"code": "0x80070005", "meaning": "ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED — Insufficient permissions; verify user account VPN access rights"}], "related_articles": ["KB-20481", "KB-20512"], "ticket_references": []}]}
